В В В В В В 1.В В В The use is for a maximum of ten persons , or if operated in
conjunction with an Adult Care Service, the number of clients together with the
number of residents exceed the total permitted for the Adult Care Service.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The use shall comply with one of the following.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For a maximum of 15 persons ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For an unlimited number of persons with a minimum lot size of
20,000 square feet; or
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If operated in conjunction with an Adult Care Service, the total
number of clients and residents shall not exceed the total permitted for the
Adult Care Service.
В В В C.В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service and Entertainment
В В В В В В 1.В В В The maximum permitted floor area is 2,000 square feet.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The following standards are required of large bars or dance halls:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Minimum Setbacks
В В В В В В В В В Setbacks for the following use areas are measured from the use area to
a zone boundary line of R-3 or more restrictive zoning :
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Outdoor Use Area: 500 feet.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Parking Area : 200 feet.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Loudspeakers: 500 feet.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The Zoning Examiner may authorize a reduction to the required
setbacks if special physical circumstances exist that will mitigate any
negative impacts.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Security Management Plan
В В В В В В В В В The applicant must submit a security management plan describing the
method and operation of security within and outside the building , including
the parking area , to the Zoning Administrator and the Police Chief. Any
changes or amendments to the plan must be filed with, and approved by, the
Zoning Administrator and the Police Chief.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Access
В В В В В В В В В Access shall be either from a street shown on the Major Streets and
Routes (MS&R) Map with no vehicular access to the site from a local street or
from a local street within a nonresidential development .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Large bars and dance halls are prohibited.
В В В D.В В В Animal Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В Overnight confinement for clinic treatment is permitted for a
maximum of five animals .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Boarding of animals is prohibited.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The activity shall be within a completely enclosed В building .
В В В В В В 4.В В В An outpatient clinic is permitted for small animals only.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The minimum perimeter yard for buildings , shelters, animal runs,
and fenced areas from a property line В adjacent to a residential zone is 100
feet.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Buildings , shelters, animal runs, and fenced areas shall be setback
at least 100 feet from any property line .
В В В В В В 7.В В В The use shall be setback at least 200 feet from any residential
zone.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Accessory use defined as either use of 25% of gross floor area
dedicated to long-term boarding or 25% of the normal animal service use
capacity. Long-term boarding is defined as a stay of five or more consecutive
calendar days.
В В В В В В 9.В В В The minimum perimeter yard for buildings , shelters, animal runs,
and fenced areas from a property line В adjacent to a residential zone is 30
feet.
В В В В В В 10.В В В Building shall be constructed so that direct unaccompanied access
by animals to outside areas is not allowed.
В В В В В В 11.В В В A solid masonry wall or fence six feet in height shall be
constructed along the perimeter of all outdoor animal runs.
В В В В В В 12.В В В Solid waste shall be removed from outdoor exercise areas every five
hours at a minimum during time periods when these areas are in use.
В В В В В В 13.В В В Animals must be accompanied by a facility employee or pet owner at
all times when outside the building . All walks and exercise periods must take
place on facility grounds.
В В В В В В 14.В В В Outdoor operations are limited to 6:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m.
В В В В В В 15.В В В The average noise level, measured at the property line , shall not
exceed 55 dB (one LDN) when measured on an "A weighted" sound level meter and
according to the procedures of the Environmental Protection Agency.
В В В E.В В В Artisan Residence
В В В В В В 1.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В To provide an artisan residence use in the NC, C-1, C-2, and C-
3 commercial zones designed to be used as both a dwelling and work space by an
artist, artisan , or craftsperson, including persons engaged in the
application, teaching, or performance of fine arts such as, but not limited to
drawing, vocal or instrumental music, painting, sculpture, and writing; and the
accessory sale of art objects produced on the premises. Buildings or spaces
within buildings shall be used jointly for commercial and residential purposes.
Nothing in this subsection limits principal uses as otherwise permitted by the
applicable zoning.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Combined Uses
В В В В В В A building or a tenant space in a commercial zone may be used as an
artisan workshop with a residential dwelling. The workshop and workshop
activities shall be located indoors unless outdoor activity is permitted by the
applicable zoning.
В В В В В В 3.В В В On-site  Sales
В В В В В В The on-site sale of the artistвҖҷs products, including occasional shows of
the artistвҖҷs works, is a permitted accessory use .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Separation and Access
В В В В В В Access to the residential use shall be only through the same access that
is provided for the artisan use.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Operational Requirements
В В В В В В The operation of the artisan residence shall not create noise,
vibrations, smoke, fumes, dust, odors, vapors, other noxious emissions, or any
other similar nuisances that are discernible beyond the boundaries of the
building enclosing the artisan residence.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Parking
В В В В В В Each artisan residence unit shall provide two on-site В motor vehicle
parking spaces for each residential component and one on-site parking space for
each 500 square feet of gross floor area (GFA) of the work space area. The GFA
of the residential component shall not be included in the parking calculation.
В В В F.В В В Automotive - Service and Repair
В В В В В В 1.В В В Auto washing is prohibited.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The service building is limited in design to accommodate no more
than two vehicles at any time. The maximum permitted vehicle space size is ten
by 25 feet.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The use must occur within an enclosed В building with one access per
bay , not on the side toward any residential zone.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The building walls shall have no openings, other than non-opening
windows, within 30 feet of the adjacent residential zone boundary line.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Auto washing, limited to a self-service, coin-operated car wash, is
permitted on the premises. No more than six bays using hand-operated, wand-type
equipment or more than one bay using automatic equipment are permitted, and
vacuum equipment shall be located at least 100 feet from any residential zone.
Mechanical equipment that powers the conveyor must be entirely enclosed.
В В В В В В 6.В В В The maximum floor area shall be 2,000 square feet.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Limited to one automatic in-bay car wash that shall not include
conveyors or motorized air-drying. It shall be enclosed within a building
except at points of ingress and egress , have a maximum floor area of 600
square feet, and not exceed 12 feet in height.
В В В G.В В В Billboards
В В В В В В 1.В В В Pedestrian Access
В В В В В В Pedestrian access shall not be located under, or within five feet of, a
vertical line intersecting the ground and any structural element extending from
a billboard, including, but not limited to, overhangs, cantilevered beams, and
elevated walkways. Refer to Section
7-01, Pedestrian Access, of the Technical Standards Manual for requirements.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Parking
В В В В В В Parking area access lanes shall not be located under, or within five feet
of, a vertical line intersecting the ground and any structural element
extending from a billboard, including, but not limited to, overhangs,
cantilevered beams, and elevated walkways.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Loading
В В В В В В Refer to Section
7.5, Off-Street Loading, for loading (servicing) space requirements and Section
7.5.4, Design Criteria, for location requirements for such loading spaces.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Landscaping and Screening
В В В В В В Refer to
Table 7.6.4-1, Landscape Border and Screening Standards, for landscaping and
screening requirements for billboards.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Height
В В В В В В The maximum permitted height shall be in accordance with Section 7A, Sign
Standards.
В В В H.В В В Child Care in Residential and Office Zones
В В В В В В 1.В В В Dispersal
В В В В В В A 600-foot separation between child care centers in residential zones is
required. Child care centers that provide only before- and after-school
programs at elementary and secondary school sites are not considered for the
purposes of this separation. The separation distance is measured from property
lines , except in the case of a child care center on a mixed use В development
В site where the separation is measured from that portion of the site devoted to
the child care use.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Licensing
В В В В В В If licensing is required by the State of Arizona for the use, proof of
such licensure shall be provided prior to issuance of a certificate of
occupancy.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Hours and Days of Operation
В В В В В В Child care centers adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning are limited
in hours of operation to 6:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. and to operation Monday through
Friday only, except as provided in Section
4.9.4.H.9, Extended Hours for any Number of Children.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Building Setbacks
В В В В В В The minimum building setback from all property lines В adjacent to R-3 or
more restrictive zoning is 20 feet, unless the setback requirements of the
applicable zone is greater. Building setbacks may be reduced from the 20 feet
to the setback required under the applicable zone if there are no openings on
the side of the building В adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning . If the
building wall is parallel to the property line , only that side of the building
facing the property line cannot have any openings. If the building is not
parallel to the property line , there cannot be any openings within 20 feet of
the property line . For the purposes of this section, вҖңno openingsвҖқ means all
windows and doors are closed by fixed walls or nonoperable windows. Where
openings are not allowed, an emergency exit door, which can only be opened from
inside the building and has an alarm, is allowed. (
Figure 4.9-B, Building Setback for Child Care in Residential and Office Zones )
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2976.jpg]
Figure 4.9-B Building Setback for Child Care in Residential and Office Zones
В В В В В В 5.В В В Recreation Areas
В В В В В В Minimum distance of outdoor recreation areas, including, but not limited
to, swimming and wading pools, ball fields, and playground equipment, to any
adjacent property in R-3 or more restrictive zoning shall be 25 feet. Where
abutting a street or alley , the setback shall be measured to the centerline of
a right-of-way . A wall shall be required between a recreation area provided on
site and adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Number of Children
В В В В В В The maximum number of children cared for shall be as follows.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В 30 children;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В 100 children; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Unlimited number of children.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Site Area
В В В В В В The minimum required site size are as follows. In no case will a site
larger than ten acres be required.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Six and six-tenths acres in RH and SR;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В  72,000 square feet in SH and RX-1;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В 32,000 square feet in RX-2;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В 14,000 square feet in R-1, R-2, and certain specified uses in O-
2;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В 10,000 square feet in R-3 and certain specified uses in O-3;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The minimum required site size shall be equal to the minimum site
size required for the zone in Section
4.9.4.H.7.a, .b, .c, or .d for the zoning district , divided by 30 and
multiplied by the maximum enrollment for which the center is licensed; and,
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В The minimum required site size shall be equal to 200 square feet
per child, multiplied by the maximum enrollment for which the center is
licensed, plus 4,000 square feet.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Street В Frontage
В В В В В В The center shall front on a Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) street with
no vehicular access to the site from a local street or on a local street within
a nonresidential development . The street В frontage requirements shall be apply
to a child care center located on an elementary school site if the child care
center is in conformance with the dispersal, licensing, recreational area and
building setbacks , hours and days of operation limitations, and site coverage
standards.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Extended Hours for Any Number of Children
В В В В В В In addition to the requirements of Section
4.9.4.H.1, .2, .3, and 8, a child care use that operates before 6:00 a.m. or
after 7:00 p.m. or on Saturday or Sunday where the site is adjacent to R-3 or
more restrictive zoning shall comply with the following.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Site Area
В В В В В В В В В The minimum site size for a child care center with extended hours of
operation or weekend operation shall be two and one half acres , except in RH
and SR where the minimum site shall be ten acres .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Building Setback
В В В В В В В В В The minimum building setback shall be 75 feet from a property line
В adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Recreational Areas
В В В В В В В В В Outdoor recreational areas, including, but not limited to, swimming
and wading pools, ball fields, and playground equipment, shall be screened and
setback from any property line В adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning as
follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В A wall, as defined in screening materials, shall be required
between a recreational area and the adjacent В property line .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The minimum setback from the adjacent property in R-3 or more
restrictive zoning shall be 100 feet. Where abutting a street , alley ,
drainage-way, or other right-of-way , the setback shall be measured to the
centerline of a right-of-way .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A reduction to the required recreational area setback may be
requested in accordance with Section
3.10.1 and
3.10.3, Board of Adjustment Variance Procedure, if the reduction meets the
requirements set forth for special exception land uses .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Vehicular Use Areas
В В В В В В В В В Vehicular use areas shall be designed so that no more than 50% of the
parking spaces provided are within 50 feet of an interior property line
В adjacent to R-3 or more restrictive zoning .
В В В I.В В В Communications
В В В В В В 1.В В В Limited to a radio or television station, provided the buildings do
not occupy more than 30% of the site and are set back at least 50 feet from any
adjoining C-1 or more restrictive zoned property.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Provider вҖҷs Communication Plan. Each wireless communication provider
shall provide a plan of its facilities to the City prior to any application for
the installation of a tower or antennas . The plan shall cover the entire City
and within three miles of the City limits. The plan shall include the
following.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В All of the provider вҖҷs existing towers and antennas , by size and
type, and their coverage areas.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В All presently anticipated future service areas and the types of
antennas and heights desired for each of the service areas .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The various types of antennas and towers used by the provider to
furnish service and when they are used. This includes drawings providing the
sizes and shapes of the antennas and equipment and written materials describing
their application.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The provider вҖҷs policy direction for the mitigation and/or
reduction of existing and proposed towers and antennas to avoid the negative
proliferation of such facilities.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The provider вҖҷs policy direction on the mitigation and/or the
reduction of the negative visual impact created by existing or proposed towers
and antennas , including any proposals to conceal or disguise such facilities
designed to be architecturally and/or environmentally compatible with their
surroundings.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The provider вҖҷs policy direction on collocation of antennas on
their own facilities or on ones from other providers or on other structures
that provide the verticality required for the antennas . The policy shall also
provide that the provider shall not enforce any requirement by an owner of
property that would prohibit collocation .
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Designation of an agent of the provider who is authorized to
receive communications and notices pursuant to this section.
В В В В В В 3.В В В General. The following shall be applicable to all wireless
communication requests.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Noninterference with Public Safety. No wireless communication
transmitter, receptor, or other facility shall interfere with police, fire, and
emergency public safety communications. The Director of Operations for the City
is authorized to determine whether any transmitter, receptor, or other facility
has interfered with public safety communications or is reasonably believed to
be an imminent threat to public safety communications. Upon making that
determination, the Director of Operations shall notify the Zoning Administrator
and the provider responsible for that facility. The Zoning Administrator may
obtain a temporary restraining order from the City Court with or without notice
to enforce this section, provided a hearing is scheduled within five days of
the CourtвҖҷs order.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В All applications for towers / antennas will be reviewed by the
Communications Division of the City of Tucson Operations Department and any
other appropriate public safety department to ensure that the proposed
installation of the towers / antennas will not interfere with any public safety
communications or operations of the City . All applications shall include a
certification by a registered or electrical engineer that each proposed antenna
or tower will be in compliance with all standards established by the Federal
Environmental Protection Agency and Federal Communications Commission (FCC)
regarding potential health and safety hazards. Submittal of information and
review of the application by the Department of Operations shall be in
accordance with all applicable standards.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Any antenna or tower for which the use is discontinued for six
months or more shall be removed, and the property shall be restored to its
condition prior to the location of the antenna or tower , all at the expense of
the provider . The City may require financial assurances to ensure compliance
with this provision.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В No new towers shall be permitted within 400 feet of a designated
Scenic Route or Gateway Route ; within a designated Historic Preservation Zone
(HPZ) or Environmental Resource Zone (ERZ); or on a protected peak or ridge as
identified in a Hillside Development Zone (HDZ) except as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Communication towers and antennas shall be permitted on a
protected peak or ridge that was used for such facilities prior to March 3,
1997, provided any new antennas and towers do not increase the area already
disturbed and the placement of any new towers in such areas is approved as a
special exception in accordance with Section
3.5.3, Zoning Examiner Legislative Procedure.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В New antennas may be permitted under Section
4.9.4.I.4 and.5 if they also comply with the purposes and review procedures of
the overlay zone.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В New communication towers may be permitted on Gateway Routes
in exceptional circumstances, provided there is no alternative and the
placement is approved in accordance with Section
3.5.3, Zoning Examiner Legislative Procedure.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The dimensional provisions of Article 6 as applicable to towers
and antennas shall be superseded by the provisions of Section
4.9.4.I.5 and by the height and setback provisions of Section
4.9.4.I.5, .6, and .7.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В All proposed wireless communication towers and antennas shall be
in compliance with all Federal Communications Commission (FCC) regulations,
including those protecting the public health and those protecting historic
districts.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Submittal Requirements. The following information is to be
submitted with each application for the installation of a tower or antenna .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В An updated provider вҖҷs Communication Plan, including any
proposed changes in the service areas , antennas , towers , or policy
direction;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The proposed antennas /tower location, the type of antennas /
tower , and the proposed service area ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A statement of compliance with FCC requirements and
specifically the areas listed in Section
3.5.4.20.C.6.;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В If the proposed installation involves a new tower , then the
following information is required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В The searched area for the proposed location;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В All existing structures , buildings , towers , etc., of
greater than 20 feet in height located within the searched area; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В A report on why collocation within the search area is not
a viable alternative.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Any technological or engineering requirements which effect or
limit the location, height, or construction of the proposed tower / antennas
should be included in reports.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The following are permitted provided they meet the required Use
Specific Standards. The PDSD Director may forward the request to the Design
Review Board (DRB) for design review and recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are collocated on an existing wireless
communication tower ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The antennas and tower are architecturally and/or
environmentally compatible with the surrounding structure(s) and general area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The existing tower is set back at least the height of the
tower structure from the boundary of any other property zoned residential or
office;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The existing tower may be replaced and/or extended up to six
feet in height to allow for the placement and architectural treatment of the
new antennas ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В The maximum extension of the new antennas and associated
attachments shall not exceed 36 inches as measured perpendicular to the tower
at the point of attachment.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The following requires approval in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure. The PDSD Director  may forward the
request to the Design Review Board (DRB) for design review and recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on the wall or roof of a building ,
or concealed within an architectural or structural element of the building ,
not exceeding the permitted height of the building ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The antennas and tower , or architectural or structural
element, are architecturally and/or environmentally compatible with the
building and general area; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Wall or roof mounted antennas are limited to six feet above
the building , or to 15 feet if the antennas are mounted on top of the roof,
the building is 40 feet high or taller, and no more than six feet of the
antennas can be seen from any point on the street which is a distance from the
building equal to the height of the building .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on an existing structure within
public right-of-way or public property;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The antennas are architecturally and/or environmentally
compatible with the structure and general area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The existing structure may be extended up to ten feet in
height to allow for the placement and architectural treatment of the new
antennas ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The new antennas do not substantially increase the visual
mass of the existing facility.
В В В В В В 6.В В В The following requires approval in accordance with Section
3.3.4, 100' Notice Procedure. The PDSD Director  shall forward the request to
the Design Review Board (DRB) for design review and recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on or within a new tower or
structure in a manner that conceals or disguises the antennas or new tower .
For purposes of this subsection, painting may be a method of concealing or
disguising a tower ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The tower , antennas , and structure are architecturally and/
or environmentally compatible with the surrounding structure(s) and general
area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A new tower is set back at least two times the height of the
tower В structure from the boundary of any other property zoned residential or
office; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The tower and antennas are 50 feet or less in height.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on a new tower or an existing
structure in a manner that is designed or painted so as to minimize their
visual impact;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The tower and antenna are architecturally and/or
environmentally compatible with the existing structures and general area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The tower is set back a minimum of 500 feet from
nonindustrially zoned property except where the nonindustrially zoned property
is used as an interstate highway or railroad; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The tower and antennas are 80 feet or less in height.
В В В В В В 7.В В В The following requires approval as a special exception in accordance
with Section
3.4.3, Zoning Examiner Special Exception Procedure. The Zoning Examiner may
forward the request to the Design Review Board (DRB) for design review and
recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on a new tower and the tower and
antennas are concealed or disguised, or the antennas are collocated on an
existing structure;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The tower and antennas are architecturally and/or
environmentally compatible with the surrounding structure(s) and general area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A new tower is set back at least two times the height of the
structure from the boundary of any property zoned residential or office; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The tower and antennas are 50 feet or less in height.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The antennas are mounted on a new tower and the tower and
antennas are concealed or disguised, or the antennas are collocated on an
existing structure;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The tower and antennas are architecturally and/or
environmentally compatible with the surrounding structure(s) and general area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A new tower is set back at least two times the height of the
structure from the boundary of any property zoned residential or office; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The tower and antennas are 80 feet or less in height.
В В В В В В 8.В В В The following requires approval as a special exception in accordance
with Section
3.4.4, Mayor and Council Special Exception Procedure. The Mayor and Council may
forward the request to the Design Review Board (DRB) for design review and
recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wireless communication antennas , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The tower or antennas are not permitted by other provisions
of this section;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В New towers require a minimum separation of one mile from any
existing tower , regardless of ownership, unless documentation establishes that
no practical alternative exists;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В All appropriate measures shall be taken to conceal or
disguise the tower and antenna from external view;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В All appropriate measures shall be taken to reduce the
negative proliferation of visible towers and antennas by the collocation of new
antennas on existing towers or with the facilities of other providers that are
located or planned for development within the proposed service area ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Notice shall be provided to all agents designated, in
accordance with Section
4.9.4.I.2.g, at least 15 days prior to the date of the public hearing before
the Zoning Examiner.
В В В J.В В В Construction Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В The use shall be setback at least 100 feet from any property line .
В В В В В В 2.В В В The yard shall be completely enclosed by screening a minimum of six
feet in height.
В В В K.В В В Entertainment
В В В В В В 1.В В В A circus, carnival, or tent show shall be permitted for no longer
than 15 days .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Circuses, carnivals, and tent shows are reviewed for compliance in
accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В В В В 3.В В В A circus, carnival, or tent show shall be setback 100 feet from the
activity to the lot line of any residential use or zone.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Motor vehicle parking areas and bicycle facilities for a circus,
carnival, or tent show shall be dust-proofed, and access to the vehicular use
areas shall be identified and controlled to minimize vehicular and pedestrian
conflicts.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The use shall be setback at least 200 feet from any property line .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Concerts, dances, and other similar high-noise activities shall be
conducted entirely within an enclosed В building or setback a minimum of 600
feet from adjacent residentially zoned property. When the activity occurs
outdoors, the high-noise activity shall be directed away from residential
areas. Modification of this criterion may be permitted by the Mayor and Council
through the special exception process provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В All speakers and similar sound projecting devices are oriented
away from adjacent residentially zoned properties;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The applicant will develop a noise mitigation plan for the use
based on the specific activities proposed and the proximity of such activities
to the residential property line ;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Noise levels are continuously monitored during the performance,
and noise emission standards are enforced by a cultural use employee (or
designee); and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The performance ends no later than 10:00 p.m.
В В В L.В В В Financial Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В Limited to a maximum of three drive-through service lanes, with one
restricted to drive-through automated teller machine (ATM) service only.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The use shall be setback at least 100 feet from any property line .
В В В В В В 3.В В В No non-chartered financial institution facilities, such as payday
loan facilities, except where permitted as a special exception under Section
4.9.4.L.4.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Non-chartered financial institutions are reviewed and considered for
approval in accordance with Section
3.4.3, Zoning Examiner Special Exception Procedure, and must comply with the
following.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A non-chartered financial institution В site shall be separated
from other non-chartered financial institution В sites by a distance of 1,320
feet.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A non-chartered financial institution В site shall be located at
least 500 feet, measured in a straight line, from the property line to a zone
boundary line of R-3 or more restrictive zoning .
В В В M.В В В Food Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В Activities may be conducted outdoors. Loudspeakers or music, live or
recorded, shall not be permitted within 600 feet of a residentially zoned
property.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Dancing shall not be permitted. Drive-in or drive-through
restaurants shall not be permitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Soup kitchens are not permitted.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Soup kitchens shall comply with the following standards.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The use shall be conducted within a completely enclosed В building
.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Seating shall be provided at 100% of the meal service capacity.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A waiting area shall be provided within the building for a
minimum of one-half of the total number of persons to be served.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Public rest rooms shall be provided.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В No other soup kitchen use shall be located within one mile.
В В В В В В 5.В В В To differentiate between a restaurant that serves alcoholic
beverages and a bar that serves food, a restaurant meets the following
standards.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The full kitchen remains open and meals are available to patrons
during all hours of operation, except as follows. Restaurants open later than
10:00 p.m. must keep the full kitchen open until at least 10:00 p.m. to
maintain their classification as a Restaurant.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Management cannot ask for age verification (carding) for
admittance to the establishment.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Management cannot restrict patronage by age or sex (i.e., Ladies
Night, Over 21, etc.) except for limited special services or events within
certain limited areas of the establishment.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A cover charge cannot be required for general admittance, except
for special services or events offered within certain areas of the
establishment.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Management shall maintain a minimum amount of table seating at
all times and not have tables removed to create a dance floor or set aside
major sections of the establishment for special events.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The gross revenue sale of food complies with A.R.S. В§ 4-205.02(G)
(2).
В В В В В В В В В 6.В В В All Food Court sites must be reviewed, permitted, and developed
in accordance with the UDC and the adopted Building Code, including those for
signage and waste collection, and shall comply with the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A business license is required to be held for the entire
facility and on-site management is required to be present during all hours of
operation to ensure compliance with the required standards.
В В В В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Retail sales vendors are permitted as an accessory use to the
Food Court but shall not exceed 50% of the designated vendor stalls.
В В В В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Canopies, awnings, or shade structures designed to shield
operators or patrons must meet the UDC and adopted Building Code requirements
and must be permanently affixed to the ground.
В В В В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Compliance with the adopted Outdoor Lighting Code and outdoor
lighting standards is required.
В В В В В В В В В 7.В В В If all, or part, of the Food Court operates outdoors, those
outdoor portions shall also comply with the following standards, including
outdoor lighting requirements:
В В В В В В В В В В В В a.В В В All vendors shall be located within a developed stall. The
number of vendors will be limited by the number of stalls.
В В В В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Permanent power pedestals for each vendor stall are required.
A central grease interceptor to serve all vendors is also required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Temporary or permanent accessible restrooms and a parking area
with delineated parking spaces with a dust-proof surface may be permitted as
exceptions to the standard requirements.
В В В В В В В В В В В В d.В В В If located within an existing developed site, such as a
shopping center parking area , excess available parking may be allocated to the
requirements for the Food Court. Parking spaces utilized for vendor parking and
patron access may continue to be counted towards the parking provided on-site.
On-site circulation standards and fire routes must be maintained. Additional
landscaping (UDC Article
7.6) and stormwater retention and detention standards (TSM Sect 4-03.0.0) are
not required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В e.В В В If located within an undeveloped site and permanent paving is
not provided, landscaping (UDC Article
7.6) and stormwater retention and detention standards (TSM Sect
4-03.0.0 ) do not apply.
В В В В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The outdoor hours of operation are limited to 7 a.m. to 10
p.m.
В В В В В В В В В В В В g.В В В The use of outdoor internal combustion generators is
prohibited.
В В В N.В В В Funeral Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В A crematorium shall be setback a minimum of 200 feet from any
property line .
В В В В В В 2.В В В A crematorium shall be setback a minimum of 500 feet from any
boundary of the site that adjoins property in a residential zone.
В В В O.В В В Medical Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В The use shall provide a minimum of one off-street loading space or
the number of spaces required in accordance with Section
7.4, Off-Street Loading, whichever is greater.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Blood donor centers are not permitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Blood donor centers shall comply with the following standards.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The site is not located in the same block as a residential zone
or any elementary or secondary school or Day Care use.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The site is located at least 300 feet, measured in a straight
line, from the property line to a residential zone boundary line or the
property line of an elementary or secondary school.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A waiting area equal to 10% of the gross floor area is provided.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В No other blood donor center is located within 1,200 feet.
В В В P.В В В Medical Service in Residential and Office Zones Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В The facility must front on a street on the Major Streets and Routes
(MS&R) Plan or on a residential street , provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The residential street intersects a street on the MS&R Plan ;
and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The property is within 150 feet of a street on the MS&R Plan ,
measured along the residential street .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Minimum Setbacks .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A minimum perimeter yard of 20 feet from any interior lot line
В adjacent to a residential zone is required.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A minimum perimeter yard of 100 feet from any property line is
required.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Requests to vary the provisions of this section, including setbacks
for existing or new buildings , are processed in accordance with Section
3.10.1 and
3.10.3, Board of Adjustment Variance Procedure.
В В В В В В 4.В В В If licensing is required by the State of Arizona for the use, proof
of such licensure shall be provided.
В В В Q.В В В Major Medical Service and Outpatient Medical Service, Accessory Uses
В В В В В В 1.В В В Food Service; General Merchandise Sales; Personal Service or other
permitted accessory use to a Medical Service, Major use, except as noted in
4.9.4.Q.2
В В В В В В В В В a. В В В The use shall be located within a principal building .
В В В В В В В В В b. В В В Access to the use shall be from within the principal building or
from the interior of the development .
В В В В В В В В В c. В В В Outside display or storage of merchandise or supplies is
prohibited.
В В В В В В В В В d. В В В A sign(s) associated with the accessory use shall not be visible
from any public street .
В В В В В В В В В e. В В В Sale of items shall be restricted to those associated with
medical services.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Transportation Services, Air Carrier, Helipad, as an accessory use
to a Medical Service, Major use.
В В В В В В В В В a. В В В The primary use shall be a medical facility licensed by the
State of Arizona as a general or special hospital with a minimum of 50 beds.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The helipad shall be in compliance with all applicable
regulations of the Federal Aviation Administration, including Circular No. 150/
5390-2C "Helistop and Heliport Design," or subsequent revisions to this
advisory circular at the time of construction.
В В В R.В В В Office Zone В Compatibility Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В Chain link fencing shall not be used to meet screening requirements.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Refuse areas shall be setback at least 20 feet from street В property
lines and interior property lines В abutting a residential use or zone, except
when alley pickup is approved by the Environmental Services Department.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Refuse areas shall be screened from the street with materials
similar to those of the principal building .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Noise-generating equipment shall be located in an area away from
adjacent residential uses or vacant residentially zoned property.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Vehicular access shall be from other than internal residential
neighborhood streets .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Consolidation of parking areas and access points shall be required
for sites with multiple buildings . Consolidation of parking areas and access
points is encouraged for adjoining В sites.
В В В В В В 7.В В В New construction shall be reviewed by the Design Review Board (DRB)
for architectural and site design compatibility with the surrounding
residential area. The DRB review shall include architectural style, building
elevations, materials on exterior facades, color schemes, new mechanical
equipment locations, lighting of outdoor areas, window locations and types,
screening, landscaping , vehicular use areas , and other contributing design
features. The DRB recommendation shall be made to the PDSD for determination of
compatibility . The DRB shall find that:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The building materials on the exterior facade are compatible with
those used within the neighborhood block ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Color schemes of the new building are compatible with those of
the developments on the block face;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The exterior facade of new construction is of a residential type
architecturally if adjacent to a residential use or vacant residentially zoned
property;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The building scale of new construction complements, rather than
is dominant in scale to, adjacent В projects ; and,
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Any new mechanical equipment located on the roof are screened and
that the screen is integrally designed as part of the architectural style of
the building . If this cannot be done, the equipment shall be located at ground
level and screened from the street .
В В В S.В В В вҖңPвҖқ Parking Zone Standards
В В В Parking is restricted to at or below grade . Structures to cover-grade
parking is permitted.
В В В T.В В В Personal Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В Laundry facilities shall not exceed 3,000 square feet of floor area
.
В В В В В В 2.В В В An attendant shall be on the premises during all open hours.
В В В В В В 3.В В В No more than 24 dry cleaning units are permitted.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The maximum floor area is 2,000 square feet.
В В В U.В В В Research and Product Development
В В В В В В 1.В В В All land use operations, including storage, shall be housed within a
completely enclosed В building . Vehicular use areas do not have to be located
within enclosed В buildings .
В В В В В В 2.В В В The minimum lot size requirement is 10,000 square feet.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Every building shall be setback from property lines В adjacent to
residential uses or zones a distance equal to three times the height of the
building .
В В В V.В В В Sale of Spirituous Liquor in Conjunction with Food Service Use
В В В В В В 1.В В В The Food Service establishment shall provide regular meal service at
regularly available tables during all hours of operation or until 10:00 p.m. to
guests for compensation. The establishment shall have kitchen facilities for
keeping, cooking, and preparing foods for meals .
В В В В В В 2.В В В The Food Service establishment shall have 75 seats or more for the
serving of meals at regularly available tables for all hours of operation.
Seating at counters, in private banquet rooms, and outdoor seating shall not
count toward the minimum 75-seat requirement.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The Food Service establishment may serve alcoholic beverages with
meals ; however, an area separate from the Food Service use shall not be
provided with tables, counter areas, and/or booths for persons to sit at
primarily for service of alcoholic beverages operating as an Alcoholic Beverage
Service establishment, unless:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The Food Service establishment has 75 seats or more for the
serving of meals at tables (seating at counters, in banquet rooms, and outdoor
seating shall not count toward the 75-seat requirement) that are available at
all hours of operation;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The Alcoholic Beverage Service area does not exceed, in size, 25%
of the Food Service floor area , including any Food Service outdoor area; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The Alcoholic Beverage Service area does not have an outside
public entrance separate from the main entrance to the Food Service use.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The Food Service establishment may serve other alcoholic beverages
in addition to beer and wine.
В В В В В В 5.В В В A Food Service use with more than 75 seats for the serving of meals
at tables that are available at all hours of operation may have a dance floor,
provided such dance floor shall not be larger than 200 square feet. Seating at
counters, in banquet rooms, and outdoor seating shall not count toward the 75-
seat requirement.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Signs are not permitted in or on a window or on the exterior of any
building or structure within the project that is visible from a public street
or adjacent residentially zoned property identifying or advertising the
Alcoholic Beverage Service use or the sale of spirituous liquors.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Alcoholic beverages shall not be provided with drive-in or drive-
through services.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service may be provided outdoors if the outdoor
area is a minimum of 100 feet from residentially zoned property excluding
public right-of-way , or is separated by a building from adjacent residentially
zoned property. If the use is within 600 feet of a residential zone, no
loudspeakers or music, live or recorded, are permitted.
В В В В В В 9.В В В A Food Service establishment that is located within 300 feet of a
residential zone, excluding public right-of-way , measured in a straight line
from the licensed premises to the zone boundary line of R-3 or more restrictive
zoning may serve alcoholic beverages upon conformance with the following
conditions:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The applicant is required to submit a mitigation plan to the PDSD
Director that will be reviewed in accordance with Section
3.3.5, 400' Notice Procedure. The mitigation  plan shall specifically address
noise from the use, including parking lot  noise, screening of lighting from
vehicle headlights and light standards on site , parking, and access to
adjacent  neighborhoods, but shall not address issues that are the purview of
the Arizona Department of Liquor Licenses and Control, such as the number of
liquor licenses in the area or the hours of liquor sales.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the use is operated in a manner that violates the mitigation
plan or conditions for permitting the use or causes adverse land use impacts,
the use may be suspended or terminated in accordance with Section
10.4, Penalties and Remedies.
В В В W.В В В Technical Service
В В В В В В 1.В В В The maximum permitted gross floor area is 1,500 square feet.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The maximum permitted gross floor area is 3,500 square feet.
В В В X.В В В Trade Service and Repair
В В В В В В 1.В В В Major
В В В If located within 30 feet of a residential use or zone, the building walls
shall have no openings, other than non-opening windows, within 30 feet of the
adjacent residential use or zone boundary line.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Minor
В В В Use shall exclude the repair and replacement of upholstery and the repair of
burglar alarms and motor vehicle stereos.
В В В Y.В В В Transportation Service, Air Carrier
В В В В В В 1.В В В The minimum site area shall be as required by the Federal Aviation
Agency (FAA) for the airport facility. The site area shall not be less than
permitted by the underlying zoning.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The Mayor and Council may restrict the number of flights and hours
of operation due to the proximity and intensity of noise-sensitive land uses .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The runway length required for safe operation shall be as shown in
the flight manual of the largest aircraft to use the site and an additional 240
feet at each end of the runway.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The runway width shall be 240 feet centered on the runway.
В В В Z.В В В TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Campsite
В В В В В В 1.В В В The use shall be in conjunction with, and within, a mobile home
park.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The overall number of mobile home spaces and campsites shall not
exceed the overall density permitted for the mobile home park.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The campsite area shall not be located adjacent to a non-mobile home
residential zone.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Access to the area shall be through the mobile home park.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The area shall comply with the mobile home park development
standards.
В В В В В В 6.В В В No more than 25% of the total spaces in the mobile home park can be
used for the campsite use.
В В В AA.В В В TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Lodging - Accessory Uses
В В В В В В 1.В В В The minimum required distance to any public street is 100 feet.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Outside entrances shall not face any public street .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The use shall be operated for the convenience of the guests.
В В В В В В 4.В В В When the Alcoholic Beverage Service use is provided in an area set
aside from the Food Service area, such as a bar or cocktail lounge, that area
of the Alcoholic Beverage Service use shall not have an outside public entrance
separate from the entrance to the principal use .
В В В В В В 5.В В В The total area of retail and service establishments shall not exceed
5% of the total floor area .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Animal -Related Uses
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В No more than one animal for each 10,000 square feet of site area
.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В No stable or corral shall be located within 100 feet of any
property line or within 300 feet of any existing buildings on an adjacent
property at the time of the improvement.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В No stable or corral shall be located or maintained closer than 50
feet to any property line or closer than 100 feet to any school, park, public
street (excepting an alley ), or dwelling on adjoining properties.
В В В В В В 7.В В В TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Lodging, shall have at least 50 rooms for
transient lodging.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service may be provided outdoors if the outdoor
area is a minimum of 100 feet from residentially zoned property or is separated
by a building from adjacent residentially zoned property. If the use is within
600 feet of a residential zone, no loudspeakers or music, live or recorded, are
prohibited.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Alcoholic beverages shall not be provided with drive-in or drive-
through services.
В В В В В В 10.В В В No alcoholic beverages , except beer and wine, shall be served.
В В В В В В 11.В В В Signs are not permitted in or on a window or on the exterior of any
building or structure visible from a public street or from adjacent
residentially zoned property identifying or advertising the Alcoholic Beverage
Service use or the sale of spirituous liquors.
В В В В В В 12.В В В Large Bars are prohibited.
В В В В В В 13.В В В Soup Kitchens are prohibited.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11410, 11/22/2016; Am. Ord. 11508, 12/5/
2017; Am. Ord. 11608, passed 12/18/2018; Am. Ord. 11609, passed 12/18/2018; Am.
Ord. 11732, 2/19/2020; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/2023; Am. Ord. 12060, 11/28/2023)
4.9.5. INDUSTRIAL USE GROUP
В В В A.В В В Craftwork Manufacturing
В В В В В В 1.В В В The area devoted to the use shall not exceed 25% of the gross floor
area or 1,000 square feet, whichever is less.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The products manufactured on site shall be sold at retail on the
premises.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The products manufactured on site shall be limited to leather-craft,
jewelry, and clothing.
В В В B.В В В Extraction
В В В В В В 1.В В В The use shall be setback at least 300 feet from any property line ,
except that the use may be closer than 300 feet to an abutting I-2 zoned parcel
with the written consent of the owner of the abutting property.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The generation of dust shall be minimized.
В В В C.В В В Manufacturing and Industrial (Includes General, Heavy Equipment,
Precision, and Primary Manufacturing Land Uses )
В В В В В В 1.В В В The manufacturing activity shall be located on the same lot or site
with the permitted use.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Access shall be from a street . Access from an alley shall also be
permitted, provided the alley is at least 20 feet wide; it is paved for its
entire block length; and zoning on both sides of the alley for its entire
length within the block is not residential or office.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Reserved.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Manufacturing uses shall be conducted wholly within completely
enclosed В buildings .
В В В В В В 5.В В В All work and activity in connection with, and accessory to, a
manufacturing use, such as loading and unloading areas, docks, and platforms,
shall be located entirely within a building or an area on the lot that shall be
enclosed on all sides by a solid wall or fence six feet in height . Such
loading and unloading areas, docks, and platforms shall be located as far from
any surrounding residential uses or zones as is reasonably possible.
В В В В В В 6.В В В The manufacturing and allied uses and activities shall be conducted
in conformance with the following use specific standards:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Noise
В В В В В В В В В The sound level of any individual operation shall not exceed the
levels permitted in Section 16-31, Excessive Noise, of the Tucson Code.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Smoke
В В В В В В В В В Smoke emission from any source shall not exceed a density greater than
that described as No. 1 on the Ringleman Chart. However, smoke may be emitted
that is equal to, but not darker than, No. 2 on the Ringleman Chart for not
more than four minutes in any 30-minute period. For the purpose of grading the
density of smoke, the Ringleman Chart, as published by the U.S. Bureau of
Mines, shall be the standard.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Glare or Heat
В В В В В В В В В Any activity producing intense glare or heat shall not be performed in
such a manner as to create a nuisance or hazard beyond the property lines .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Odors
В В В В В В В В В Emission of odorous gases or other odorous matter shall not be
permitted in such quantities as to be offensive to owners or occupants of
adjoining property or in such a manner as to create a nuisance or hazard beyond
the property lines .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Vibration
В В В В В В В В В No vibration shall be permitted that is discernible beyond the
property lines to the human sense of feeling for a duration of three minutes or
more in any one hour of the day between the hours of 7:00 a.m. and 7:00 p.m. or
of a duration of 30 seconds or more in any one hour between the hours of 7:00
p.m. and 7:00 a.m.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Fly Ash, Dust, Fumes, Vapors, Gases, and Other Forms of Air
Pollution
В В В В В В В В В No emission shall be permitted that can cause damage to health,
animals , vegetation, or other forms of property or that can cause any
excessive soiling of the air.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Liquids and Solid Waste
В В В В В В В В В No waste shall be discharged into the streets , drainage-ways, or on
property possibly creating a danger to the public health and safety, and no
waste shall be discharged in the public sewage system that might endanger the
normal operation of the public sewage system.
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Illumination
В В В В В В В В В Illumination of buildings and open areas shall be located and directed
so as to eliminate glare toward streets and adjoining properties and shall
comply with the requirements of the Tucson Code, Chapter 6, (Outdoor Lighting
Code).
В В В В В В 7.В В В Soap manufacturing is limited to cold process only.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Outdoor storage, when permitted, shall not be located in a street
В perimeter yard . Outdoor storage areas shall be screened in accordance with
Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening. No materials or products shall be stacked or
stored to exceed the height  of the screen  within 50 feet of an adjoining
residential zone or street . Outdoor display of finished products is permitted
within a street В perimeter yard  in an area limited to 10% of the site area  or
10,000 square feet, whichever is less. Such display area shall not encroach
into required landscape buffer areas. Outdoor storage in the P-I zone is
limited to 25% of the site area .
В В В В В В 9.В В В Accessory Manufacturing uses to Commercial Services, Retail Trade,
or Wholesaling Use Groups, are permitted:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В In the C-2 zone, limited to 25% of the gross floor area except as
follows. Accessory manufacturing uses may occupy more than 25% of the gross
floor area if the use complies with Section
4.9.5, Industrial Use Group.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В In the C-3 zone, limited to 33% of the gross floor area except as
follows. Accessory manufacturing uses may occupy more than 33% of the gross
floor area if the use complies with Section
4.9.5, Industrial Use Group.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В In the OCR-1 and OCR-2 zones , limited to 50% of the gross floor
area . Exception: The accessory may exceed 50% of the gross floor area if it
complies with the applicable use-specific standards in Section
4.9.5, Industrial Use Group.
В В В В В В 10.В В В The accessory use is limited to:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Those products that are wholesaled, manufactured, or stored on
the premises; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В No more than 25% of the total area, but not to exceed 2,500
square feet.
В В В D.В В В Motion Picture Industry
В В В В В В 1.В В В Buildings and structures shall be setback at least 100 feet from the
boundary of the site .
В В В E.В В В Perishable Goods Manufacturing
В В В В В В 1.В В В The manufacturing of fish or meat products, sauerkraut, sugar beets,
vinegar, and yeast and the rendering or refining of fats and oils shall not be
permitted.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Slaughterhouses, meat packing plants, fertilizer yards, and plants
for the reduction of animal matter is not permitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The building setback shall be at least 300 feet from any property
line .
В В В В В В 4.В В В The area devoted to the accessory land use is limited to a maximum
of 33% of the gross floor area .
В В В В В В 5.В В В The accessory land use is limited to baked goods and confectionery
products.
В В В В В В 6.В В В The accessory land use is limited to the manufacturing of beer,
вҖңmicrobrewery.вҖқ
В В В В В В 7.В В В The area devoted to the accessory land use shall not exceed 25% of
the gross floor area or 1,000 square feet, whichever is less.
В В В В В В 8.В В В The products manufactured on site must be sold at retail on the
premises.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Accessory Manufacturing uses to Commercial Services, Retail Trade,
or Wholesaling Use Groups, are permitted in the:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В In the C-2 Zone , limited to 25% of the gross floor area except
as follows. Accessory manufacturing uses may occupy more than 25% of the gross
floor area if the use complies with Section
4.9.5.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В In the C-3 Zone , limited to 33% of the gross floor area except
as follows. Accessory manufacturing uses may occupy more than 33% of the gross
floor area if the use complies with Section
4.9.5.
В В В F.В В В Radioactive Materials Restrictions
В В В В В В 1.В В В Manufacturing activities involving the use, storage, or disposal of
radioactive material shall be prohibited, except as permitted below.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Radioactive material that does not become an integral part of the
manufactured product or is exempt from the licensing requirements of, or is
permitted under, a general license issued by the Arizona Atomic Energy
Commission or its legally established successor, may be used, stored, or
disposed of as part of a manufacturing activity.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Radioactive material may be used in medical diagnosis and therapy or
in medical, educational, or industrial research and development .
В В В G.В В В Salvaging and Recycling
В В В В В В 1.В В В Salvaging and Recycling is limited to recycling collection bins
only.
В В В В В В 2.В В В All Salvaging and Recycling activities and storage shall occur
within an enclosed В building .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Salvaging and Recycling is limited to empty household product
containers, such as, but not limited to, aluminum cans, glass and plastic
bottles, and newspaper.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Junkyards, salvaging of motorized vehicles, such as cars, trucks,
airplanes, and similar vehicles; salvaging of hazardous materials; and
landfills are not permitted.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Salvaging and Recycling is restricted to collection of recycling
materials.
В В В В В В 6.В В В In addition to the requirements of Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening, there shall be a screen  wall at least six feet
in height  between this use and any residential zone .
В В В В В В 7.В В В The household goods donation center shall be located in a permanent
building staffed by an attendant who remains on the premises during the
centerвҖҷs hours of operation.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Security procedures or physical barriers shall be provided to
prevent after-hours drop-off of donated items and to prohibit nighttime access
to these items.
В В В В В В 9.В В В The applicant shall submit a security management plan that describes
the operation of the center, to the Zoning Administrator for review and
approval. The plan shall include: the days and hours of operation for the
center; descriptions of the planned security procedures and/or physical
barriers planned to prohibit both vehicular access when the center is closed
and outside storage when the center is closed; the outdoor lighting schematic;
and the operational procedures planned to implement the security management
plan. Any changes or amendments to the security management plan shall be filed
with and approved by the Zoning Administrator. If the use is operated in a
manner that violates the security plan or conditions for permitting the use,
the use may be suspended in accordance with Section
10.4, Penalties and Remedies.
В В В В В В 10.В В В Signs shall be posted on the site providing the days and hours of
operation and stating that donations are not to be left after the center has
closed.
В В В В В В 11.В В В One drive-through service lane may be provided for customer drop-
off of donated items.
В В В В В В 12.В В В The household goods donation center shall be located on, and have
access from, either a street designated as an arterial street in the Major
Streets and Routes Plan (MS&R) or a local street in a commercial or industrial
park that has direct access to an arterial street .
4.9.6. RECREATION USE GROUP
В В В A.В В В Golf Course
В В В В В В 1.В В В Standards for Permitted and Special Exception Land Uses
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В At least nine holes are required.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В There must be at least 75 feet between a tee and its hole.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A tee and cup must be at least 100 feet from any property line .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A driving range shall be placed so that flying balls are directed
toward the interior of the site .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Subject to lighting requirements, Section
4.9.13.C, Lighting.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Activities may occur outdoors.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Standards for Accessory Uses to Golf Courses
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Permitted only as part of, and in conjunction with, a Food
Service use. All requirements for dispensing alcoholic beverages listed for
Food Service, Section
4.9.6.A.2.b, apply.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The Alcoholic Beverage Service area may not have a separate
outside entrance. Access shall be through the Food Service use area.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Large bars are not permitted.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Food Service
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The Food Service establishment shall have kitchen facilities
for keeping, cooking, and preparing foods for meals .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Food Service establishments with at least 75 seats for dining
at regularly available tables may operate a cocktail lounge or bar to sell all
spirituous liquors for on-premises consumption.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Soup kitchens are not permitted.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Personal Service and General Merchandise Sales
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Personal Service and General Merchandise Sales shall be
integrated into the main building associated with the principal use , such as a
clubhouse or registration office. Access shall be from within such main
building .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В No sign associated with the Personal Service or General
Merchandise Sales shall be visible from the exterior of the building .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В General Merchandise Sales shall be limited to the sale of
items related to the principal use only.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В In addition to Section
4.9.6.A.2.a, .b, and .c, the following requirements apply.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The minimum distance to any public street shall be 100 feet.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Outside entrances shall not face any public street .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The use is operated for the convenience of the users of the
Golf Course.
4.9.7. RESIDENTIAL USE GROUP
В В В A.В В В Artist Studio/Residence in I-1
В В В В В В 1.В В В The use is limited to buildings in existence on February 25, 1991,
within the designated Artist Studio/Residence Area as defined in Section
11.4.2, Definitions-A.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The use shall not displace existing industrial uses.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The appearance and structural integrity of the building shall be
preserved or enhanced.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Residential use of the property shall be incidental and accessory to
the artist studio use, with no greater than 50% of the floor area devoted to
the accessory residential use.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The accessory residential use shall be occupied by an artist who is
also the occupant of the principal artist studio use.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Adequate measures shall be provided to assure the health, safety,
and welfare of the occupants in relation to any industrial process, use, or
storage carried out in the artist studio/residence or on adjacent properties.
В В В В В В 7.В В В The use shall not impair or interfere with the continued industrial
use of adjacent properties or with the purposes of the industrial zone.
В В В B.В В В Family Dwelling
В В В В В В 1.В В В All dwellings shall be under the same ownership and on the same
parcel .
В В В В В В 2.В В В All dwellings shall comply with the dimensional standards in
accordance with Article 6.
В В В В В В 3.В В В One dwelling must be at least 25% smaller than the other dwelling.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The dwellings may be attached or detached.
В В В В В В 5.В В В A manufactured housing unit is not considered a dwelling for the
purposes of this section, unless it is located in a subdivision recorded after
January 1, 1982, and not located in a re-subdivision of any subdivision
recorded prior to January 1, 1982.
В В В В В В 6.В В В A manufactured housing unit is not considered a dwelling for the
purposes of this section, unless the following standards are met or a
subdivision В plat that has been clearly identified for manufactured housing has
been approved and permits for manufactured homes have been issued for 25% or
more of the lots prior to December 27, 2000:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В It is placed on an excavated and backfilled foundation (ground
set). If the home is located in a floodplain, the bottom of the lowest floor
joist shall be a minimum of one foot above the 100-year/base flood elevation,
and the foundation of the home shall be placed on the fill ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В No more than eight inches of perimeter skirting wall shall be
visible from the street В frontage of the property. The perimeter skirting wall
shall match the exterior color of the home;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The exterior siding materials shall be either hardboard, vinyl,
or stucco;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Concrete or masonry steps shall be provided for the entry on the
street side of the dwelling;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В All entry doors on the street side of the dwelling shall be
inward-swinging doors; and,
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The roofing style and materials shall be either flat roof
(Pueblo), steel rib roof, or shingle or tile roof with a 3:12 roof pitch or
greater. All pitched roofs, except those that are disguised by a parapet, shall
have a minimum six inch eave on all sides of the dwelling.
В В В В В В 7.В В В For any new single-family dwelling in the R-1 zone with five
bedrooms , three parking spaces are required. For every bedroom over five, one
additional space is required for each bedroom . For all single-family dwellings
in the R-1 zone with five or more bedrooms , and for all lots containing two or
more single-family dwellings, with the second dwelling having more than one
bedroom , all parking spaces must be located on-site , either in a side or rear
perimeter yard . Parking spaces may not be located in a vehicular use area in
any front street В perimeter yard . A parking area is not required to be
developed to a commercial standard. A dustproof parking surface is required to
at least a minimum of two inches of compacted pea gravel. An existing covered
residential parking area may be used.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Subject to the requirements of this Section
4.9.7.B.9, any group dwelling use existing prior to February 15, 2012 shall be
treated as a lawful nonconforming use and structure in accordance with Article
9, Nonconforming Uses , Buildings , and Structures, except as otherwise
expressly provided in this Section and in Tucson Code Section 16-37. A
protected development right  plan approved by the Mayor and Council prior to
the enactment of this ordinance shall be treated as a nonconforming use  for a
maximum number of unrelated persons  equivalent to the number of bedrooms  on
the approved plan.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The right to treatment as a nonconforming use or structure under
this section is established by the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Evidence that the property was registered as a rental
property as required by A.R.S. В§33-1902 on or before February 15, 2012, or is
otherwise timely registered as required by that statute; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Evidence acceptable to the Zoning Administrator that
establishes the maximum number of unrelated persons to whom the dwelling was
leased pursuant to subsection (a) above. Such evidence may include executed
leases, tax records, an approved protected development right plan as described
in this section, or other documentation. An executed lease that was executed
during the calendar year 2009, 2010, or 2011 shall be sufficient under this
subsection to establish the number of persons to whom the dwelling was leased.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If a group dwelling is established for treatment as a lawful
nonconforming use as provided in subsection (1) above, the maximum number of
unrelated persons to whom the dwelling may be leased shall be that number
established by the evidence provided under subsection (1)(b) above.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Notwithstanding the provisions of Section
9.2.1.B, an established nonconforming use under this Section
4.9.7.B.9 shall not be deemed abandoned or lost based upon the leasing of the
dwelling after February 15, 2012 to less than five unrelated persons , to
related persons , or to a single family ; or upon the failure to lease the
dwelling, provided that it is continually registered as a rental property as
described under A.R.S. В§33-1902.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The nonconforming use under this Section
4.9.7.B.9 may be suspended or lost as provided in Section
9.2.1.B if it is declared a public nuisance by court order pursuant to Tucson
Code Section
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В To the extent any of the provisions of Tucson Code Section 16-37
or this Section
4.9.7.B.9 conflict with Article 9: Nonconforming Uses , Buildings , and
Structures relating to the discontinuance or abandonment of a nonconforming use
, the provisions of this Section and Tucson Code Section 16-37 shall control.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Occupancy of a dwelling by five or more unrelated persons is a group
dwelling and is not permitted.
В В В В В В 10.В В В The following standards apply to family dwellings in the Rio Nuevo
District:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The maximum density is 17 units per acre ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A minimum lot size of 2,500 square feet is required;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The maximum permitted lot coverage is 90%;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A minimum perimeter yard equal to one and one-half times the
height of the proposed exterior building wall is required from a property line
В adjacent to a residential zone; and,
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В A perimeter yard is not required from a property line В adjacent
to a nonresidential zone.
В В В В В В 11.В В В Privacy mitigation is required when multistory residences are
proposed within 10 feet of a property line В adjacent to existing single-story
residences and the existing residences are zoned R-2 or more restrictive.
Balconies, windows (except for clerestory and translucent windows), or any
other features that overlook the rear and side yards of adjacent residences are
discouraged. Privacy mitigation measures, such as the incorporation of
vegetation, screening elements, walls, building placement and orientation,
balcony and window placement and orientation, or other design strategies shall
be incorporated.
В В В В В В 12.В В В Parking for middle housing is required per UDC Table
7.4.4-1. Required parking may be met with on-street parking or waived if the
site is within a quarter-mile walking distance of an "All Ages and Abilities
Bikeway" as defined by the Department of Transportation and Mobility or a
public transit route. Parking provided is exempt from Section
7.6.4, Landscape Standards. Front yards are permitted one driveway no more than
20 feet in width and up to 400 square feet of additional vehicular use area.
В В В В В В 13.В В В Middle housing development and multifamily development of 3 or 4
units shall not require a commercial site plan per UDC
3.3.3.G., require commercial development standards, or require improvements to
public streets other than repairs due to construction activity.
В В В В В В 14.В В В Street perimeter yard s may be reduced to match the perimeter yard
of an adjacent property on the same street. The perimeter yard may be reduced
to no less than 10 feet. Additional reductions may be potentially requested
through a variance or design development option.
В В В C.В В В Family Dwelling or Mobile Home Dwelling as Accessory Use in Industrial
Zones
В В В В В В 1.В В В The dwelling is permitted as an accessory use to an Industrial,
Wholesaling, or Storage Use in the P-I, I-1, and I-2 zones .
В В В В В В 2.В В В The use is restricted to one single-family or mobile home dwelling
for a caretaker of the principal use .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The dwelling shall conform to the development standards for the
principal use .
В В В D.В В В Home Occupations are permitted as Accessory Land Uses to Mobile Home
Dwelling or Family Dwelling as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Home occupations other than those specified below are required to
comply with Section
4.9.7.E, Home Occupation : General Standards; or,
В В В В В В 2.В В В The following uses within the specified zones are required to comply
as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Home Occupation : Day Care is permitted in SR, SH, RX-1, RX-2, R-
1, R-2, R-3, MH-1, MH-2, O-1, O-2, O-3, NC, and C-1 subject to: 4.9.7.E.1, .2,
.3, .5, .6, .7, .9, .10, .11, & .13 and F.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Home Occupation : Group Dwelling is permitted in IR, RH, SR, SH,
RX-1, RX-2, R-1, R-2, R-3, MH-1, and MH-2 subject to: 4.9.7.G.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Home Occupation : TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Lodging is permitted
in SR, SH, RX-1, RX-2, R-1, MH-1, O-1, O-2, and NC subject to: 4.9.7.H.1 & .5 -
.11.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Home Occupation : TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Lodging is permitted
in R-2 and MH-2 subject to: 4.9.7.H.2 & .5 - .11.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Home Occupation : TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, Lodging is permitted
in R-3 and O-3 subject to: 4.9.7.H.4 - .11.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Home Occupation : Where Animal Production, Crop Production,
General Farming, and/or Stockyard Operation is permitted as a principal use,
the same standards and procedure required to establish the applicable principal
use is required when establishing the home occupation.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Home Occupation : Urban Farm is permitted in O-1, O-2, O-3, NC,
C-1, C-2, C-3, MU, OCR-1, OCR-2 subject to: 4.9.2.E and 4.9.7.E.5.
В В В E.В В В Home Occupation : General Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В Home occupations require review and consideration for approval in
accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The home occupation shall be clearly secondary to the residential
use of the dwelling.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The home occupation shall be conducted in such a manner that it is
compatible with the residential character of the neighborhood in which it is
located.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Except for multifamily development , no more than 25% of all
buildings on the lot may be devoted to the home occupation . For multifamily
development , no more than 25% of the dwelling unit may be devoted to the home
