not required when the use of the non-industrially zoned property is a railroad
or freeway right-of-way :
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Extraction;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Hazardous Material Manufacturing;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Perishable Goods Manufacturing;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Primary Manufacturing;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Refining; or,
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Salvaging and Recycling.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Use must comply with the applicable standards of Section
4.9.5, Industrial Use Group.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The airport authority land use class may contain residential,
commercial, and industrial land uses compatible with the airport environs
overlay. These uses include but are not limited to typical airport uses such as
terminals, runways, control towers, radio and telecommunication towers, fueling
and maintenance stations, helipads, hangars, parking facilities, various
military and governmental uses and passenger-oriented food and personal
services, retail sales, and vehicle rentals. Additionally, the land use class
allows the following uses:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Aeronautical uses associated with an airport including
manufacturing, assembly, storage, training and flight schools and similar uses;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Permitted or special exception uses in the underlying zone;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Industrial parks containing corporate business centers,
wholesaling, manufacturing, agriculture, civic, commercial, industrial, retail,
storage, and utility uses provided they comply with the design and development
standards applicable to the permitted or special exception uses in the
underlying zone.
В В В (Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11127, 11/6/2013; Am. Ord. 11328, 12/8/
2015; Am. Ord. 11409, 11/9/2016; Am. Ord. 11577, 8/8/2018)
4.10.В В В ACCESSORY USES , BUILDINGS , AND STRUCTURES
В В В The accessory use , building , and structure standards are located in
Section
6.6,В Accessory Uses, Buildings and Structures.
4.11.В В В TEMPORARY USE OR BUILDING
В В В Certain land uses or buildings not permitted within specific zones may be
permitted on a temporary basis if authorized in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure, provided such request for a temporary
use complies with the following.
4.11.1.В В В QUALIFICATION FOR PERMIT
В В В For certain land uses or buildings to be permitted on a temporary basis, the
land use or building shall comply with one or more of the following special
circumstances.
В В В A.В В В The circumstances constitute a substantial hardship, such as, but not
limited to, a natural disaster, e.g., fire or flood, or a government action
that has resulted in damage to an existing building on the subject property.
В В В B.В В В A temporary building , such as a mobile or modular unit, utilized for
the management or oversight of construction (e.g., contractorвҖҷs office) or
occupied as a caretakerвҖҷs facility or a home for the eventual resident may be
permitted during the construction of a permanent building . The temporary
building shall be on the same site as the construction.
В В В C.В В В The temporary location of off-street parking facilities during the
structural expansion or remodeling of an existing building may be permitted.
Such temporary facilities do not have to comply with requirements of a
permanent parking facility but shall, at a minimum, provide the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Screening from adjacent residential development . Since this is a
temporary facility, screening cannot be achieved by the use of landscaping
unless mature vegetation that can act as the screen exists on the site ; and,
В В В В В В 2.В В В Dust-proofing.
В В В D.В В В A temporary real estate office may be permitted during construction of
a project , provided:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The temporary use shall be terminated one year from the date the
approval was granted. Additional 12-month extensions may be granted, provided
sales activity for the project continues and 10% or more of the lots or units
remain unsold;
В В В В В В 2.В В В The temporary office building is located on a lot and complies with
all zoning standards applicable to that lot ;
В В В В В В 3.В В В The temporary office building is located in the same subdivision
within which sales occur; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В The temporary use or building complies with any additional
conditions required by the approval authority.
В В В E.В В В A temporary construction equipment yard for public improvement projects
involving street В improvements or the placement of utilities within public
rights-of-way , provided solutions are implemented to mitigate potential
negative impacts to adjacent residential development . Such solutions include,
but are not limited to, screening of equipment, setbacks , hours of operation,
and limited or restricted use of residential streets .
В В В F.В В В The temporary placement of a mobile telecommunications tower facility
on nonresidential property may be permitted upon the demonstration by a
telecommunications provider that the facility is necessary for its operations;
the facility is setback from any residentially zoned property by a distance
equal to the height of the proposed tower and base; and the tower and base do
not exceed 65 feet from the existing grade . The temporary use authorized by
this subsection shall be approved for a period not to exceed 60 days and may be
approved for one extension of time not to exceed 60 days .
4.11.2.В В В BOND REQUIRED
В В В A cash or assurance bond in the amount of $1,000 for a residential use and
$5,000 for a nonresidential use is required, unless stipulated otherwise in the
qualification standards in Section
4.11.1, Qualification for Permit, to guarantee termination of the temporary
use. The cash or assurance  bond shall be provided to the City  before any
permit for the use is issued.
4.11.3.В В В REFUND
В В В The bond, as required in Section
4.11.2, Bond Required, shall be refunded when the temporary use or building  is
removed by the applicant in accordance with Section
4.11.4, Removal.
4.11.4.В В В REMOVAL
В В В The temporary use or building shall be removed on or before the date of
expiration of the approval period. Should the zoning compliance certificate be
revoked, a temporary use shall be removed within 15 days .
В В В A.В В В Removal is to include all improvements installed to accommodate the
temporary use or building .
В В В B.В В В If, after the removal, the property is left graded and vacant, the
property shall be reseeded in accordance with the requirements of the grading
ordinance for graded but undeveloped properties.
4.11.5.В В В FORFEITURE
В В В If the temporary use or building is not removed or the site does not achieve
compliance, the bond required by Section
4.11.2, Bond Required, shall be forfeited.
4.11.6.В В В TIME LIMIT
В В В Approval for a temporary use or building may be granted for one year, with
an additional one year period granted for good cause, unless otherwise
stipulated in the temporary use or building qualification standards in Section
4.11.1, Qualification for Permit.

5.1.В В В PURPOSE OF OVERLAY ZONES
В В В This article provides for overlays that impose standards and procedures that
are in addition to those required under base zoning standards. Where there is a
conflict between the standards of a base district and an overlay district , the
standards of the overlay district shall apply, except for the Urban Overlay
District (UOD) and Downtown Area Infill Incentive District (IID) that provide
flexible development options to landowners rather than mandatory requirements.
5.2.В В В HILLSIDE DEVELOPMENT ZONE (HDZ)
5.2.1.В В В INTRODUCTION
В В В Tucson is surrounded by mountains. These mountainous areas exhibit steep
slopes that may contain unstable rock and soils. Development on potentially
unstable soils or rock can be hazardous to life and property. Development in
these areas should utilize construction methods that ensure slope stabilization
and minimize soil erosion . TucsonвҖҷs mountains and foothills are valuable
scenic resources that should be preserved. Dominant peaks and ridges should be
protected in order to preserve the City вҖҷs unique visual setting, promote its
economic well-being, and encourage tourism. Regulating the intensity of
development according to the natural characteristics of hillside terrain, such
as degree of slope , significant vegetation and landforms, and soil stability
and existing drainage patterns, will allow for development in hillside areas
while minimizing the physical impacts of such development .
5.2.2.В В В PURPOSE
В В В This zone provides for the reasonable use of hillside areas and related
lands while protecting the public health, safety, and general welfare by:
В В В A.В В В Determining whether certain types of soil conditions exist, such as
loose or easily eroded soils or rocky soils that may require blasting, and
utilizing appropriate engineering technology to result in stable slopes during
and subsequent to development ;
В В В B.В В В Reducing water runoff, soil erosion , and rock slides by minimizing
grading and by requiring revegetation and grading restoration;
В В В C.В В В Permitting intensity of development В compatible with the natural
characteristics of hillside terrain, such as degree of slope , significant
landforms, soil suitability, and existing drainage patterns;
В В В D.В В В Preserving the scenic quality of the desert and mountain environment
through the retention of dominant peaks and ridges in their natural states;
В В В E.В В В Reducing the physical impact of hillside development by encouraging
innovative site and architectural design, minimizing grading , and requiring
restoration of graded areas;
В В В F.В В В Providing safe and convenient vehicular access by encouraging
development on the less steeply sloped terrain; and,
В В В G.В В В Promoting cost-efficient public services by encouraging development on
the less steeply sloped terrain, thereby minimizing service extensions and
utility costs and maximizing access for all necessary life safety services.
5.2.3.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The provisions of the Hillside Development Zone (HDZ) apply to newly
proposed subdivisions or individual lot В developments in the areas listed
below. (See
Figure 5.2-A.)
В В В A.В В В Protected Peak and Ridge Areas
В В В Any lot or parcel containing protected peak and ridge areas designated for
protection by the Mayor and Council, as shown on the City Zoning Maps . The
whole parcel is included if any portion of the parcel is within the Protected
Peak and Ridge Area.
В В В B.В В В Slope Areas of 15% or Greater
В В В Any lot or parcel containing slopes of 15% or greater, as shown on the HDZ
Maps. The whole parcel is included if any portion of the parcel is over 15%.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2980.jpg]
Figure 5.2-A: Applicability of Hillside Development Zone
В В В C.В В В Approved Subdivisions
В В В Any recorded subdivision В plat approved in compliance with the Pima County
HDZ or the City of Tucson HDZ may be developed in compliance with the
conditions and stipulations as approved. If the plat is resubdivided, it shall
comply with all provisions of the HDZ currently in effect.
В В В D.В В В New Development
В В В New development that occurs on parcels or proposed subdivisions that include
property designated as HDZ shall be reviewed for compliance with these
standards in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В E.В В В Exceptions
В В В В В В 1.В В В The HDZ standards do not apply to those lots or parcels located
within the City limits south of the Rillito River/Tanque Verde, east of the
Silverbell Road-Congress Street -Grande Avenue-Mission Road alignment, west of
Harrison Road, and north of the Los Reales-Interstate 10 alignment unless
otherwise identified on the HDZ maps.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The HDZ standards shall not apply to vacant residentially-zoned lots
or parcels , legally created as of September 15, 1980, and containing an area
of 16,000 square feet or less, unless, after July 19, 1989, the lot or parcel
is divided into two or more parcels or used for other than one single-family
dwelling.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The HDZ standards shall not apply to lots or parcels annexed from
Pima County that were exempt from the Pima County HDZ at the time of
annexation, unless the lot or parcel is divided into two or more parcels or
used for other than one single-family dwelling.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The density restrictions of Section
5.2.5 shall not apply to any lot or parcel that was zoned R-1, R-2, R-3, or O-
3 and located within the City limits prior to December 10, 1979, unless the lot
or parcel is divided into two or more parcels or used for other than one
single-family dwelling.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The HDZ standards shall not apply in the following situations if the
structure or vehicular circulation area existed prior to September 15, 1980.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Any addition to a structure that does not exceed one 1,000 square
feet or 25% of the enclosed area of the structure, whichever is greater.
Additions shall be cumulative after September 15, 1980.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Any alteration of, or addition to, a vehicular circulation area
that does not exceed 1,000 square feet or 25% of the existing vehicular
circulation area, whichever is greater. Additions shall be cumulative after
September 15, 1980.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Any paving of an existing vehicular circulation area.
В В В F.В В В Designation, Amendment, or Change of Boundaries for a Hillside
Development Zone , a Protected Peak , or Ridge
В В В В В В 1.В В В Designation
В В В В В В The Mayor and Council may designate new property as subject to this
Hillside Development Zone or a new peak or ridge for protection by the
establishment of a 300-foot setback . Consideration shall be in accordance with
Section
3.5.3, Zoning Examiner Legislative Procedure. The change to an existing
designation, amendment to or change of the boundaries of an existing HDZ area
shall be through the same process.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Standard for Designation
В В В В В В Peaks or ridges or new HDZ areas designated for protection shall:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Have been designated by Pima County as a Hillside Development
Zone or as a protected peak or ridge prior to annexation by the City ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Contain significant slopes which should be protected;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Be significant in relationship to the surrounding property;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Be highly visible from lower elevations; or,
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Form a silhouette against the sky when viewed from a Scenic Route
.
5.2.4.В В В PERMITTED USES
В В В Any use permitted by the underlying zone shall be allowed.
5.2.5.В В В DEVELOPMENT STANDARDS
В В В The following development standards shall apply to lots and parcels that are
affected by this zone . Any lot or parcel created shall meet the slope /size
standards of
Table 5.2-1 except as provided below. All development must be setback at least
300 feet from each protected peak or ridge as delineated on the City Zoning
Maps .
В В В A.В В В Single-Family Residential Development
В В В В В В 1.В В В Existing Lot or Parcel Where No Land Division is Proposed
В В В В В В The average natural cross-slope (ACS ) shall be calculated for the entire
lot or parcel . If the ACS is 15% or greater, Columns A, B, and D of
Table 5.2-1 shall apply. If the minimum land area required for the lot or
parcel , based on the ACS , is greater than the area of the lot or parcel ,
natural areas may be designated on the lot or parcel , in accordance with
Section
5.2.6, to reduce the ACS percentage. Such designated natural areas shall be
excluded from the ACS calculation but shall be included in the land area for
the lot or parcel . (See
Figure 5.2-B)
В В В В В В 2.В В В Existing Lot or Parcel Where Land Division is Proposed
В В В В В В The average natural cross-slope shall be calculated for the entire lot or
parcel . If the ACS is 15% or greater, Columns A and C of
Table 5.2-1 apply. Natural areas may be designated on the lot or parcel in
accordance with Section
5.2.6 to reduce the ACS percentage. Such natural areas shall be excluded from
the ACS calculation but shall be included in the land area for the lot or
parcel . (See
Figure 5.2-B)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the land division requires a subdivision В plat , all areas of
15% or greater slope within the lot or parcel , except within natural areas,
shall be delineated. These sloped areas shall then determine the design of the
development according to the following standards.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В For any proposed lot within the parcel where the areas of 15%
or greater slope shall be located outside the buildable area , the minimum lot
size standards of the underlying zone apply. The buildable area may be
redefined to exclude areas of steeper slope in order to comply with this
standard. Grading shall occur only within the buildable area and access to the
buildable area . Grading for access may cross a delineated sloped area.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В For any proposed lot within the parcel where the buildable
area contains areas of 15% or greater slope , the minimum size required for
that lot shall be one acre unless a greater size is required by the underlying
zone . The amount of grading permitted is indicated in Column D of
Table 5.2-1, based on the area of the lot , Column B.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Yard setbacks for the applicable zone shall be applied to the
entire parcel or to each lot within the parcel if the parcel is divided into
lots .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If a subdivision В plat is not submitted, the land area of each
lot created shall comply with Columns A, B, and D of
Table 5.2-1.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2978.jpg]
Figure 5.2-B: Natural Areas in HDZ
В В В B.В В В Multifamily Residential Development
В В В В В В 1.В В В The ACS is calculated for the entire lot or parcel . If the ACS is
15% or greater, Columns A, B, C, and D of
Table 5.2-1 applies.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Natural areas may be designated on the lot or parcel , in accordance
with Section
5.2.6, to reduce the ACS percentage. Such natural areas shall be excluded from
the ACS calculation but shall be included for the density and lot size
calculations. (See
Figure 5.2-B.)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is less
than 15% and contains no areas of 15% or greater slope , 100% of that portion
may be graded, subject to Section
5.2.7.B.1.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is less
than 15% but contains areas of 15% or greater slope , 80% of that portion may
be graded.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is 15%
or greater, Columns B, C, and D of
Table 5.2-1 apply, based on the entire area of the lot or parcel .
