area.
В 
В В В C.В В В Flexible Lot Development (FLD)
В В В The purpose of the FLD in the HDZ is to preserve the sloped areas while
encouraging development on the flatter portions of a lot or parcel . An FLD
shall meet the standards of Section
8.7.3, as well as the following standards: (See
Figure 5.2-C.)
В В В В В В 1.В В В All structures shall be located outside the 300-foot protected peak
or ridge setback area and the protected area shall be preserved as a natural
area as provided in Section
5.2.6, Natural Areas;
В В В В В В 2.В В В The FLD application may be used for either single-family or
multifamily development . In order to apply the FLD, the ACS of the area to be
developed shall be less than 15%. This may require excluding steeper slopes as
natural areas in order to reduce the ACS of the remaining portion. Such natural
areas shall be excluded from the ACS calculation but shall be included for
density and lot size calculations. Density shall be regulated by the underlying
zone , based on the entire area; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В For property within the HDZ, sloped areas in excess of 15% with an
area greater than or equal to 7,000 square feet shall be delineated and set
aside as Natural Undisturbed Open Space and designated as common area.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2982.jpg]
Figure 5.2-C: Flexible Lot Development in HDZ
В В В D.В В В Nonresidential Development
В В В В В В 1.В В В The ACS shall be calculated for the entire lot or parcel . If the
ACS is 15% or greater, Columns A, B, and D of
Table 5.2-1 apply. Column C does not apply.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Natural areas may be designated on the lot or parcel , in accordance
with Section
5.2.6, to reduce the ACS percentage.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is less
than 15% and contains no areas of 15% or greater slope , 100% of that portion
may be graded, subject to Section
5.2.7.B.1.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is less
than 15% but contains areas of 15% or greater slope , 80% of that portion may
be graded.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If the ACS of the remaining portion of the lot or parcel is 15%
or greater, Columns B and D of
Table 5.2-1 shall apply, based on the entire area of the lot or parcel .
В В В E.В В В Mixed Development
В В В When a mix of development is proposed, each type of development shall meet
all standards for that development , as required by this section.
5.2.6.В В В NATURAL AREAS
В В В Natural areas may be designated on any lot or parcel , subject to the
following standards.
В В В A.В В В Development other than hiking trails shall not be permitted within the
legally described boundaries of a natural area. (See
Figure 5.2-D.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2984.jpg]
Figure 5.2-D: Hiking Trails in Designated Natural Areas in HDZ
В В В B.В В В The natural area shall be delineated in a surveyable manner on the
tentative and final plats or on the site plan , whichever is applicable, and
designated by legal description on a document recorded with the Pima County
Recorder.
В В В C.В В В A natural area may be designated as a deed-restricted portion of a
privately-owned lot or as a separate parcel . This parcel may be under the
ownership of a homeownersвҖҷ association or deeded to any organization willing to
accept responsibility for the perpetual preservation of the natural area,
subject to approval and acceptance by the City of Tucson.
В В В D.В В В To protect the natural areas, covenants that run with the land shall be
provided in favor of the City of Tucson and of all owners with record interest
in the natural area.
В В В E.В В В If natural areas are designated, then at least one such natural area,
if in a parcel of four acres or more in size, shall be at least one-half acre
in size or immediately adjacent and contiguous to other land also designated as
natural area that, in the aggregate, is at least one-half acre in size. Section
5.2.6 applies only to natural areas and not to other common areas and open
spaces , such as recreation areas, road medians, etc., that are not designated
as required natural area.
5.2.7.В В В HILLSIDE SITE IMPROVEMENT STANDARD
В В В A.В В В Building Height
В В В Buildings are limited to a building height of 24 feet for residential
development and 30 feet for nonresidential development or the maximum height
permitted by the underlying zone , whichever is more restrictive. If the
building is also located within the boundaries of other overlay zones , the
most restrictive of the standards shall apply. (See
Figure 5.2-E.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2986.jpg]
Figure 5.2-E: Height
В В В B.В В В Site Improvement Standard
В В В All proposed site work, including grading , shall comply with the Section
2-01, Excavating and Grading, of the Technical Standards Manual and the
following standards:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Grading
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Site or Lot В Grading
В В В В В В В В В Projects subject to the provisions of the HDZ ordinance shall have a
grading plan approved by the City Engineer or designee. Review of a grading
plan may occur concurrently with the review of the subdivision , site plan , or
base plan. All development shall meet the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The portions of the site to be graded must be shown on a site
or grading plan and by staking on the site . A City -approved grading plan is
required prior to any grubbing, grading , or clearing. Grubbing, grading , and
clearing shall occur only within the areas identified on the grading plan and
staked on the site or lot ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The portions of the site to be left ungraded shall remain
undisturbed and shall not be used for stockpiling of materials or excess fill ,
construction vehicle access, storage of vehicles during construction, or
similar uses. If natural areas are designated on a site or lot , temporary
fencing, as approved and Inspected by the City Engineer or designee, shall be
installed prior to construction where they abut construction areas in order to
prevent encroachment into the natural areas.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Cut and Fill
В В В В В В В В В The following are required of cut and fill В slopes :
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The maximum amount of cut shall not exceed 30 feet;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The uppermost point of an exposed cut В slope shall be no
higher than 15 feet above design grade ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The uppermost point of a nonexposed cut В slope shall be no
higher than the highest point of any building set into the cut , provided that:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В This cut shall be concealed from view by a method such as
backfilling; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В The backfilled area must be revegetated in accordance with
Section
5.2.7.B.2.b.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The vertical distance of exposed fill and/or exposed
retaining material shall not exceed 15 feet;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Exposed cut and fill В slopes shall either be revegetated or
protected by constructed means, such as riprapping or retaining walls; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Cut or fill В slopes that encroach into a floodplain require
City Engineer or designee approval.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Vegetation Retention and Revegetation
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Vegetation Retention
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Existing viable and transplantable trees with four inch or
greater trunk diameter and cacti shall be preserved in their original
locations, except for building sites and access and utilities serving building
sites .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В When retention of the above trees and cacti in their original
locations is not possible due to building site location, the trees and cacti
shall be salvaged and replanted in areas requiring revegetation , whenever
possible.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Revegetation
В В В В В В В В В All cut or fill В slopes that are no steeper than three horizontal to
one vertical and all utility trenches or septic leaching fields that are not
located in parking or driveway areas shall be revegetated in compliance with
the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В All plants used in revegetation shall be the same as the
native vegetation on the site prior to grading or clearing or any adjacent
В site ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Revegetation shall be accomplished with the following minimum
standards for plants:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В Desert trees, a minimum of 15-gallon size with a minimum
trunk diameter of two inches measured at the soil level, or large specimen
cactus shall be placed at the same vegetation density found on the site prior
to any clearing, grubbing, or grading ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Seeds for trees, desert shrubs, and grasses shall be
planted with a density adequate to control erosion and may use one of the
following methods of planting: Raked into the soil with appropriate mulch
materials; hydroseeding; anchored mulches; or established on jute, rolled
straw, or similar material. Another method may be approved by PDSD.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A temporary watering system shall be installed until the
revegetation materials are established.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Slope Stabilization
В В В В В В All slopes steeper than a ratio of three horizontal to one vertical, with
the exception of retaining walls, shall be stabilized with properly engineered
stone riprapping or sculptured rock as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Stone riprapping shall be hand-placed on the slope ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The stabilizing material used shall blend with the natural
appearance of the site or lot and the surrounding terrain; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Vegetation retention and revegetation should be used in
conjunction with riprapping.
В В В C.В В В Color
В В В All exposed exterior walls and roofs of structures , retaining walls, and
accessory structures , except satellite dishes, shall be earth tone in color
and shall blend with the predominant natural colors found on the lot or parcel
. Satellite dishes may be black. White is not permitted.
5.2.8.В В В MAINTENANCE AND PROTECTION
В В В Measures may be required prior to approval of a subdivision В plat or
issuance of building permits, such as covenants, assurances , or homeownersвҖҷ
associations , as may be necessary to ensure the long-term maintenance of slope
control measures.
5.2.9.В В В ENFORCEMENT
В В В A.В В В The developer and/or property owner shall be responsible for the
following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Submitting a Hillside Development Zone Plan in accordance with
Section
9-01.0.0, Hillside Development Zone, of the Technical Standards Manual;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Submitting average natural cross-slope and sloped area analyses in
accordance with Section
9-01.3.0, Slope Analysis Standard, of the Technical Standards Manual, certified
by an Arizona registered professional, such as an engineer, land surveyor,
architect, or landscape architect, for review and verification by the City
Engineer or designee;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Surveying, staking, and inspection of the lot or parcel by a
certified engineer or land surveyor to determine compliance with the provisions
of this section; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В On-site enforcement by certifying to the City Engineer or designee
that the development complies with the HDZ standards during the period of
development .
В В В B.В В В If violation of any provision of the HDZ occurs, the property owner is
responsible for bringing the violation into compliance with the HDZ standards.
This may require restoration of the site as closely as possible to its original
undisturbed condition, topography, and vegetation, in order to remove the
violation .
5.2.10.В В В VARIANCES
В В В A request for a variance from the 300-foot setback from a protected peak or
ridge as provided for in Section
5.2.6, Natural Areas, shall meet, in addition to the findings required for a
variance, the following conditions. Consideration shall be in accordance with
Sections
3.10.1 and
3.10.3, Board of Adjustment Variance Procedure.
В В В A.В В В The proposed development is for a single-family residence;
В В В B.В В В The only area to be graded within the protected peak and ridge setback
area is for a building pad and access to the building ;
В В В C.В В В The visible external portion of the building or structure, except for
doors, windows, and mechanical equipment, shall be constructed of materials
that will blend with the predominant natural colors found on the lot or parcel
;
В В В D.В В В The highest portion of the structure is no higher than the elevation of
the peak or ridge ;
В В В E.В В В No more than six feet of the external portion of the structure is
exposed, except at points of ingress and egress ;
В В В F.В В В All utilities on the lot or parcel are located underground; and,
В В В G.В В В The project is designed so that glass surfaces do not reflect beyond
the lot or parcel boundaries. This can be accomplished by methods such as using
nonreflective glass or providing overhangs or other window shading, structural
elements, or landscaping that, when mature, will screen windows.
5.3.В В В SCENIC CORRIDOR ZONE (SCZ)
5.3.1.В В В INTRODUCTION
В В В Tucson is located on a magnificent City В site , with mountain ranges in all
directions and attractive foothills leading up to the mountains. This setting
is a scenic resource of great value for the City , for its inhabitants, and for
its economy. Specifically, beautiful surroundings help to attract tourists to
the City , and nationwide experience indicates that the most desirable new
sources of employment have been locating in areas with the most attractive
environments. Preservation of scenic resources is, therefore, important for
both aesthetic and economic reasons. These valuable scenic resources encompass
views up to and into the mountains, including the mountain profiles and their
foothills, and views from the mountains across the valley. They also include
significant natural vegetation and geological formations along designated
Scenic Routes .
5.3.2.В В В PURPOSE
В В В To protect the City вҖҷs unique visual setting and promote its economic well-
being, performance standards are required to assure design sensitivity to the
natural scenic quality. These standards are established to provide for the
preservation of:
В В В A.В В В Views of prominent mountain ridge lines that form the limits of scenic
viewsheds and provide a natural backdrop for sensitively designed development ;
В В В B.В В В Viewsheds that provide the observer with a visual perspective of the
area in terms of foreground, middle ground, and background;
В В В C.В В В The scenic quality of the desert and mountain environment through the
retention of native vegetation and natural topography; and,
В В В D.В В В View windows through an aesthetic screening or siting of developmental
elements that are incompatible with the natural qualities of the surrounding
area.
5.3.3.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В A.В В В Except as provided below, the provisions of the Scenic Corridor Zone
(SCZ) apply to any portion of all real properties or parcels that are located
within 400 feet of the future right-of-way line of any Scenic Route designated
on the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) Plan . (See
Figure 5.3-A.)
В В В B.В В В Exception. Utility facilities constructed or installed in accordance
with a certificate of environmental compatibility issued prior to the adoption
of this section, under the authority of A.R.S. Title 40, Chapter 2, Article
6.2, are exempt from the provisions of this section.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2988.jpg]
Figure 5.3-A: Scenic Corridor Zone
5.3.4.В В В SCENIC ROUTE BUFFER AREA
В В В A.В В В Requirement
В В В В В В 1.В В В A buffer area 30 feet wide, adjacent to the MS&R future right-of-way
line , is required and shall be preserved and maintained in its natural state;
В В В В В В 2.В В В No development or improvements shall occur in a Scenic Route buffer
area except as provided in Section
5.3.4.C, Permitted Improvements; (See
Figure 5.3-B.)
В В В В В В 3.В В В The buffer area shall be restored as closely to its natural state as
possible In areas where public safety or the delivery of public services
precludes preservation of existing vegetation; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В The buffer area is in lieu of the landscape border required along
street frontages under Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening Standards.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2990.jpg]
Figure 5.3-B: Preservation and Reestablishment of Vegetation Adjacent to Scenic
Route
В В В B.В В В Measurement of the Scenic Routes Buffer Area
В В В В В В 1.В В В The Scenic Routes buffer area is measured from the future MS&R
right-of-way , except as follows.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Exception. Where the MS&R roadway design includes installation of a
sidewalk , pedestrian way, or bike path, the buffer may be measured from the
MS&R projected back of the sidewalk , pedestrian way, or bike path if the
buffer does not encroach more than ten feet into the future MS&R right-of-way
with the written permission of the City of Tucson Department of Transportation.
The use of the MS&R right-of-way is permitted only if the area can be
landscaped.
В В В C.В В В Permitted Improvements
В В В The following improvements are permitted in the Scenic Routes buffer area:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Driveways or access lanes , if their area does not exceed 20% of the
Scenic Routes Buffer Area (Buffer Area). In this instance, the width of the
Buffer Area is 30 feet in accordance with Section
5.3.4.A.1. For the purposes here, the Buffer Area is calculated as follows:
Multiply the distance of the propertyвҖҷs frontage along the Scenic Route
measured at the MS&R right-of-way line by 30 feet. If, due to topographical or
engineering constraints, the driveway or access lane exceeds 20% of the Buffer
Area, the width of the Buffer Area shall be increased to accommodate the square
footage resulting from the following calculation: (Distance of the frontage
along the Scenic Route measured at the MS&R right-of-way line - total width of
driveways or access lanes ) x 24 feet = Required Buffer Area in square feet.
Specific sign types may also be placed in the Scenic Routes buffer area in
accordance with Article
7A.11.3В Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ) District.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Trenching for the placement of utility lines, if the area is
revegetated in accordance with Section
5.3.4.D, Revegetation of Site;
В В В В В В 3.В В В An area not larger than 18 square feet and not exceeding 30 inches
in height per lot or parcel for the placement of utility transformers,
pedestals, and service meters and hookups for utilities ; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В Selected vegetation may be removed when the Department of
Transportation determines that removal is necessary for public safety if the
removed plants are replaced with native vegetation .
В В В D.В В В Revegetation of Site
В В В В В В 1.В В В Any portion of the Scenic Route buffer area and the MS&R right-of-
way disturbed by development activity shall be revegetated with native
vegetation .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Within the SCZ, excluding the Scenic Routes buffer area, all
disturbed areas on the site that are visible from the Scenic Route and are not
covered by permanent improvements shall be revegetated with native plants,
plants from the Drought Tolerant Plant List, or a combination of both.
В В В В В В 3.В В В In areas not visible from the Scenic Corridor Zone , vegetation may
consist of native plants indigenous to the site or drought tolerant plant
material. Oasis area landscaping material may also be planted within this area
at the levels permitted by the landscaping ordinance.
В В В E.В В В Cut and Fill
В В В Exposed cut or fill В slopes shall be no greater than a one foot rise or fall
over a three foot length.
(Am. Ord. 11127, 11/6/2013; Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
5.3.5.В В В STRUCTURE HEIGHT
В В В A.В В В The maximum height of a structure is one-third the distance of the
structure from the future right-of-way line , with the following exceptions.
(See
Figure 5.3-C.)
В В В В В В 1.В В В Principal structures , with a maximum height of 12 feet, may be
constructed anywhere within the buildable area of the parcel .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Nonresidential structures shall not exceed 30 feet in height.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Residential structures shall not exceed 24 feet in height.
В В В B.В В В Where there is a conflict between these structure height standards and
those of the existing underlying zone or Hillside Development Zone (HDZ)
(Section
5.2), the most restrictive standard shall apply.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2992.jpg]
Figure 5.3-C: Maximum Structure Height in Scenic Corridor Zone
5.3.6.В В В SITING AND VIEW CORRIDORS
В В В A.В В В Siting of structures shall be such that existing natural topography and
vegetation is minimally disturbed. Grading beyond that necessary for siting of
buildings , parking, private yards, and structural improvements is prohibited.
All existing viable and transplantable vegetation with a caliper of four inches
or greater and all Saguaro cacti shall be preserved or relocated on the site .
В В В B.В В В A site that has at least 200 feet of frontage along a Scenic Route must
provide view corridors as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The view corridor(s) shall have a combined width of at least 20% of
that frontage ; and,
В В В В В В 2.В В В There must be at least one view corridor from the Scenic Route into
and through that portion of the project that lies within the SCZ. (See
Figure 5.3-D.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2994.jpg]
В Figure 5.3-D: View Corridors in Scenic Corridor
В В В C.В В В Drainageways shall be maintained in their natural states when possible,
and the discretionary authority shall be exercised only under unusual
circumstances. In situations where the discretionary authority is exercised by
the City Engineer or designee, modifications shall be in accordance with the
вҖңFloodplain, Stormwater, and Erosion Hazard Management Regulations.вҖқ
5.3.7.В В В PARKING AREAS
В В В Landscaping and screening of parking areas shall comply with Section
7.6.4.B, Vehicular Use Areas.
5.3.8.В В В SCREENING
В В В Screening shall comply with Section
7.6.5, Screening Standards.
5.3.9.В В В UTILITIES
В В В A.В В В All new utilities for development on private property and on public
right-of-way along Scenic Routes shall be underground unless relief is
otherwise granted pursuant to UDC sections
5.3.14, Variances and/or through a Zoning Examiner special exception process
per section
4.9.11.A.12.
В В В B.В В В Where possible, existing poles shall be used to provide the required
transition to underground service to new developments В adjacent to scenic
corridors. However, a new pole set in line with the existing overhead system
when necessary to serve approved new developments is not deemed to be a new
utility. Upgrades and reinforcements of existing overhead facilities is allowed
to the extent that the total number of electrical circuits or communication
cables is not increased. Relocation of overhead utility facilities required by
public improvement districts along scenic corridors shall conform with existing
franchise standards.
В В В C.В В В When an existing development is expanded by 50% or more in floor area
or land area, new and existing utilities to all portions of the development
shall be located underground unless relief is otherwise granted pursuant to UDC
sections
5.3.14, Variances and/or through a Zoning Examiner special exception process
per Section
4.9.11.A.12. Incremental expansion shall be cumulative. Additions to single-
family dwellings are exempt from this standard.
(Am. Ord. 11942, 7/12/2022)
5.3.10.В В В ADDITIONAL DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS
В В В A.В В В Building or structure surfaces, that are visible from the Scenic Route
, shall have colors that are predominant within the surrounding landscape, such
as desert and earth tones. Single-family dwellings, except in subdivisions
recorded after May 28, 1985, are exempt from this standard.
В В В B.В В В Fencing and freestanding walls facing the Scenic Route shall meet the
material restrictions in Section
7.6.5, Screening Standards.
В В В C.В В В Signs are required to comply with the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В 
Article 7A.11.3, Scenic Corridor Zone (SCA) District;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Signs shall use colors that are predominant within the surrounding
landscape, such as desert and earth tones;
В В В В В В 3.В В В No commercial advertising sign, except a sign pertaining to a use
conducted on the premises or a sign advertising the sale or lease of the
property upon which the sign is located, and no billboard shall be erected
within 400 feet of the right-of-way line on any Scenic Route ; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В When the standards of this section and Section 3-32 conflict, the
more restrictive of the two prevails.
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
5.3.11.В В В REVIEW AND APPROVAL PROCEDURE
В В В A.В В В Applications for projects within the SCZ are reviewed and considered
for approval in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В B.В В В At the request of the PDSD Director or applicant, the Design Review
Board (DRB) shall review building elevations, landscaping , parking areas , and
other contributing design features to substantiate compliance with the
standards required in making a decision.
В В В C.В В В The decision to approve or deny the project shall be based on the
purpose, intent, and specific standards of this section, on the objectives
specified in the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) Plan for scenic corridor
development , and on the following standards that provide for the preservation
of:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Views of prominent mountain ridge lines that form the limits of
scenic viewsheds and provide a natural backdrop for sensitively designed
development ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Viewsheds that provide the observer with a visual perspective of the
area in terms of foreground, middle ground, and background;
В В В В В В 3.В В В The scenic quality of the desert and mountain environment through
the retention of native vegetation and natural topography; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В View windows through an aesthetic screening or siting of
developmental elements that are incompatible with the natural qualities of the
surrounding area.
5.3.12.В В В SUBMITTALS
В В В Submittals shall be in compliance with the Administrative Manual.
5.3.13.В В В GRADING
В В В Grading cannot occur until 30 days prior to building construction. For
commencement of grading , the building construction plans shall be, at minimum,
in the review process for permits, or the building construction permits shall
have already been ready to be issued. Grading permits shall cover only those
areas for which base plans have been approved.
5.3.14.В В В VARIANCES
В В В Variances from the SCZ standards are processed in accordance with Section
3.10.1 and
3.10.3, Board of Adjustment Variance Procedure. The Design Review Board review
all requests for variances from SCZ standards and forward a recommendation to
the Board of Adjustment as provided in Section
2.2.6.C.2.
5.3.15.В В В DESIGNATION, AMENDMENT, OR CHANGE OF BOUNDARIES FOR A SCENIC CORRIDOR
ZONE
В В В Designation, amendment, or change of boundaries for a Scenic Corridor Zone
are processed in accordance with Section
5.4.3, MS&R Map Adoption and Amendment.
5.4.В В В MAJOR STREET AND ROUTES В SETBACK В ZONE (MS&R)
5.4.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В The purpose of this overlay zone is to implement policies in the City вҖҷs
General Plan , the Regional Transportation Plan, and the Air Quality Plan, with
specific emphasis on the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) Plan . The MS&R Plan
В projects the future arterial and collector street needs of the community and
is a tool to implement the development of a safe and efficient street system
and the design of all land uses serviced by that system, while assuring the
economic viability of new and expanding land uses and the continued economic
stability of the community. These goals are achieved by providing for the:
В В В A.В В В Capability of street widening in a timely manner in order to increase
the safety of the street system, allow for adequate street capacity, and
provide for the movement of traffic safely and efficiently;
В В В B.В В В Capability of timely improvement of the street system to assist in
reducing air pollution that:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Poses a significant threat to the health of the residents;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Negatively impacts on sectors of the economy dealing with
astronomical observation, research, tourism and convention, health, and
filmmaking; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Adversely affects the quality of life.
В В В C.В В В Economic viability of new land uses located on the MS&R system by
establishing design standards that will provide adequate on-site facilities to
accommodate and serve those land uses after street widening and improvement;
В В В D.В В В Improvement of the MS&R system by establishing standards that assure
availability of land for street widening purposes, including alternate modes of
transportation such as bicycle , pedestrian, and mass transit;
В В В E.В В В Stability of residential neighborhoods that are in close proximity to
streets on the MS&R system, minimizing any unsafe encroachment into adjacent
neighborhoods by requiring all new and expanding land uses located on the MS&R
system to maintain adequate facilities to serve their needs after any street
improvement; and,
В В В F.В В В CommunityвҖҷs continued economic stability by establishing design
standards to provide for the economic viability of all properties located along
the MS&R system after street В improvements .
5.4.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The MS&R standards apply to the following uses on property, any portion of
which abuts or is adjacent to a street designated on the City вҖҷs or County вҖҷs
MS&R Plan :
В В В A.В В В New structures ;
В В В B.В В В Changes of use, including new structures occurring on vacant land;
В В В C.В В В Uses of land or structures legally existing as of June 27, 1988, that
are expanded in floor area , lot coverage , parking, seating capacity, or any
other expansion of use, as listed below. Exception. An expansion of off-street
parking for a nonconforming existing use that increases the number of spaces to
the minimum required by the UDC for that use is exempt from counting toward the
25% expansion: (See
Figure 5.4-A.)
В В В В В В 1.В В В If the expansion is less than 25%, the MS&R applies to the proposed
expansion. The remainder of the use or structure shall be governed by
provisions in force at the time of initial approval for the use or structure;
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the expansion is 25% or more, the MS&R applies to the proposed
expansion and to the parking and landscaping standards that apply to the
overall development ; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Expansions that occur after the effective date of the MS&R shall be
cumulated in determining the 25% expansion; and,
В В В D.В В В When one or more of the standards of the MS&R, the Gateway Route , or
the Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ) (Section
5.5) apply to the same street , the most restrictive standards apply.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-2996.jpg]
Figure 5.4-A: Applicability of MS&R Setback Provisions
5.4.3.В В В MS&R ADOPTION AND AMENDMENT
В В В The major streets and routes applicable under these provisions are
established by the MS&R Map, which is adopted as a component of the MS&R Plan .
The adoption and amendment of the MS&R Plan , including designations of Scenic
Routes and Gateway Routes , are processed in accordance with Section
3.6, Land Use Plan Adoption and Amendment Procedure. Designations of extensions
of Scenic Routes and Gateway Routes may also be adopted with the adoption of
original City zoning in accordance with Section
3.5.3, Zoning Examiner Legislative Process.
5.4.4.В В В ESTABLISHMENT OF MS&R RIGHT-OF-WAY LINES AND GATEWAY ROUTES
В В В MS&R right-of-way line locations are established utilizing the future right-
of-way width for those streets identified in the MS&R Plan . The widths are
measured in accordance with the method established by the MS&R Plan . Gateway
Routes are those major streets or routes designated as Gateway Routes by the
MS&R Plan .
5.4.5.В В В PERMITTED USE OF MS&R RIGHT-OF-WAY AREA
В В В The area between the MS&R right-of-way lines is also referred to as the MS&R
right-of-way area , whether publicly or privately owned. In addition to roadway
В improvements in conjunction with and accessory to development on abutting
properties, the following are permitted in the MS&R right-of-way area :
В В В A.В В В No portion of the MS&R right-of-way area that is publicly owned shall
be used toward complying with UDC standards, unless specifically stipulated;
В В В B.В В В Landscaping in compliance with Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening Standards;
В В В C.В В В Structural improvements , provided:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The MS&R is not a Scenic Route ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В The structure is not a building ;
В В В В В В 3.В В В That, if the improvement is for off-street parking , such parking is
in addition to the amount required for the existing use(s), and the area is
fully improved as required for any parking area . The screening and landscaping
required by Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening Standards, for vehicular use areas , street
landscape borders , and Gateway Routes  may be located temporarily in the MS&R
right-of-way area  until such time as this area is used for right-of-way
purposes. Upon elimination of the parking area , all parking-related
improvements , such as required screening and landscaping , as specified on the
site plan  required by Section
5.4.5.F, shall be moved to the location specified on the site plan  at no cost
to the City ; (See
Figure 5.4-B.)
В В В В В В 4.В В В The structural improvement, other than landscaping , access, or
public improvements , is located at least eight feet from the edge of the
existing roadway (curb);
В В В В В В 5.В В В The improvement does not obstruct the existing street вҖҷs sight
visibility triangle;
В В В В В В 6.В В В That, if the improvement is a sign, it is permitted as an on-site
sign by the Sign Code. Such sign shall be removed at the time of street
widening at no expense to the City ; and,
В В В В В В 7.В В В Approval is granted by the Mayor and Council for any structural
improvement located within publicly owned right-of-way.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3000.jpg]
В 
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3002.jpg]
Figure 5.4-B: Permitted Uses of the Major Street and Route Right-of-Way Area
В В В D.В В В The following applies when calculating the various standards:
В В В В В В 1.В В В On development projects that are not part of a subdivision В plat or
rezoning application, the site area within the MS&R right-of-way may be used as
follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For calculation of lot coverage or open space as non-lot coverage
or open space area, regardless of what improvements are located within that
area;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For calculation of an adjustment for nonresidential projects to
off-street parking in accordance with Section
5.4.6.B or floor area standards provided the adjustment does not reduce the
amount of required parking by 20% or more of the amount that would be required
without the adjustment.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В For calculation as part of the site , floor area , and density
calculations. If the entire MS&R right-of-way area , excluding access and
public improvements , to the edge of the paved roadway is landscaped, that part
of the site within the MS&R right-of-way area may be included at one and one-
eighth its size (multiply the area size by 1.125), provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The landscaping is visible from the public right-of-way ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The landscaping is over and above that required by the UDC ;
and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The street is not scheduled for construction within a period
of three years from the date of issuance of a building permit.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В If, in addition to landscaping the entire MS&R area, that area is
dedicated to the City at nominal cost , the area may be included at one and
one-fourth its size (multiply the area dedicated by 1.25) in lieu of the 1.125.
In this situation, Section
5.4.5.D.1.c(3) would not apply.
В В В В В В 2.В В В On development projects that are part of a subdivision В plat
application but not part of a rezoning application, Section
5.4.5.D.1.c may be utilized.
В В В E.В В В Landscaping that is provided to achieve additional density or floor
area is considered a requirement of the UDC and shall be maintained in
compliance.
В В В F.В В В An approved site plan is required indicating how the project will
comply with UDC standards when the MS&R right-of-way can no longer be used as
part of the site . Such plan shall be an exhibit to an executed covenant for
recordation stating the responsibility of the property owner, successor, or
assignee as to the removal of improvements and compliance with the UDC at no
cost to the City .
5.4.6.В В В MS&R STREET В PERIMETER YARD AND PARKING ADJUSTMENT
В В В A.В В В Setback
В В В The required minimum street perimeter yard from a street that is designated
a major street or route on the MS&R Map shall be the same as the perimeter yard
for the base zoning district in accordance with Section
6.3.4, Dimensional Standard and Exceptions Tables, and other related sections
if the street  is not a designated Scenic Route  on the MS&R Map. Along a
street  that is designated a Scenic Route , the required perimeter yard  is
regulated in accordance with Section
5.3.4.A, Scenic Route Buffer Area.
В В В B.В В В Adjustment
В В В An adjustment to the amount of required off-street parking for
nonresidential projects is permitted to compensate for the setting aside of
property for eventual use as part of an MS&R improvement in accordance with
Section
5.4.5.F. The amount of adjustment depends on the individual situation. To
determine the amount, an adjustment factor is applied and calculated as
provided below.
В В В C.В В В Applying Adjustment Factor
В В В The adjustment factor (percentage) is utilized to accomplish one of the
following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В To lower the ratio of required off-street parking spaces to the
square footage of use;
В В В В В В 2.В В В To increase the square footage of use for the amount of off-street
parking provided; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В To increase the seating capacity of a use for the amount of off-
street parking provided.
В 
EXAMPLE 1: Application of Adjustment Factor
В 
вҖўВ В В Required Parking Ratio: One space for every 200 square feet of floor area .
В 
вҖўВ В В Adjustment Factor: 11.2 percent. (Refer to Section
5.4.6.C)
В 
вҖўВ В В Proposed Building Square Footage: 30,440 square feet.
В 
Application 1. Adjustment to the Parking Ratio. If the adjustment factor is applied
to the parking ratio, then the ratio (200) is multiplied by the adjustment factor
(0.112). The result (22.4) is added to the normally required ratio (200) to provide
the new adjusted ratio (222.4) that would be applicable.
В 
Application 2. Adjustment to Building Size. If the adjustment factor (11.2%) is
applied to the floor area  (30,440), first the parking would be calculated on the
30,440 square feet that, at one space per 200 square feet, would equal 152 parking
spaces that shall be provided. Then, the 30,440 square feet is multiplied by the
adjustment factor (0.112). The result (3,409) is then added to 30,440 square feet to
provide a total building  square footage of 33,849 that could be placed on the
property.
В 
Application 3. Adjustment to Seating Capacity. To apply the adjustment factor in a
situation where the required off-street parking  is calculated by the number of
seats, such as one space for every five seats, the adjustment factor (0.112) is
multiplied by the number of seats (5). The result (0.56) is added to the number of
seats (5) to provide the new adjusted ratio of one space for every 5.56 seats that
would be applicable.
В 
В В В D.В В В Calculation of Adjustment Factor
В В В В В В 1.В В В The adjustment on each project is based on the amount (by
percentage) of site area that is within the MS&R right-of-way area (A). This
adjustment factor is calculated by dividing that area (A) by the gross site
area that includes area A. The adjustment factor is calculated to the nearest
thousandth as shown below. (See
Figure 5.4-C.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3004.jpg]
Figure 5.4-C: Adjustment of Off-Street Parking Requirements
В В В Formula: A Г· (A + B) = Adjustment factor
В В В Where:
В В В A = Site area within MS&R right-of-way
В В В B = Site area outside the MS&R right-of-way
В В В A + B = Site area or gross site area
В 
EXAMPLE 2: Utilization of Formula
В 
A = 15,000 square feet
B = 75,000 square feet
A Г· (A + B) = Adjustment factor (percentage)
15,000 Г· (15,000 + 75,000) = Adjustment factor
15,000 Г·90,000 = Adjustment factor
.167 = 16.7% = Adjustment factor
В 
EXAMPLE 3. Adjustment Utilizing Section
5.4.5.H.1.d.
В 
If area A were dedicated  to the City , then area A would be allowed to count
at one and one-fourth its size in the adjustment calculation as provided in
Section
5.4.5.D.1.d. For example, utilizing the site  conditions in Example 1, where A
is equal to 15,000 square feet utilizing the provision of Section
5.4.5.D.1.d, area A would now be equal to 18,750 square feet (15,000 multiplied
by 1.25). The calculation would be as follows with B = 75,000 square feet:
В 
A Г· (A + B) = Adjustment factor
18,750 Г· (18,750 + 75,000) = Adjustment factor
18,750 Г· 93,750 = Adjustment factor
0.20 = 20% = Adjustment factor
В 
(Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014)
5.4.7.В В В MS&R RIGHT-OF WAY USE MODIFICATION REQUEST
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В This section provides an administrative process allowing improvements in
privately and publicly owned MS&R right-of-way areas . This procedure is
intended to allow flexibility in using right-of-way areas where no negative
impact is created for surrounding property or the City 's major streets and
routes planning and where there is a finding that the subject right-of-way area
will not be used for road expansion or similar uses in the future.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В В В В The following improvements may be considered under this Major Streets &
Routes Plan Right-of-Way Modification Request (MSMR): buildings , signs,
required parking and landscaping and any other improvement deemed similar by
the PDSD Director. The MSMR does not apply to scenic routes . Permission to use
MSMR shall not preclude any other necessary regulatory relief process, such as
a Design Development Option or Variance.
В В В C.В В В Application
В В В В В В Applications must include property ownership information, a site plan ,
and other information as required by the PDSD and Department of Transportation
Directors as necessary to evaluate the request. Except as provided herein,
review of an MSMR is processed in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В D.В В В Findings for Approval
В В В В В В The PDSD Director may approve a MSMR as provided by this section if all
of the following applicable criteria are met and documented in findings.
В В В В В В 1.В В В There is a written finding by the Department of Transportation
Director that the subject right of way area will not be used for road, drainage
or other needed expansions in the future and any other special condition
required for approval has been met, and
В В В В В В 2.В В В The modification is not a request previously denied as a variance,
and
В В В В В В 3.В В В The modification is not to a condition of approval for a rezoning,
Special Exception Land Use, variance, or Individual Parking Plan, and
В В В В В В 4.В В В The modification does not adversely impact adjacent properties or
development, and
В В В В В В 5.В В В The modification does not impede sight visibility at points of
ingress into, egress from, or within the vehicular use area for either
vehicular or pedestrian traffic or otherwise create or increase a safety
hazard, and
В В В В В В 6.В В В The modification provides an improvement that is better integrated
into the design character of the immediate neighborhood, and
В В В В В В 7.В В В The MSMR does not replace the need for an approval as a Parking
Design Modification Request per Sec.
7.4.10.
(Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014; Am. Ord. 11386, 7/6/2016)
5.5.В В В GATEWAY CORRIDOR ZONE (GCZ)
5.5.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В The purpose of this overlay zone is to implement policies in the City вҖҷs
General Plan , the Regional Transportation Plan, and the Air Quality Plan, with
specific emphasis on the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) Plan . The MS&R Plan
В projects the future arterial and collector street needs of the community and
is a tool to implement the development of a safe and efficient street system
and the design of all land uses serviced by that system, while assuring the
economic viability of new and expanding land uses and the continued economic
stability of the community. These goals are achieved by providing for the
visual improvement of major streets and routes designated as Gateway Routes by
implementing standards for the design and landscaping of the roadway and
adjacent В development , thereby providing:
В В В A.В В В A favorable visual impression of Tucson to tourists and visitors at
entry points to the City and on routes leading to major recreation attractions;
В В В B.В В В Enhancement of the built environment along routes of important
commercial development ;
В В В C.В В В Incentives for private investment and economic development by providing
an attractive streetscape;
В В В D.В В В Aesthetic buffering through the implementation of screening or siting
of developmental elements that are incompatible with the urban character of
adjacent uses;
В В В E.В В В The improvement and use of the pedestrian environment along major
transit routes; and,
В В В F.В В В Some measure of air quality control by requiring landscaping with live
vegetation to assist in purifying the air of carbon dioxide through oxygenation
and dilution.
5.5.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The GCZ standards apply to the following uses on all property, any portion
of which abuts or is adjacent to a street designated on the City вҖҷs or county
вҖҷs MS&R Plan :
В В В A.В В В New structures ;
В В В B.В В В Changes of use, including new structures occurring on vacant land; or,
В В В C.В В В Uses of land or structures legally existing as of June 27, 1988, that
are expanded in floor area , lot coverage , parking, seating capacity, or any
other expansion of use, as listed below. Exception. Expansion of off-street
parking for a nonconforming existing use that increases the number of spaces to
the minimum required by the UDC for that use is exempt from counting toward the
25% expansion.
В В В В В В 1.В В В If the expansion is less than 25%, the GCZ applies to the proposed
expansion. The remainder of the use or structure shall be governed by
provisions in force at the time of initial approval for the use or structure;
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the expansion is 25% or more, the GCZ applies to the proposed
expansion and to the parking and landscaping standards that apply to the
overall development ; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Expansions that occur after June 27, 1988, shall be cumulated in
determining the 25% expansion.
В В В D.В В В When one or more of the standards of the MS&R setback В zone , the
Gateway Route , or the Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ) apply to the same street ,
the most restrictive standards apply.
В В В E.В В В At street  intersections where a Scenic Route  and Gateway Route
intersect, development  review and standards of the Gateway Route  apply for
700 feet along the Scenic Route  from each side of the intersection. The 700
feet shall be measured from the MS&R right-of-way line  of the Gateway Route .
This does not apply to
Article 7A, Sign Standards.
В В В F.В В В The following are exempt from the application of the Gateway Route
standard:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Utility facilities constructed or installed in accordance with a
certificate of environmental compatibility issued prior to June 27, 1988, under
the authority of A.R.S. Title 40, Chapter 2, Article 6.2;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Single-family В lot legally existing on June 27, 1988; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Development within the Downtown Redevelopment District .
В В В G.В В В Where widening of a Gateway Route is planned for construction within
three years after the date of a building permit application, landscaping is not
required to be implemented until the road project has been completed, provided
the developer posts financial assurances to ensure compliance.
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
5.5.3.В В В REVIEW AND APPROVAL PROCEDURE
В В В Applications for projects within the GCZ are reviewed and considered for
approval in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В The City shall not issue any type of approval or construction permit, nor
shall any improvement, construction, or alteration of a building or structure
be allowed along a Gateway Route , until approval is granted.
5.5.4.В В В DEVELOPMENT STANDARDS
В В В In addition to other applicable standards in other sections of the UDC ,
such as landscaping and screening, the following development standards are
required of projects in the GCZ:
В В В A.В В В Signs
В В В Signs as permitted by
Article 7A, Sign Standards, are allowed within the required landscaped area
with the exception of billboards. Billboards are not permitted within 400 feet
of the MS&R right-of-way line .
В В В B.В В В Utilities
В В В В В В 1.В В В New Utilities
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В New utilities for development on private and on public right-of-
way along Gateway Routes shall be underground unless relief is otherwise
granted pursuant to UDC sections
5.3.14, Variances or through a Zoning Examiner special exception process per
section
4.9.11.A.12.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Existing poles shall be used to provide the required transition
to underground service to new development В adjacent to Gateway Routes . When
necessary to serve new development , a new pole set in line with, but not
extending, an existing overhead system used to serve new development is not
considered a new utility.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Upgrades and reinforcements of existing overhead facilities are
allowed to the extent that the total number of electrical circuits or
communication cables is not increased.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Existing Utilities
В В В В В В Where an existing development is expanded in floor area or land area to
any degree, new and existing utilities to all portions of the development shall
be located underground unless relief is otherwise granted pursuant to UDC
sections
5.3.14, Variances or through a Zoning Examiner special exception process per
section
4.9.11.A.12. Additions to single-family dwellings are exempt from this
provision. Additions to single-family dwellings are exempt from this provision.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Relocation of Utilities
В В В В В В Relocation of overhead utility facilities required by public improvement
districts along Gateway Routes shall conform with existing franchise
requirements.
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020; Am. Ord. 11942, 7/12/2022)
5.5.5.В В В DESIGNATION, AMENDMENT, OR CHANGE OF BOUNDARIES
В В В Designation, amendment, or change of boundaries for a Gateway Route shall be
established through the amendment to the designation on the MS&R Map in
accordance with Section
5.4.3, MS&R Adoption and Amendment.
5.5.6.В В В VARIANCES
В В В Variances to the Gateway Corridor Zone standards are processed in accordance
with Sections
3.10.1 and
3.10.3, Board of Adjustment Variance Procedure. The Design Review Board shall
review variance requests as provided in Section
2.2.6.C.6 and forward its recommendations to the Board of Adjustment.
5.6.В В В AIRPORT ENVIRONS ZONE (AEZ)
5.6.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В The purpose of this overlay zone is to protect the health, safety, and
welfare of persons and property in the vicinity of Tucson International Airport
(TIA) and Davis-Monthan Air Force Base (DMAFB) and protect the long term
viability of DMAFB. This is accomplished by: (1) Reducing noise and safety
hazards associated with aircraft operations; (2) preserving the operational
stability of these airports ; and (3) assisting in the implementation of
policies and recommendations in the City вҖҷs General Plan and Airport Environs
Plan, the Air Installation Compatible Use Zone Report (AICUZ ), and the Airport
Noise Control, Land Use Compatibility (ANCLUC) Study and the Davis-Monthan
Joint Land Study (DMJLUS). The overlay district further:
В В В A.В В В Promotes the compatibility of uses with aircraft operations through the
establishment of standards for the regulation of building height and density ;
В В В B.В В В Addresses potentially life-threatening situations in areas exposed to
aircraft accident potential through restrictions on the congregation of large
numbers of people or high concentrations of people and by restrictions on
concentrations of people who are unable to respond to emergency situations,
such as children, the elderly, the handicapped, and persons undergoing medical
treatment;
В В В C.В В В Increases the protection of persons exposed to high levels of aircraft
noise by requiring acoustical treatment in buildings located within these areas
and regulating those uses that are sensitive to such noise;
В В В D.В В В Prohibits uses that create potential hazards to the safe approach and
departure of aircraft; and,
В В В E.В В В Recognizes the role of Davis-Monthan Air Force Base in the Tucson
community and protects the City вҖҷs interest in ensuring the continued viability
and operation of Davis-Monthan AFB by limiting incompatible land uses in the
Approach-Departure Corridor (ADC).
5.6.2.В В В ESTABLISHMENT, DESIGNATION, AND AMENDMENT OF AEZ MAPS
В В В The Airport Environs Zone (AEZ) includes districts and zones that do not
necessarily have the same boundaries. The boundaries of these districts and
zones are identified for Tucson International Airport (TIA) and for Davis-
Monthan Air Force Base (DMAFB) by the Airport Environs Zone (AEZ) Overlay Map
series kept on file in the offices of the City Clerk and the Planning and
Development Services Department (PDSD). The AEZ Overlay Map series is hereby
established as the official AEZ Overlay Map series, The AEZ Overlay Map became
effective on May 16, 1990, as amended on January 28, 1991, April 27, 1992,
October 28, 2002 and January 1, 2005.
В В В The AEZ is made up of the following 11 zones and districts (See Illustrative
Maps 5.6-B and 5.6-C.):
В В В A.В В В The following zones and districts are established in the TIA environs:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Compatible Use Zone-One (CUZ-1) ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Compatible Use Zone-Two (CUZ-2) ;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Compatible Use Zone-Three (CUZ-3) ;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Noise Control District 65 (NCD 65) - High Noise District with
exposures of 65-70 Ldn designated at TIA; and,
В В В В В В 5.В В В Noise Control District 70 (NCD 70 - High Noise District with
exposures of 70+ Ldn designated at TIA.
В В В B.В В В The following zones and districts are established in the DMAFB
environs:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Approach Departure Corridor One (ADC-1) - Northwest end of DMAFB
runway;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Approach Departure Corridor Two (ADC-2) - Southeast end of DMAFB
runway up to 30,000 feet from end of runway;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Approach Departure Corridor Three (ADC-3) - Southeast end of DMAFB
runway 30,000 to 50,200 feet from end of runway;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Noise Control District -A (NCD A) - High Noise District with
exposures of 65-70 Ldn designated at DMAFB; and,
В В В В В В 5.В В В Noise Control District - B (NCD B) - High Noise District with
exposures of 70+ Ldn designated at DMAFB.
В В В C.В В В The Airport Hazard District (AHD) apply to both the TIA environs and
the DMAFB environs. The AHD is a specifically designated area of land where
uses that constitute hazards to aircraft operations are prohibited and heights
are limited.
В В В D.В В В Designation, amendment, or change of boundaries for an Airport Environs
Zone shall be established through the amendment to the designation on the AEZ
Maps in accordance with Section
3.5, Rezoning (Change of Zoning) Procedure.
5.6.3.В В В APPLICABILITY IN THE TIA ENVIRONS
В В В A.В В В Sections
5.6.3,
5.6.4,
5.6.5,
5.6.6, and
5.6.11 apply to the TIA environs.
В В В B.В В В Where more than one district or zone applies to a property, the
standards of all applicable districts or zones shall apply. Where standards
conflict, the most restrictive standards shall apply.
В В В C.В В В The Airport Environs Zone (AEZ) applies to the following on all
property located within the TIA boundaries. For property partially within the
TIA, the provisions apply to only those portions within the boundaries of the
AEZ. For areas outside the City limits, the AEZ standards will apply upon
annexation.
В В В В В В 1.В В В New development ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В A change in, expansion of, or addition to the use of an existing
structure as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The residential density , employee density , and emergency
evacuation plan and training standards of Section
5.6.5, Compatibility Use Zones for the TIA Environs, apply to the entire
existing structure if the change, expansion, or addition results in an increase
in any of the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Employee density ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Residential density ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Number of employees; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Number of persons of the general public for whom the
structure was intended or designed to accommodate.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The noise attenuation standards of Section
5.6.6, Noise Control Districts, apply to the entire existing structure if the
use of the existing structure is changed from any other land use to one or more
of the following uses:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Residential;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Place of public accommodation ; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Administrative or Professional Office.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Expansion of an existing development as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the gross floor area of a structure or the gross floor area on
a project site is expanded by less than 50%, the AEZ applies only to the areas
of expansion;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the gross floor area of a structure is expanded by 50% or
more, the standards of Section
5.6.6 apply to the entire structure. Exception: the sound attenuation standard
in this section does not apply to an expansion of the following types of
structures existing prior to May 16, 1990:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В A single-family or duplex dwelling;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В A mobile home; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В A manufactured housing unit.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If the gross floor area on a project site is expanded by 50% or
more, the employee density , lot coverage , and emergency evacuation plan and
training standards of Section
5.6.5 apply to the entire project site .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Cumulation of Expansions. Expansions are cumulated over time from
May 16, 1990. Once a structure or project site is brought into conformance with
the provisions of this section, subsequent expansions are accumulated as of the
date the existing structure or project site is brought into conformance .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Nothing contained in this section affects existing property or the
right to its continued use for the purpose legally used at the time these
requirements become effective, nor do these requirements affect any reasonable
repairs to, or alterations of, buildings or property used for such existing
purposes.
5.6.4.В В В TIA ENVIRONS
В В В The following provisions apply to the TIA Environs.
В В В A.В В В Permitted Uses
В В В The land uses permitted are those permitted by the underlying zone , except
as restricted by this section.
В В В B.В В В Posting of Occupancy Limitations
В В В Any restriction of occupancy required under this ordinance as a condition of
building permit issuance or certificate of occupancy shall be posted on the
premises. The owner/manager(s) of the premises cannot exceed these limitations.
5.6.5.В В В COMPATIBLE USE ZONES FOR THE TIA ENVIRONS
В В В Land use standards within the Compatible Use Zones are as follows:
В В В A.В В В CUZ-1
В В В В В В 1.В В В Single-family and multifamily dwellings and mobile homes are
permitted only if the property is zoned IR, RH, SR, RX-1 (UR), RX-2, R-1, R-2,
MH-1 (MH), MH-2 (MHP), or R-3 and such zone was in place prior to May 16, 1990.
Other uses allowed by the underlying zoning are permitted, except as modified
by Section
5.6.5.D, Prohibited Uses.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Flexible Lot Developments, Section
8.7.3, are not permitted; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Residential development shall comply with the underlying zoning,
with no more than 25 dwelling units per building .
В В В В В В 2.В В В No more than one employee for every 250 square feet of gross floor
area of all buildings on a project site at any time shall be accommodated by
intention, design, or in fact.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Structures or uses with 50 or more employees shall develop an
emergency evacuation plan and training program shall be developed and
implemented. Fire Department approval of this plan and program is required.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The maximum permitted height limit is the most restrictive of the
underlying zone , 75 feet, or as restricted by Section
5.6.11, Airport Hazard Districts for TIA and DMAFB Environs.
В В В B.В В В CUZ-2
В В В В В В 1.В В В Single-family and multifamily dwellings and mobile homes are
permitted, only if the property is zoned IR, RH, SR, RX-1 (UR), RX-2, R-1, R-2,
MH-1 (MH), MH-2 (MHP), or R-3 and such zone was in place prior to May 16, 1990.
Other uses allowed by the underlying zoning are permitted, except as prohibited
by Section
5.6.5.D, Prohibited Uses.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Flexible Lot Development (Section
8.7.3) are not permitted; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Residential development shall comply with underlying zoning, with
no more than 25 dwelling units per building .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Structures or uses with 50 or more employees shall develop an
emergency evacuation plan and training program shall be developed and
implemented. Fire Department approval of this plan and program is required.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The maximum permitted height limit is the most restrictive of the
underlying zone , 75 feet, or as restricted by Section
5.6.11, Airport Hazard Districts for TIA and DMAFB Environs.
В В В C.В В В CUZ-3
В В В В В В 1.В В В In CUZ-3 , single-family and multifamily dwellings and mobile homes
are permitted, only if the property is zoned IR, RH, SR, RX-1 (UR), RX-2, R-1,
R-2, MH-1 (MH), MH-2 (MHP), or R-3 and such zone was in place prior to May 16,
1990. Other uses allowed by the underlying zoning are permitted, except as
prohibited by Section
5.6.5.D, Prohibited Uses.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Flexible Lot Development, Section
8.7.3, are not permitted; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Minimum lot area per dwelling unit is 144,000 square feet.
В В В В В В 2.В В В In CUZ-3 , no development shall exceed 75% lot coverage and 0.375
Floor Area Ratio (FAR).
В В В В В В 3.В В В In CUZ-3 , no structure or use or contiguous В structure or use shall
accommodate, by intention or design, in whole or in part, at any one time, more
than 50 employees.
В В В D.В В В Prohibited Uses
В В В В В В 1.В В В Public assembly of more than 50 persons is prohibited within CUZ-1 ,
CUZ-2 , and CUZ-3 .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exception: Accessory Food Service for employees only is permitted
provided such uses are provided on-site entirely within a structure devoted to
a permitted principal use .
В В В В В В 2.В В В In addition to public assembly , the following uses are prohibited
within CUZ-1 and CUZ-2 :
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Adult care homes, adult care facilities, specialized treatment
homes, and group homes for the seriously mentally ill ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Civic Assembly of 50 or more persons ;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Day Care;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Educational Use: Elementary and Secondary Schools; and,
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Medical Service.
В В В В В В 3.В В В In addition to public assembly , the following uses are prohibited
within CUZ-3 :
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service (on premises);
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Civic Assembly;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Cultural Use;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Day Care;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Educational Use;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Entertainment (indoor and outdoor);
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Financial Service (except automated teller);
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Food Service (on premises, except as accessory use );
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В General Merchandise Sales (retail stores over 2,500 square feet
gross floor area );
В В В В В В В В В j.В В В Medical Service - Major;
В В В В В В В В В k.В В В Medical Service - Outpatient;
В В В В В В В В В l.В В В Membership Organization;
В В В В В В В В В m.В В В Recreation (indoor and outdoor);
В В В В В В В В В n.В В В Religious Use;
В В В В В В В В В o.В В В Swap Meet or Auction; and,
В В В В В В В В В p.В В В TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation.
5.6.6.В В В NOISE CONTROL DISTRICTS
В В В A.В В В NCD-65
В В В В В В 1.В В В Within Noise Control District-65, the following uses shall be
provided with sound attenuation, to reduce the interior noise level to an Ldn
of 45 or less, as specified in Section
9-03.0.0, Sound Attenuation within the Airport Environs Zone, of the Technical
Standards Manual:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Site -built residential uses;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Places of public accommodation ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Administrative and Professional Offices.
В В В В В В 2.В В В A manufactured housing unit is not considered equivalent to a
single-family dwelling within the boundaries of NCD-65 , unless located on a
property zoned MH-1 or MH-2 or unless it can be demonstrated that the unit
provides adequate sound attenuation to reduce the interior noise level to Ldn
45.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Prohibited Uses: Within NCD-65 , Day Care uses are prohibited.
В В В B.В В В NCD-70
В В В В В В 1.В В В Within Noise Control District-70, the following uses shall be
provided with sound attenuation to reduce the interior noise level to an Ldn of
45 or less, as specified in Section
9-03.0.0, Sound Attenuation within the Airport Environs Zone, the Technical
Standards Manual:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Site -built residential uses;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Places of public accommodation ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Administrative and Professional Offices.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Single-family and multifamily dwellings are permitted, provided the
property is residentially zoned as of May 16, 1990, and provided the interior
noise level is reduced to an Ldn of 45 or less as specified in Section
5.6.6.B.1.
В В В В В В 3.В В В A manufactured housing unit is not considered equivalent to a
single-family dwelling within the boundaries of NCD-70 , unless located on a
property zoned MH-1 or MH-2 or unless it can be demonstrated that the unit
provides adequate sound attenuation to reduce the interior noise level to Ldn
45.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Special Exception Land Uses .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The following uses are generally considered to be inappropriate
within the high- noise area, NCD-70 . They may be approved as Special Exception
Land Uses upon application, review, and approval in accordance with Section
3.4.3, Zoning Examiner Special Exception Procedure. In addition to the standard
notice required for Special Exception Land Use applications, the Tucson Airport
Authority and Davis-Monthan Air Force Base shall be notified of all such
applications within the boundaries of the Airport  Environs Zone (AEZ) (Section
5.6).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Civic Assembly;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Cultural Use;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Educational Use - Postsecondary Institution;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Entertainment;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Medical Service - Major; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Swap Meet or Auction.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В In addition to the required findings and conditions specified in
Sections
3.4.5 and
3.4.6, respectively, these uses shall be consistent with the intent of the
Airport Environs Zone (AEZ) and the Airport Environs Plan or the Air
Installation Compatible Use Zone Report (AICUZ ) Report and shall be capable of
sound attenuation to mitigate the effects of high noise. In addition, all
activity associated with the use shall be shown to take place within an
enclosed В building . An acoustical engineer shall demonstrate that the proposed
use is insulated to an interior noise level of an Ldn of 45 or less.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Prohibited Uses. Within NCD-70 , the following uses are prohibited:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Day Care; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Educational Use: Elementary and Secondary Schools.
5.6.7.В В В APPLICABILITY IN THE DMAFB ENVIRONS
В В В Sections
5.6.7,
5.6.8,
5.6.9,
5.6.10, and
5.6.11 apply to the DMAFB Environs.
В В В Where more than one district or zone is applicable to a property, the
standards of all applicable districts or zones shall apply. Where standards
conflict, the most restrictive shall apply.
В В В The Airport Environs Zone (AEZ) applies to the following on all property
located within the DMAFB Environs boundaries. For property partially within the
AEZ, the provisions shall apply to only those portions within the boundaries of
the AEZ. For areas outside the City limits, the AEZ overlay provisions will
apply upon annexation.
В В В A.В В В New Development
В В В Starting January 1, 2005, Section
5.6.8, Approach-Departure Corridors for DMAFB Environs, and Section
5.6.9, Noise Control Districts for DMAFB Environs, apply to property located
within the zones  and following districts: ADC-1. ADC-2, ADC-3, NCD-A and NCD-
B .
В В В B.В В В Changes, Expansions and Additions to Use of Existing Development
В В В The following provisions shall apply starting January 1, 2005.
В В В В В В 1.В В В For a change of use of an existing structure, the provisions of
Section
5.6.8, Approach-Departure Corridors for DMAFB Environs, applies to the entire
existing structure if it results in an increase in the number of employees.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The noise attenuation standards and performance standards
established by Section
5.6.9, Noise Control Districts for DMAFB Environs, applies to the entire
existing structure if the use of the existing structure is changed to one or
more allowed uses requiring sound attenuation.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Dwelling units existing within the AEZ prior to January 1, 2005 may
expand or reconstruct provided that the new construction conforms to AEZ
standards.
В В В C.В В В Nothing contained in this section shall affect existing property or the
right to its continued use for the purpose legally used at the time these
standards become effective, nor shall these standards affect any reasonable
repairs to, or alterations of, buildings or property used for such existing
purposes.
В В В D.В В В Nothing in these provisions shall preclude the renovation of existing
structures .
В В В E.В В В Proposed expansion of a nonconforming use or structure is subject to
the provisions of Sections
9.2.2 and
9.3.2, respectively.
В В В F.В В В Owners of property within the DMAFB Environs and in the vicinity of the
military airport should be aware that A.R.S. Title 28, and A.R.S., Title 32,
contain provisions that may apply to some properties regulated under this
section. The provisions require property owners to inform potential purchasers,
lessees, and renters that a property is in an airport В zoning district .
Nothing herein requires property owners to provide notice that is in addition
to the standards provided by state law.
В В В G.В В В The land uses permitted are those permitted by the underlying zone ,
except as restricted by Sections
5.6.8 and
5.6.9.
5.6.8.В В В APPROACH DEPARTURE CORRIDORS (ADC) FOR DMAFB
В В В The required land use standards within the ADCs are as follows.
В В В A.В В В ADC-1
В В В В В В 1.В В В Performance Standards
В В В В В В The following performance standards are required in the ADC-1:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В No more than 30 employees per acre of site area is permitted;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The minimum project site area is three acres ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The maximum FAR is .50 of the project site area.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Prohibited Land Uses
В В В В В В The following land uses are prohibited in the ADC-1:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Civic Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Civic Assembly- Outdoor and Indoor;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Cultural Use;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Religious Use; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Educational Use.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Commercial Services Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Administrative and Professional Offices;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Commercial Recreation;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Day Care;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Entertainment - Outdoor & Indoor;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Food Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Medical Services;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Personal Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (9)В В В Transportation Service - Air Carrier;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (10)В В В Travelers Accommodation-Campsite; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (11)В В В Travelers Accommodation-Lodging
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Industrial Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Hazardous Material Manufacturing.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Recreational Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Parks and Recreation.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Residential Use Group (all uses within the group)
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Restricted Adult Activities Use Group (all uses within the group)
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Retail Trade Use Group (all uses within the group, except for
Marijuana Dispensary Off-site Cultivation and Marijuana Dispensary Off-site
Manufacturing Location)
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Storage Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Hazardous Material Storage.
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Wholesaling Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Hazardous Material Wholesaling.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Exceptions
В В В В В В The following are exceptions to the Prohibited Uses in ADC-1:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Land uses in the Commercial Services Use Group and Retail Trade
Use Group are permitted in ADC-1 only if the property was zoned RCV, NC, C-1,
C-2, C-3, C-3, P or RV prior to January 1, 2005. (Rezoning from these zones to
zones that provide for allowed uses in ADC-1 is encouraged);
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Land uses in the Commercial Services Use Group and the Retail
Trade Use Group are permitted on property zoned P-I, I-1 or I-2 in ADC-1 only
if a protected development right plan was approved by Mayor and Council
specifically for such use prior to June 30, 2005;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Developments not in conformance with the performance standards of
5.6.8.A.1 are permitted only if a protected development right plan, was
approved by Mayor and Council specifically for such development prior to June
30, 2005;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Single-family В dwelling units are permitted only if the property
is zoned IR, RH, SR, RX-1, RX-2, R-1, R-2, or R-3, MH-1 or MH-2 and such zone
was in place prior to January 1, 2005. (Rezoning from these zones to zones that
provide for allowed uses in ADC-1 is encouraged).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Flexible Lot Development, Section
8.7.3, are not permitted; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Residential development is limited to no more than one
dwelling unit per acre .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Parcels less than the minimum size required in ADC-1 and recorded
prior to January 1, 2005 may be developed in conformance with all other
standards specified in Section
5.6.8.A.1., 2, and 3;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Individual parcels of less than three acres may be separately
owned provided each such parcel is part of a site plan and covenants provided
in this subsection that encompasses at least three acres . The City shall be a
party for notification purposes to the covenants; and,
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Non-contiguous В parcels located within ADC-1 may be included
within a single plat or site plan for the purpose of determining employee
limits, floor area ratios and other performance standards provided there are
recorded covenants requiring conformance with the approved plat or site plan in
the form approved by the PDSD Director . Non-contiguous В parcels that do not
meet the above standards may be considered through Special Exception process as
specified in Section
5.6.10.
В В В B.В В В ADC-2
В В В В В В 1.В В В Performance Standards
В В В В В В The following performance standards apply in the ADC-2:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В No more than 20 employees per acre of site area at any time may
be accommodated by intention, design, or in fact;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The minimum project site area is five acres ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The maximum FAR is .30 of the project site area.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Prohibited Land Uses

В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Civic Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Civic Assembly - Outdoor and Indoor;

В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Educational Use; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Religious Use.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Commercial Services Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Administrative and Professional Offices;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Alcoholic Beverage Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Commercial Recreation;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Day Care;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Entertainment - Outdoor and Indoor;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Food Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Medical Services;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Personal Service;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (9)В В В Transportation Service - Air Carrier;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (10)В В В Travelers Accommodation - Campsite; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (11)В В В Travelers Accommodation - Lodging.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Industrial Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Hazardous Material Manufacturing.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Recreational Use Group
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Parks and Recreation.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Residential Use Group (all uses in the group)
