В 
В В В B.В В В BUILDING FORM
В В В В В В 1.В В В FaГ§ade Standards .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Each ground floor commercial street faГ§ade shall provide a
minimum of 60% doors and windows, with each suite providing a primary entrance
from the street .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Each ground floor residential faГ§ade shall provide a minimum of
25% doors and windows, with each unit providing a primary entrance from a
shared entry or a direct entry from the street .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Primary entrances shall be visible or identifiable from the
street . Secondary entrances may be provided from off-street parking areas.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A single plane of a faГ§ade at the street level may not be longer
than 50 feet without architectural relief by features such as but not limited
to windows, trellises, arcades, public art, or doors.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Buildings shall not exceed 160 feet in faГ§ade length along
streets , public spaces, pedestrian only streets , or private streets that have
permanent public access easements, in the R-1, R-2, and O-1 zones . In all
other zones , buildings shall not exceed 500 feet in faГ§ade length or 1,800
feet in total perimeter length.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Privacy mitigation is required when buildings over two stories
are proposed within 50 feet of adjacent single-story residences which are zoned
R-2 or more restrictive. Balconies, windows (except for clerestory and
translucent windows), or any other features that overlook the rear and side
yards of adjacent residences are discouraged. Privacy mitigation measures, such
as the incorporation of vegetation, screening elements, walls, building
placement and orientation, balcony and window placement and orientation, or
other design strategies shall be incorporated.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В All faГ§ade standards shall be reviewed and considered for final
approval as part of the building permit review. Preliminary review and feedback
may be obtained during the pre-application and site plan (development package)
review. Standards shall be met, regardless of prior site approvals for a
subdivision В plat , site plan , development package or other permits related to
the development В project .
Figure 5.11.6-A: Commercial Building Form Standards
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-16561.png]
В 
Figure 5.11.6-B: Residential Building Form Standards
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-16563.png]
В В В В В В 2.В В В Ground Floors.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The minimum residential ground floor height measured from floor
to floor shall be ten feet.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If a residential faГ§ade is less than five feet from a property
line , then it shall be elevated one to five feet above finish floor of the
adjacent В sidewalk to provide privacy for the individual residential unit.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The minimum nonresidential ground floor height measured from
floor to floor shall be 12 feet.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Street facing ground floors should have active uses. Buildings
with residential ground floors are encouraged to have gyms, leasing offices and
other tenant common space on the ground floor facing the street .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Setback Exceptions.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Porches, patios, and terraces without any vertical structural
elements may be established all the way to the property line .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Porches, patios, terraces, balconies, and porte-cocheres with
vertical structural elements may project into required setback area up to ten
feet, provided the setback area is not reduced to less than is required per the
adopted building code.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Bay and bow windows may project into the setback up to three
feet, provided the setback area is not reduced to less than is required per the
adopted building code.
В В В C.В В В BUILDING PLACEMENT AND SCALE
В В В В В В 1.В В В Step Backs.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Building Step backs shall be required along any CCT site
boundaries which are adjacent to an O-1 or R-2 or less restrictive zoned
private property line or public alley as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Within 30 feet of the shared property line , the building
shall be no taller than two stories (25 feet).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Within 50 feet of the shared property line , the building
shall be no taller than four stories (50 feet).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Within 80 feet of the shared property line , the building
shall be no taller than six stories (75 feet).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Any boundary along an alley may include the width of the
alley in the total step back required distance.
Figure 5.11.6.-B: Step Backs Between O-1 or R-2 or Less Restrictive Adjacent
Property
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-16565.png]
В В В В В В 2.В В В Building Orientation.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum Street В Setback . Non-residential and multi-family
В buildings which do not meet the minimum street В setback shall provide any of
the following in the area between the minimum and maximum setback area:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Required streetscape features per Section
5.11.6.E.2.;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Required public places per Section
5.11.6.E.3.;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Enhanced landscaping (beyond the standard requirements) which
includes 75% coverage with vegetative ground cover, or
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Landscaping in conjunction with other amenities which
contribute to the purpose of the CCT as determined by the Zoning Administrator.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Street Corners. When two or more public or private streets
intersect as part of a site , the buildings shall be located at the street
corners and meet the minimum setback В standards as required for the two streets
.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Buildings with Amenities. On sites with multiple street types,
medium traffic volume streets are generally best suited for a range of uses
such as retail, commercial, multifamily residential, and community spaces.
Ground floor spaces are particularly suitable for pedestrian-focused amenities.
Buildings fronting on public spaces or designated trails shall provide
pedestrian amenities, active ground floor uses, or outdoor dining options.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Street Relationship. Any development В project with Corridor
street В frontage shall maintain a relationship with the Corridor street by
either orienting the primary entrances to the Corridor, providing public places
along the Corridor, or designing for mixed use В development along the Corridor.
В В В D.В В В CONNECTIVITY
В В В В В В 1.В В В Integrated Street Network.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Existing public streets or an element identified in Section
5.11.D.1.b. shall be extended through the site , from the adjacent
neighborhoods, and into and through the proposed development В site .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Public spaces, pedestrian only streets , or private streets that
have permanent public access easements may be utilized in lieu of a public
street to connect neighborhoods and development В sites .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Existing or planned greenways, shared use paths, or other
dedicated facilities which are separated from vehicle traffic shall be
connected and extended through the development В site .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В To the extent feasible, public alleys shall be extended through
the site to provide access to parking, loading, and service areas .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Blocks shall be interspersed with Open Space , Private or Public
Streets , Alleys or Private Driveways. In the R-1, R-2, and O-1 zones , block
length shall not exceed 400 feet from property line to property line . In all
other zones , block length shall not exceed 500 feet from property line to
property line .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Pedestrian Connections.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Pedestrian connections shall be provided from any rear parking
area either through or adjacent to a building , to at least one publicly
accessible street В sidewalk . Connections shall be provided with one per every
200 feet of street В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В At least one connection shall be provided within 200 feet of a
corner.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Connections shall be designed with a clear height of at least ten
feet and a clear width of at least eight feet or 25% of the length, whichever
is greater, and shall be surfaced with ADA compliant materials.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Connections shall be designed to ensure visibility and safety for
pedestrians by maintaining sightlines between destinations and including
uniform lighting.
В В В E.В В В PEDESTRIAN AMENITIES
В В В В В В 1.В В В Shading.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Shade shall be provided for at least 50% of all sidewalks and
pedestrian access paths and 35% of all public or private open space as measured
at 2:00 pm on June 21 when the sun is 82 degrees above the horizon.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Shade may be provided by trees, arcades, canopies, shade
structures or buildings .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Shading devices, attached or detached to a building , may
encroach into the setback area by ten feet, provided the setback area is not
less than required per the adopted building code.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The use of plantings and shade structures in the City В right-of-
way is permitted to meet this standard with the approval of the Department of
Transportation and Mobility and will be evaluated for compliance with required
utility setbacks .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Streetscape Features.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В When Type 2-4 projects are proposed with more than 75 feet of
corridor street В frontage , sidewalks and street В landscape areas shall be
provided on all existing and new public and private streets . The minimum width
of sidewalk and landscape areas is provided below based upon street type.
Information on the streets types may be found in the Department of
Transportation and Mobility Street Design Guide.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Urban or Suburban Thoroughfare: eight feet sidewalk , ten
feet landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Urban or Suburban Connector: eight feet sidewalk , eight feet
landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Industrial Street : six feet sidewalk , eight feet landscape
border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Downtown/University District : ten feet sidewalk , ten feet
landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Neighborhood Commercial District : ten feet sidewalk , ten
feet landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Neighborhood Street : six feet sidewalk , ten feet landscape
border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Shared Street : maintain existing sidewalk , ten feet
landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Sidewalk and landscape areas are required for the length of the
corridor street В frontage of the site . Sidewalks must be designed to connect
to existing sidewalks at the termination of the site boundaries.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Sidewalk and landscape areas which are provided beyond a project
site's В frontage along an existing street may substitute for shading
requirements per Section
5.11.6.E.1. and public space requirements per Section
5.11.6.E.3. Once 100 linear feet of streetscape improvements are provided
beyond the site's frontage , shading requirements are considered fully
substituted. For each 50 linear feet of streetscape improvements which are
provided beyond the site's frontage , 500 square feet of public space may be
substituted. An agreement granting permission to construct and assuming future
maintenance responsibility shall be recorded with any affected property owners
along the frontage area.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Along the existing City corridors, there are a variety of
conditions and creative solutions will be necessary. In many cases, existing
sidewalks or landscape areas may already exist. In some cases, combining
(widening) existing and required areas will be feasible and desired, in other
cases it will be more appropriate to duplicate required areas on private
property. Preference will be given to designs which prioritize a safe and
enjoyable experience for pedestrians with landscaping serving as a buffer from
automobile traffic. Final placement of required sidewalks and landscape areas
will be determined in consultation with PDSD and DTM staff.
Figure 5.11.6.-C: Example Sidewalk and Landscape Design Alternatives
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-16567.png]
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Required street landscape borders and sidewalks may be located
entirely within the adjacent В right-of-way area or within the Major Streets and
Routes right-of-way area with approval of the City Engineer or designee.
Maintenance shall be the responsibility of the adjacent property owner.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Sidewalks provided on private property shall be connected to any
sidewalks , bike infrastructure , or on-street parking located in the public
right-of-way .
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Outdoor seating, dining areas, and landscaping may be located in
the sidewalk area where minimum accessible width around the design feature can
be provided per U.S. Access Board Public Right-of-Way Accessibility Guidelines.
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Street landscape borders as required per section
7.6.4 shall be modified per the CCT to require one canopy tree for every 25
linear feet of landscape border or fraction thereof, excluding vehicular
ingress or egress points. Trees shall be planted in an unpaved depressed
planting area, which must be a minimum of 100 square feet in area and eight
feet in width.
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Street landscape borders provided as part of a CCT development
В project shall serve to meet the requirements of Section
7.6.4.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Public Spaces.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Public spaces shall be provided for all development В projects .
The minimum amount of public space is based on project type and size.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Type 1 project : 500 square feet or 5% of total site area ,
whichever is greater.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Type 2 project , less than one acre: 1,000 square feet or 5%
of total site area , whichever is greater.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Type 2 project , more than one acre: 5% of total site area .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Type 3 project , less than one acre: 1,000 square feet or 5%
of total site area , whichever is greater.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Type 3 project , more than one acre: 10% of total site area .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Type 4 project : 10% of total site area .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Public spaces are intended for community gathering focal points
and may be provided as plazas, courtyards, enhanced walkways, pocket parks, or
other areas. Public spaces may be in different areas of the project , but each
space must be a minimum of 300 square feet of usable space with a minimum width
of 15 feet. Public spaces shall not be leftover areas of the site and may not
include parking, loading, or maintenance areas.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Public spaces shall be designed with the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Features which provide for active observation on the majority
of the public spaces' sides. This shall include ground floor building entrances
and windows. When a commercial or retail space is proposed, outdoor dining,
vendors, or outdoor retail displays would also be appropriate.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Connections to the space from the site and to nearby areas;
and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Lighting which is uniform and provides for safety.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Plazas or parks shall incorporate at least three of the following
features to activate the area:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Seating that is a minimum 16 inches in height and 30 inches
wide. Ledge benches shall be a minimum 30 inches deep.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В 50% shade.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Water features.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Public art.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Outdoor eating areas or food vendors.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Playgrounds or play features.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Splash pads.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Recreational equipment.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (9)В В В Bike amenities.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Public spaces may be private or semi-private. For residential
development , half of the required public space may be provided within private
or resident-only areas located on the ground floor or street level. For
nonresidential development , patron-only spaces such as restaurant seating may
also serve as required public space.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Commercial rainwater harvesting is required for projects meeting
the criteria of Technical Standards Manual 4-01. as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Type 1 project : not required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Type 2 and 3 projects : only required for new landscape
areas. New landscape areas must be depressed six to nine inches to maximize
water harvesting. If hardscape is removed or reconfigured it must be redesigned
to create positive drainage towards landscape areas and any curb cuts that feed
water harvest infiltration areas. Other standards of
TSM 4-01. shall not apply. Modifications to this standard may be granted by the
Landscape Architect when negative drainage impacts are created related to
existing conditions.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Type 4 project : Full compliance with
TSM 4-01. is required.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Interior landscape borders required per Section
7.6.4. may be satisfied by providing public spaces in accordance with this
section, except for landscape borders required adjacent to residential zones on
the site boundary.
В В В F.В В В SERVICES AND UTILITIES
В В В В В В 1.В В В Mechanical equipment, trash enclosures, and loading areas shall be
located outside of the required street В setback area and may not be located
within 20 feet of any off-site residentially zoned property or within 30 feet
of any on-site public space.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Passengers drop off or loading areas may be located between a street
and the building .
В В В G.В В В PASSENGER VEHICLES
В В В В В В 1.В В В Parking Requirements. Vehicular parking shall be required based upon
a parking statement which identifies the proposed uses, proposed vehicular
parking, existing and future transportation and mobility conditions, and
anticipated needs. Parking may be provided on a public or private street , in
parking lots , or in parking structures . Decisions regarding parking
statements will be made by staff from Planning and Development Services in
consultation with traffic engineering staff from the Department of
Transportation and Mobility. The parking statement must provide the following:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The sources used in establishing the parking statement.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Type and approximate square footage of all uses, specifically
including:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The amount of combined residential and non-residential
development which could share parking based on hours of operation and peak use;
and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The amount of residential development which typically
includes a low car-ownership rate, such as housing for the elderly, for those
with physical disabilities, or for low-income families.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The number and location of proposed vehicular parking spaces,
including:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Standard, accessible, and electric vehicle parking spaces;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Spaces both within and outside the site that can accommodate
shared parking arrangements; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В On-street parking spaces within the proposed site and those
within 500 feet walking distance of the site boundary, including current
approximate utilization rates during daytime and evening hours.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The availability, location, and distance to any alternative mode
of transportation, including:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Public transit stops;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Multimodal paths, greenways, or bike boulevards; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Bicycle parking spaces which are above and beyond the
standard requirement.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The planned interventions to mitigate safety hazards and minimize
parking impacts relative to any established residential use within 500 feet
walking distance of the site boundary.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Any other information deemed appropriate by the PDSD Director ,
or their designee, such as a traffic study.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Accessible parking spaces shall be provided in accordance with the
adopted building code and is based on the number of standard parking spaces
provided. In instances where standard parking is not provided, the City of
Tucson Building Official shall determine the appropriate amount of accessible
parking spaces required during review of the Development Package.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Vehicle Placement. Parking areas and driveways should be located to
minimize the impact on walkable streets , the pedestrian experience, and
pedestrian safety.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Parking lots .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В New parking lots or garages shall be situated to the rear of
a building . If placement to the rear is not feasible, the parking lot or
garage shall be located with the lesser dimensional width facing the street and
shall include enhanced landscaping on the street -facing side that includes 75%
coverage with vegetative ground cover.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Existing parking lots that are in the front of an existing
building may remain without improvements when:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В The parking lot includes ten or less vehicular parking
spaces;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В A Type 1 project is proposed; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В No other vehicular parking is available on the site .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Existing parking lots that are in the front of an existing
building may remain with improvements when:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В The parking lot includes 11 or more vehicular parking
spaces;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В A Type 2, 3, or 4 project is proposed;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В A designated pedestrian amenity or public open space area
of a minimum of 1,000 square feet is provided to break up the parking lot ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (d)В В В The parking lot В improvements include all reasonable
attempts to comply with the connectivity, landscaping , and rainwater
harvesting development В standards ; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (e)В В В The parking lot В setback В standards in
Table 5.11.6.-1. shall not apply.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Existing or proposed parking lots intended for transit
ridership (park and ride) are permitted along a street В frontage but not at a
street corner upon confirmation with SunTran regarding the usage of the parking
lot .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Parking lots which have any frontage along a street shall be
screened by a minimum 29-inch masonry wall.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Parking lots shall provide canopy trees as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В Parking lots in Type 1 and 2 projects with ten or less
vehicular parking spaces shall provide at least one canopy shade tree.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Trees shall be planted in an unpaved depressed planting
area, which must be a minimum of 150 square feet in area and eight feet in
width.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В Parking lots which provide green space for large trees may
reduce tree planting requirements if the following criteria are met. The green
space may be counted towards required rainwater harvesting and public space per
5.11.6.E.3. if all other standards are met. The required tree planting shall be
one tree per eight parking spaces for the parking spaces which meet the
distance requirement. The green space must:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Be publicly usable or densely vegetated with 50%
coverage of vegetative ground cover;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В ii.В В В Allow for large-scale mature growth of shade trees;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В iii.В В В Provide trees which are located within ten feet of the
parking spaces of which only those spaces are eligible for the reduction; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В iv.В В В Be a minimum of 1,000 square feet in area.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (d)В В В Parking spaces which are covered by shade from parking
canopies, plantings in the right-of-way , or a building as measured at 2:00 pm
on June 21 when the sun is 82 degrees above the horizon may be excluded from
providing the required tree plantings.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Access.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Driveways may be shared between adjacent В parcels or for a
single site but shall not exceed maximum allowed width. If a driveway is shared
between adjacent В parcels or for a single site , a recorded shared access
agreement or easement shall be required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В In multi-family or single-family , attached developments ,
one or two space garages and carports shall be accessed by alleys or on-site
drive aisles only.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Driveways should follow the recommendations of the City of
Tucson Street Design Guide (2021) and the City of Tucson's Transportation
Access Management Guidelines. Should conflicts in standards exist, priority
should be given to designs which optimize pedestrian safety and maintain safety
for other travel modes.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Garages and structured parking.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Semi-subterranean garages may project above the adjacent
finished grade by up to three and one-half feet.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Structured parking shall not be a street -facing faГ§ade.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Where structured parking is integrated with a building , a
"wrap" with a minimum depth of 20 feet of habitable ground floor space shall be
provided between the parking structure and the street .
