6.4.В В В RULES OF MEASUREMENT AND EXCEPTIONS TO DIMENSIONAL STANDARDS
6.4.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В This section is intended to provide uniform and consistent methods to
measure and apply the dimensional standards in
Tables 6.3-1 through
6.3-7 and to provide exceptions to these standards.
6.4.2.В В В LOT SIZE
В В В A.В В В Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В MS&R Street В Frontage
В В В В В В A lot with frontage on a street designated on the MS&R Plan shall be
designed with sufficient width and depth so that motor vehicles can enter or
leave the lot without backing out onto the MS&R street .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Lot Width at Street В Frontage
В В В В В В If a lot has street В frontage , the lot width at the street В frontage
shall be of a size sufficient for the purposes of locating motor vehicle and
pedestrian access improvements as required by the UDC or Technical Standards
Manual.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Uncombining Lots that Result in Nonconforming Lot Size Prohibited
В В В В В В Lots that have been combined in the past, either through the Pima County
Assessors Office or by development , may not be split back into the original
platted lots if the original lots do not meet current UDC requirements for
minimum lot size . Any rights to a nonconforming lot size were given up at the
time the lots were combined. Plats by themselves do not establish the right to
construct.
В В В B.В В В Exceptions
В В В The following are exempt from the minimum lot size standards of this
article:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Parcels that are:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exclusively and permanently restricted by plat , deed, or
covenant for private use, such as, but not limited to, common areas for
parking, recreation, open space , drainage, or detention or retention of
stormwater; or,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Dedicated to the public for parks, open space , or other similar
public purposes, such as placement of well sites, electrical substations, or
other utilities .
В В В В В В 2.В В В An existing lot that is less than the minimum lot area required by
the applicable zone because of a governmental act or condemnation or voluntary
transfer of property in lieu of condemnation may be developed if the proposed
development on the lot conforms to all other requirements of the UDC ; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В A lot which is less than the minimum lot area required by the
applicable zone may be developed with a single-family dwelling when all of the
following apply:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The lot was existing and of record on September 20, 1948, or was
legally created under Pima County jurisdiction and of record at the time of
annexation;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The lot is zoned for residential development ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The proposed development on the lot conforms to all other
standards of this article.
6.4.3.В В В В LOT COVERAGE AND SITE COVERAGE
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В This section provides a uniform and consistent method of determining and
applying the lot or site coverage standards, whichever is applicable.
В В В B.В В В Application
В В В В В В 1.В В В Site coverage is the area of an overall project site , inclusive of
individual lots , covered by the improvements provided below. Site coverage ,
instead of lot coverage , is used when calculating coverage limits for the
following: 1) Flexible Lot Developments; and, 2) a nonresidential development
that is subdivided into individual lots , but functions as a site , such as is
the case with some shopping centers. This type of nonresidential development
typically has a cross access agreement.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Lot coverage applies to all other types of development in accordance
with the underlying zone .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The following improvements are included in the lot or site coverage
, whichever applies: (See
Figure 6.4.3-A: Lot and Site Coverage.)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Any area of a building within the outside edges of the exterior
walls at ground level (design grade ). Any raised extensions of the ground
floor, such as bay windows and stairs that are less than six feet above the
ground, are considered part of the building вҖҷs lot coverage ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Vehicular use areas , measured within the outside edges of any
area allocated to vehicle use, whether improved or unimproved. To determine the
size of an unimproved vehicular use area , the minimum dimensions for parking
spaces, access lanes , and pedestrian facilities, as required by the
appropriate Development Standard, are used to determine lot coverage ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Storage areas, measured from outside edge to outside edge of any
area allocated to storage use. Storage areas include enclosed or open areas
used for storage or display of materials, equipment, refuse, or vehicles.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3048.jpg]
Figure 6.4.3-A: Lot and Site Coverage
В В В C.В В В Exceptions
В В В The following are excluded from the lot coverage and site coverage
calculations to encourage design flexibility and provide for additional
amenities.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Buildings
В В В В В В The following are not included in the lot or site coverage calculations,
provided the areas are located on the ground floor.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Roofed areas, open on at least one side, which provide shelter to
exterior areas, such as balconies, entrances, stoops, and terraces , provided
they are not used for utilities , maintenance, laundry, storage, or motor
vehicle parking.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Any part of a building used for recreational purposes provided
the recreation area:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Is separated by a wall from nonrecreational areas such as
laundry rooms;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Is designated for common use by tenants;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Is not used by the general public; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Is not within a dwelling unit .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Any outdoor area over an underground building which is either
landscaped, used for recreation purposes, or both. The roof of the building can
be a maximum of three feet above design grade measured to its highest point.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Roofed interior space which provides a physical connection to the
outdoors and natural lighting, either directly or indirectly. In addition, the
space:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Shall not, in whole or in part, be designated as tenant area;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Shall not have more than 15% of the total area allocated
toward corridor space;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Shall be at least 30 feet wide in any horizontal direction;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Shall have a minimum floor-to-ceiling height of 20 feet; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Shall not be within a dwelling unit .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Solar Collectors. Solar collectors, whether part of the principal
structure or an accessory structure , are not included in lot coverage
calculations.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Vehicular Use Areas
В В В В В В Within a vehicular use area , any landscaped area greater than 25 square
feet is excluded, provided the landscaped area is:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Larger than three feet in width; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Is curbed to protect it from vehicular traffic.
В В В D.В В В Lot Coverage and Site Coverage Calculation
В В В В В В 1.В В В The amount of lot coverage per site is not to exceed the maximum lot
coverage percentage permitted by the project вҖҷs underlying zone .
В В В В В В 2.В В В The lot coverage percentage is determined by dividing the total area
of improvements in accordance with Section
6.4.3.B, Application, and 6.4.3.C, Exceptions, by the total land area of the
site .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The amount of site coverage per overall project site , inclusive of
individual lots , vehicle use area, etc. is not to exceed the maximum site
coverage percentage permitted by the project вҖҷs underlying zone in accordance
with Section
8.7.3.D, Regulations for FLD Projects.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The site coverage percentage is determined by dividing the total
area of improvements in accordance with Section
6.4.3.B.2 and
6.4.3.C, Exceptions, by the total land area of the site  included in the
project вҖҷs subdivision В plat  or site plan , whichever is applicable, inclusive
of individual residential lots , functional open space , vehicle use areas,
etc. Compliance with the site coverage  standard is not measured on an
individual residential lot  basis, but on an overall project  basis.
6.4.4.В В В HEIGHT
В В В A.В В В Measurement of Building Height
В В В Building height is the vertical distance measured from the design grade
elevation to the highest point of a flat roof; to the deck line of a mansard
roof; or to the middle (between the eave and ridge ) of the highest gable of a
pitched or hipped roof, except as follows: (See
Figure 6.4.4-A, Measurement of Building Height .)
В В В В В В 1.В В В Historic Preservation Zone (HPZ)
В В В В В В Building height is the vertical distance measured between the highest
part of a structure and the finished grade at the midpoint of the front facade
of the principal structure, excluding chimneys, mechanical equipment, and other
miscellaneous additions.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Airport Environs Zone (AEZ)
В В В В В В Building height within the AEZ is measured in accordance with Section
6.4.4. When there is a conflict between the maximum heights permitted by the
project вҖҷs zone and the standards in Section
5.6.11, Airport Hazard Districts for TIA and DMAFB Environs, the more
restrictive applies.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ)
В В В В В В The vertical distance of a building is measured from design grade , at
all points within the building footprint , to the highest point on a roof. Any
wall extending above the roof, such as a parapet wall or a fire wall, is
considered part of the roof for purposes of measuring height .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Hillside Development Zone (HDZ)
В В В В В В See height measurement standards in Section
5.2.7.A, Building Height.
В В В B.В В В Measurement of All Other Structures
В В В В В В 1.В В В General
В В В В В В The height of all structures , other than buildings , is the vertical
distance measured from design grade at the base of the structure at all points
along that structure to the top of the structure. For structures within six
feet of a property line , the design grade used for measuring height is the
lower of the two grades on each side of that property line . Perimeter walls
are measured from the base of the wall that faces an adjacent property.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Airport Environs Zone (AEZ)
В В В В В В Structure and tree height is measured in accordance with Section
6.4.4. When there is a conflict between the maximum heights permitted by the
project вҖҷs zone and the standards in Section
5.6.11,В Airport Hazard Districts for TIA and DMAFB Environs, the more
restrictive applies.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3050.jpg]
Figure 6.4.4-A: Measurement of Building
В В В C.В В В Height Exceptions
В В В The following structural elements may extend above the maximum allowed
height, subject to any limitations listed: (See
Figure 6.4.4-B, Exceptions to Building Height.)
В В В В В В 1.В В В Ornamental Elements
В В В В В В Ornamental elements of the buildings and structures , such as spires,
cupolas, belfries, clock towers, and domes, provided that such elements:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Are not for human occupancy; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Do not exceed ten feet above the allowed structure height ,
except as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Ornamental elements of buildings and structures proposed to
exceed ten feet above the allowed structure height are processed in accordance
with Section
3.4.3, Zoning Examiner Special Exception Procedure. Additionally, the Design
Review Board shall review and forward a recommendation to the Zoning Examiner
on the proposal. Ornamental elements proposed to exceed ten feet above the
permitted height must comply with the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В The ornamental element is an integral part of thebuilding
вҖҷs architecture, and is not for human occupancy;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В The ornamental elements proposed to exceed ten feet above
the allowed structure height shall not be used for signage;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В The applicant shall demonstrate how the proposal minimizes
impacts to existing land uses by including a view shed analysis and an analysis
of the project site grades and adjacent property grades as part of their
submittal; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (d)В В В Setbacks required for the architectural element will be
one foot for each additional foot in height that it exceeds the base allowance
of ten feet.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Functional Elements
В В В В В В Chimneys, mechanical penthouses, and bulkheads for stairwells, elevator
shafts, or skylights, provided that such elements:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Are not for human occupancy;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Do not exceed ten feet above the allowed structure height ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Do not exceed 25% of the total roof area of the building on which
they are located.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Mechanical and Accessory Equipment
В В В В В В Mechanical equipment, except solar energy equipment, and accessories
necessary to the operation or maintenance of the building , provided that such
elements:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Do not exceed six feet above the allowed structure height ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Do not exceed 25% of the total roof area of the building on which
they are located;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Are concealed by an opaque В screen ; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Are located away from the edge of the building a distance
equivalent to the height of the elements.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Solar Energy Equipment
В В В Solar energy equipment, provided that such elements do not exceed the
allowable height limits by more than ten feet.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Parapet Walls and Safety Railings
В В В В В В Parapet walls and safety railings, provided they do not exceed the
allowable height by more than four feet.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Communications Antennas (Excluding Communications Facilities)
В В В В В В Communications antennas not in conjunction with a communications
facility, excluding dish antennas greater than three feet in diameter, provided
such elements do not exceed the allowable height limits by more than five feet.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Dish Antennas
В В В В В В Dish antennas , greater than three feet in diameter, provided that such
elements:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Do not exceed the allowable height limits by more than ten feet;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Are located as close to the center of the roof structure as
possible;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Are screened from view, to the maximum extent possible without
interfering with reception, from the street В frontage of the property at
pedestrian level by an opaque В screen , such as a parapet wall at the roofвҖҷs
edge; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Are located on a building with a structure height greater than 40
feet.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Communications Antennas (Associated with Communications Facilities)
В В В В В В Communications antennas , other than those associated with wireless
communications, in conjunction with a communications facility do not have a
maximum height limitation, provided the antennas are located at least one foot
distance for every foot in height measured from all property lines of
residentially zoned property.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Change of Zoning
В В В В В В If the Mayor and Council authorize a rezoning of a property and a
building (s) exists on the property that exceeds the maximum required height
for the proposed land use, the existing buildings shall be considered
conforming. This exception applies only to buildings existing at the time of
authorization, except as provided by Section
6.4.5.B.10, Historic Preservation Zone (HPZ).
В В В В В В 10.В В В Historic Preservation Zone (HPZ)
В В В В В В New construction in a Historic Preservation Zone (HPZ) may exceed the
height standards of the zone if additional height is needed to be compatible
with the height of other buildings within the development zone as determined by
the Planning and Development Services Department Director .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3052.jpg]
Figure 6.4.4-B: Exceptions to Height Limit
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013)
6.4.5.В В В PERIMETER YARDS
В В В A.В В В Application
В В В A perimeter yard is required in accordance with the applicable zone as
provided in
Tables 6.3-1 through
6.3-7. The minimum required width of the perimeter yard for each development is
based on the zoning classification of the adjacent В parcel (s) along each
individual lot line .
В В В B.В В В Measurement
В В В В В В 1.В В В The width of the perimeter yard is the distance measured
horizontally from a specified point to the face of each exterior building wall
at the wallвҖҷs highest point. The specified point is the property line when it
is an interior property line . Along a street В property line , the specified
point is described in Section
6.4.5.C, Street В Perimeter Yard. (See
Figure 6.4.5-B & -
C.)
В В В В В В 2.В В В A vertical structural member(s) (i.e., post, column, dormer wall)
supporting a roof extension from the principal structure is considered a wall
for applying perimeter yard requirements.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The perimeter yard may have different widths at various points along
the same property line , because the perimeter yard width is measured to the
face of each vertical exterior surface of the building .
В В В В В В 4.В В В On cul-de-sac and eyebrow front lots , the minimum street yard depth
may be measured from a straight line drawn between the front lot corners,
rather than measured directly from the curved front property line , except as
provided in Section
6.4.5.C.2.b, Carports and Garages in Single-Family and Duplex Development. In
no case, however, shall the minimum street yard  be reduced in excess of 50% by
this alternative measurement. (See
Figure 6.4.5-A.)
В В В В В В 5.В В В Where an unbroken section of a wall or a building is not parallel
with the lot line , the required perimeter yard may be applied to the average
width; however, such perimeter yard shall not be narrower at any point than
half the required width, nor narrower at any point than three feet. (See
Figure 6.4.5-A.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3054.jpg]
В 
Figure 6.4.5-A: Perimeter Yard Measurement
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-5425.jpg]
В 
See Sec.
6.4.5.C
Figure 6.4.5-B: Example of Perimeter Yard (Wall Height = 20')
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-5427.jpg]
В 
See Sec.
6.4.5.C
Figure 6.4.5-C: Example of Perimeter Yard (Wall Height = 32')
В В В C.В В В Street В Perimeter Yard
В В В The street В perimeter yard width is determined as provided below or unless
special zoning requirements dictate a greater distance or different point of
measurement.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Residential Development
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For single-family, duplex, and middle housing development , the
minimum required front street В perimeter yard is 20 feet measured from the
street В property line .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Where a lot abuts more than one street, the owner chooses which
street В lot line is the front lot line . From other than the front lot line ,
the street В perimeter yard is ten feet, as measured from the street В property
line . For carports and garages which face the street , the minimum required
street В perimeter yard is 20 feet regardless of the street .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В For carports and garages in single-family and duplex development
, the structural setback may be modified in accordance with Section
3.11.1, Design Development Option as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В For garages , a minimum of 18 feet (the length of a standard
parking space) must be provided in front of the garage door so that a full 18-
foot parking space is available for parking on site.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В For carports , if the distance between the back of street
curb and the carport is eight feet or less, a minimum of one-foot must be
provided.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В For residential development occurring as part of a unified
subdivision, reduced setbacks are permitted as designated on the approved
tentative plat . The minimum required front street В perimeter yard is five
feet. Carports and garages are permitted as follows:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В If accessed from the side perimeter yard or perpendicular to
the street , the minimum distance from street В property line is five feet, or
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В If accessed from the front or parallel to the street , the
minimum distance from street В property line is 18 feet, or
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В If accessed from the front or parallel to a street with an
ADT of 140 or less, the distance from the back of curb to the garage or carport
may be eight feet or less. The garage or carport must be setback a minimum of
one foot from the property line .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Nonresidential Development
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For nonresidential and multifamily development В abutting an
Arterial or Collector Street the minimum required street В perimeter yard is ten
feet, as measured from the existing or future property line as determined by
the adopted Major Streets and Routes Plan , whichever is greater.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For nonresidential and multifamily development В abutting a Local
Street : the minimum required street В perimeter yard is 20 feet, as measured
from the street В property line .
В В В D.В В В Exceptions
В В В Encroachment into the required perimeter yard is allowed as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Interior Property Lines
В В В В В В Along interior property lines , the following may extend two feet into
the perimeter yard , provided the side or rear yard is not reduced to less than
three feet:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Chimney;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Roof overhang; or,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Bay window.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Street В Property Lines
В В В В В В Along street В property lines , the following may extend into the required
width as provided below.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Within established areas , the following may extend two feet into
the required street yard :
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Chimney;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Roof overhang;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Stairs;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Bay window; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Open structures .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В On any corner lot , no fence, structure, object, or planting
shall be erected or maintained to interfere with the sight visibility triangle
provisions set forth in the Technical Manual.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Structures within Front Yards
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Shade structures or carports may project up to ten feet into
required perimeter yards . Such architectural features include, but are not
limited to, overhangs, moveable insulating walls and roofs, detached solar
collectors, reflectors, and piping.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A wall or fence not over six feet high may be erected within the
limits of a perimeter yard .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Adjacency to Certain Nonresidential Uses in Residential Zones
В В В В В В For projects in a nonresidential zone В adjacent to a residential zone ,
the perimeter yard may be modified to equal the nonresidential use adjacent to
nonresidential zone В perimeter yard standard for the project вҖҷs zone based upon
a finding by the Zoning Administrator that one of the following applies:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The adjacent residential zone is a:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Governmental use;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Right-of-way ; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Easement dedicated for floodplain, drainage, or utility
easement.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For annexed properties only, the adjacent residential zone is
used for commercial, industrial, storage, utility, or warehousing purposes at
the time of annexation.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/
2023; Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
6.4.6.В В В FLOOR AREA RATIO
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В This Section provides a uniform method for calculating the floor area ratio.
В В В Floor Area (FA), as defined, does not include any interior motor vehicle
parking or off-street loading that is accessory to the principal use .
В В В B.В В В ApplicabilityВ В 
В В В The FAR dimensional standard applies to residential and nonresidential
development in the Airport Environs Zone. The maximum permitted FARs are
identified in Section
5.6. The calculations are as follows:
В В В C.В В В Measurement
В В В В В В 1.В В В General
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В FARs are used in conjunction with lot coverage , perimeter yard
requirements, and height limitations to determine and define the building bulk
that may occur on a given site .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The FAR provides the maximum amount of floor area that can be
developed on a site . However, the maximum permitted FAR is based on the most
favorable situation and may not be achievable due to other development and
dimensional standards .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The floor area may be divided into any number of dwelling units ,
principal buildings , and accessory buildings provided the combined square
footage does not exceed the permitted floor area of the site .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Calculation
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Multiply the area of the site , expressed in square feet, by the
applicable FAR. The result is the maximum amount of floor area permitted on the
site . See Figure 6.4.6-A for an example of the FAR calculation.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В See the definition of "Gross Floor Area " (Section
11.4.8) and exception provided below for the types of improvements and site
area that are and are not included in the FAR calculation.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Exception. Any site area В dedicated or sold at nominal cost to
the City for public purposes is included as part of the site area for
calculation of density , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The additional density is less than ten percent of the
density permissible for the rest of the site area ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Such dedication was not a condition of approval for
applications, such as, but not limited to, rezonings or variance requests;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Such dedication does not include right-of-way В dedicated as
part of a subdivision В plat ; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В A deed for the area or a legally binding agreement to
dedicate or sell at nominal cost to the City is submitted as part of the
application.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-5429.jpg]
В 
Example: Nonresidential Project
FAR: 0.50
Site Area : 25,000 sq ft
Calculation
Site Area x FAR = Floor Area
25,000 sq ft x 0.50 = Floor Area
12,500 sq ft = Floor Area
Figure 6.4.6-B Floor Area Ratio (FAR) Calculation
(Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014)
6.4.7 RESIDENTIAL DENSITY
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В This Section provides uniform methods for determining residential
densities on individual sites .
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В В В В Residential density for all residential projects is calculated as
follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Residential density in residential projects is based on one dwelling
unit per minimum size parcel by area, exclusive of any street and drainageway
dedications or exclusive use easements. Because the acreage of a parcel used
for street and drainageway purposes differs for each development В project , it
is difficult to provide a simple calculation that would give an exact density
figure.
В В В В В В В В В The following provide two methods of calculating approximate density
for a project . The number of units obtained through these calculations is an
estimate and can only be verified by the actual design of the project . Should
there be a need to express a density ratio per acre , for comparison purposes
only, such ratio will be calculated using the second method assuming 30 percent
of the site would be used for streets , drainageways, or other uses whose
acreage is not included to calculate allowable density .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The first method of calculation gives the highest possible
density that can be achieved on a parcel . However, to achieve this density ,
the following two design criteria must be present. (See Figure 6.4.7-
A Residential Density , Method 1)
В В В В В В В В В В В В i.В В В The property can be developed without additional dedications
for streets , drainageways, or exclusive use easements.
В В В В В В В В В В В В ii.В В В Each lot is the minimum size permitted by the underlying zone
.
В В В В В В В В В В В В The site area , expressed in square feet, is divided by the minimum
lot size permitted by the underlying zone .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-5433.jpg]
В 
В В В В В В В В В В В В Site Area : 71,400 square feet (sf) or 1.64 acres
В В В В В В В В В В В В Minimum lot size : 7,000 sf
В В В В В В В В В В В В Approximate No. of UnitsВ В В = Site Area Г· Minimum Lot Size
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В = 71,400 sf Г· 7,000 sf
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В = 10.2 units
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В = 10 units + .2 remainder
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Approximate DensityВ В В = Units Г· Site Area
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В = 10.2 units Г· 1.64 acres
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В = 6.22 dwelling units/acres
Figure 6.4.7-A Residential Density , Method 1
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The second method assumes that the project design requires
dedication of either streets , drainageways, or exclusive use easements or will
have other site conditions that do not allow every square foot of the property
to be included in an individual lot . Also, due to varying widths of street and
drainageway rights-of-way and lot configurations, it is impossible to project
an exact number of units or lots that can be achieved. Since the amount of site
area used for streets , drainageways, etc., varies from project to project , it
is necessary to approximate the amount of site area by percentage that will be
needed for those purposes. It will be assumed, to illustrate the calculations,
that 30 percent of the site area will be utilized for such purposes. (See
Figure 6.4.7-B Residential Density , Method 2)
В В В В В В В В В В В В The calculation is the site area , expressed in square feet, minus
30 percent of the site area , divided by the minimum lot size permitted by the
underlying zone .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-5435.jpg]
В 
Site Area : 87,120 square feet (sf) or 2 acres
Minimum lot size : 7,000 sf
Site area for streets, etc.: 26,136 sf (approximately 30%)
Approx. No. of UnitsВ В В = (Site Area - 30% of Site Area ) Г· Minimum Lot Size
В В В В В В В В В = (87,120 sf - 26,136 sf) Г· 7,000 sf
В В В В В В В В В = 8.71 units
В В В В В В В В В = 8 units + .71 remainder
Approx. DensityВ В В = Units Г· Site Area
В В В В В В В В В = 8 units Г· 2 acres
В В В В В В В В В = 4 dwelling units/acres
Figure 6.4.7-B Residential Density , Method 2
В В В В В В 2.В В В Residential density in multifamily projects developed is calculated
in the following manner.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Multiply the area of the site , in acreage, by the density (units
per acre ratio) permitted by the underlying zoning.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Example:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Units Per Acre Ratio: 15.0
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Project В Site Area : 6 acres
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Calculation:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Units per acre x site area = no. of units
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В 15 x 6 = 90 units
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Exception. Any site area В dedicated or sold at nominal cost to
the City for public purposes is included as part of the site area for
calculation of density , provided:
В В В В В В В В В В В В i.В В В The additional density is less than 10 percent of the density
permissible for the rest of the site area ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В ii.В В В Such dedication was not a condition of approval for
applications, such as, but not limited to, rezonings or variance requests;
В В В В В В В В В В В В iii.В В В Such dedication does not include right-of-way В dedicated as
part of a subdivision В plat ; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В iv.В В В A legally binding agreement to dedicate or sell at nominal
cost to the City is submitted as part of the application.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Residential density in Flexible Lot В Development В projects is based
on the developable area (as defined in Section
11.4.5 Definitions - D) of the site .
(Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014)
6.5.В В В PRINCIPAL LAND USE
6.5.1.В В В ONE OR MORE PRINCIPAL USES , SEPARATE PROJECTS
В В В One or more principal uses conducted as separate projects on a parcel is
considered individually in determining the respective Land Use Classes and
dimensional standards
6.5.2.В В В TWO OR MORE PRINCIPAL USES , SAME PROJECT
В В В Two or more principal uses conducted as a single project , which integrates
elements of the various uses, such as, but not limited to, vehicular parking,
vehicular and pedestrian access, refuse collection, and loading services, as
common use among the various uses in the project , shall have dimensional
standards applied as follows:
В В В A.В В В Two or More Uses, Same Dimensional Standards
В В В On projects with two or more uses that have the same dimensional standards,
the dimensional standard requirements shall be applied on the site as if there
were only one use.
В В В B.В В В Two or More Uses, Different Dimensional Standards
В В В Projects without a residential component shall calculate the residential
density (RAC), setbacks and the minimum lot size standards separately for the
residential use. All other uses shall use the dimensional standards of the
principal use having the least restrictive dimensional standards.
6.6.В В В ACCESSORY USES , BUILDINGS , AND STRUCTURES
В В В Accessory uses and buildings are permitted in all zoning districts . Those
land uses and buildings associated with and incidental to a principal building
are accessory and subject to the provisions described below.
6.6.1.В В В ACCESSORY USES
В В В In all zones , an accessory use shall comply with the following:
В В В A.В В В The use shall be incidental or secondary to the principal use and is
located on the same parcel as the principal use ;
В В В B.В В В The use shall be intended for the occupants, residents, customers,
employees, lessees, or guests of the principal use ;
В В В C.В В В The use shall not substantially alter the exterior appearance or
character of the principal use or building to which it is incidental ; and,
В В В D.В В В Animals may be kept for personal use in all zones subject to Tucson
Code, Chapter 4, and any applicable health regulations.
В В В E.В В В Medical Marijuana Designated Caregiver Cultivation Location
В В В В В В 1.В В В A designated caregiver may cultivate at their residence for a single
qualifying patient subject to compliance with A.R.S. В§В 36-2806.01;
В В В В В В 2.В В В All conditions and restrictions for medical marijuana dispensary
off-site cultivation locations apply except that the designated caregiver
cultivation location cultivation area is limited to a total 250 square feet
maximum, including any storage areas; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В More than one designated caregiver may co-locate cultivation
locations as long as the total cultivation area does not exceed 250 square feet
maximum, including storage areas.
В В В F.В В В Medical Marijuana Qualifying Patient Cultivation Location:
В В В В В В 1.В В В A qualifying patient may cultivate at their place of residence
subject to compliance with A.R.S. В§В 36-2806.01; and,
В В В В В В 2.В В В A qualifying patient, cultivating marijuana, shall comply with all
lawful, applicable health regulations, including those promulgated by the
Arizona Department of Health Services and any other authorized regulatory
agency.
(Am. Ord. 11890, 12/7/2021)
6.6.2.В В В ACCESSORY BUILDINGS AND STRUCTURES
В В В In all zones , the buildings used for accessory uses shall comply with the
following:
В В В A.В В В The standards of this Section
6.6.2 apply in all zones to buildings used for accessory uses , except as noted
in the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В A structure if five feet or less in height and ten square feet or
less in area, such as a doghouse, a refuse container enclosure, or play
equipment.
В В В В В В 2. В В В A shelter for food producing animals if six feet or less in height
and 16 square feet or less in area.
В В В B.В В В An accessory building shall be built only on a lot occupied by a
principal building or use;
В В В C.В В В An accessory building  shall be developed in accordance with the
dimensional standards of the principal land use , except as provided by this
section and, when applicable, Sections
6.6.3, Residential Uses, and
6.6.5, Urban Agriculture Uses and Activities;
В В В D.В В В An accessory structure , except for an accessory dwelling unit , that
exceeds the allowable height of a wall within a perimeter yard and is detached
from a principal structure  shall comply with the perimeter yard width
standards of the principal structure , except that the accessory structure may
be built to a parcel line with the consent of the adjoining or, when separated
by an alley, adjacent property owner(s) or as permitted by Section
6.6.5, Urban Agriculture Uses and Activities;
В В В E.В В В Accessory structures , such as light poles, flagpoles, and other tall
and narrow structures that are similar, shall be exempt from the setback
requirement. Amateur radio towers are exempt from the setback requirement and
are allowed to a maximum height of 100 feet;
В В В F.В В В An accessory building , except for a stable or enclosure for animals ,
may be attached to a principal building , provided that its construction
complies with the development requirements of the principal building ;
В В В G.В В В The use of solar energy collectors for the purpose of providing energy
for heating or cooling shall be permitted in all zones , whether as part of a
principal building or as an accessory building . Such solar collection devices
shall not be included in computing lot coverage ;
В В В H.В В В All structures  for livestock and other large farm animals such as
ratites, as defined in the Tucson Code Chapter 4, Animals and Fowl, shall be
set back at least 50 feet from all property lines , except corrals that shall
be set back ten feet from all property lines . In addition, the proximity of
corrals and barns or other structures for large farm animals from a dwelling
unit on adjacent property shall be in accordance with Tucson Code Chapter 4,
Section 4-27;
В В В I.В В В The maximum height of a wall or fence within a perimeter yard shall be
six feet; however, the wall or fence may be higher than six feet, but no higher
than ten feet, if: (See
Figure 6.6.1-A, Height of Wall or Fence within a Side or Rear Perimeter Yard .)
В В В В В В 1.В В В At least 75% of the area above six feet in height is left
unobstructed and open through the use of architectural elements, such as
arches, columns, or wrought iron;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Part of, or located on top of, a retaining wall no higher than ten
feet measured from design grade and no higher than six feet measured from the
top of the retaining portion of the wall;
В В В В В В 3.В В В A greater height is required through the rezoning process or the
special exception land use process; or,
В В В В В В 4.В В В A greater height is required by a specific UDC standard.
В В В J.В В В In nonresidential zones , walls or fences, as permitted in Section
6.6.2.I above, may exceed the height standards, provided the wall or fence
complies with the perimeter yard standards applicable to buildings on the site
;
В В В K.В В В Accessory uses , buildings , and structures are not required to provide
additional parking, landscaping and screening, or loading spaces beyond what is
required of the principal use .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3064.jpg]
Figure 6.6.1-A: Height of Wall or Fence Within a Side or Rear Perimeter Yard
(Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014; Am. Ord. 11328, 12/8/2015; Am. Ord. 11890, 12/7/
2021)
6.6.3.В В В RESIDENTIAL USES
В В В В В В The buildings used for an accessory use to a residential use shall comply
with the following:
В В В В В В A.В В В An accessory building may be a building that is used as sleeping
quarters by the residents of the dwelling unit in accordance with Sections
6.6.1.A and B. The sleeping quarters may include bedrooms , bathrooms, and a
sitting room, provided the building complies with Section
6.6.1.C and is not the dominant use of the property;
В В В В В В B.В В В An accessory dwelling unit may be used as living quarters in
accordance with Sections
6.6.1.A. and B. Two accessory dwelling units are permitted per parcel developed
with a Family Dwelling as defined in Section
11.3.7.A., with the exception of a Multifamily Developmen . A parcel developed
with a duplex is permitted one accessory dwelling unit . The accessory dwelling
unit may include a kitchen, bedrooms , bathrooms, and a sitting room, provided
the building complies with Section
6.6.1.C. and is not the dominant use of the property. The following standards
apply to these accessory dwelling units :
В В В В В В В В В 1.В В В Accessory dwelling units are not subject to maximum residential
density standards per Section
6.3.4.
В В В В В В В В В 2.В В В On a lot or parcel of more than one acre in size, one further
accessory dwelling unit is permitted in addition to those permitted per UDC
6.6.3.B., if at least one accessory dwelling unit on the lot or parcel is a
restricted affordable dwelling unit , as defined in
11.4.2. The affordable unit must be deed restricted and proof must be provided
as a condition of the building permit.
В В В В В В В В В 3.В В В Vehicular parking is not required to be provided for accessory
dwelling units .
В В В В В В В В В 4.В В В If the accessory dwelling unit is built as a new structure it
must be developed with a high albedo level (>60 SRI) or other cool roof
technology per the ICC Green Construction Code.
В В В В В В В В В 5.В В В An accessory dwelling unit is limited to 75% of the gross floor
area of the principal dwelling, not to exceed 1,000 square feet of gross floor
area . An accessory dwelling unit may be up to 650 square feet of gross floor
area , regardless of the size of principal dwelling.
В В В В В В В В В 6.В В В The minimum side and rear yard setback for an accessory dwelling
unit is five feet.
В В В В В В В В В 7.В В В Except as specified above, an accessory dwelling unit shall be
developed in accordance with the dimensional standards of the principal land
use.
В В В В В В В В В 8.В В В Accessory dwelling units proposed within an overlay zone or other
zoning area with specific design processes or guidelines, shall be subject to
those regulations when no more restrictive than those applicable to single-
family dwellings in that overlay and are no more restrictive than the entirety
of UDC
6.6.3.B.
В В В В В В В В В 9.В В В Accessory dwelling units are not required to match the exterior
design, roof pitch or finishing materials of the single-family dwelling that is
located on the same lot as the accessory dwelling unit .
В В В В В В В В В 10.В В В If an established and occupied family dwelling proposes one or
more accessory dwelling units in accordance with UDC
6.6.3.B., the lot or parcel shall not require a commercial site plan per UDC
3.3.3.G., be considered a multifamily development, require commercial
development standards, or require improvements to public streets other than
repairs due to construction activity.
В В В В В В В В В 11.В В В Accessory dwelling units must follow the landscaping standards
in UDC
7.6.2.C. New Single Family , Middle Housing, and ADUs.
В В В В В В C.В В В Detached accessory buildings , including accessory dwelling units ,
are not permitted in the buildable area extending the full width of the lot
between the principal building and the front street lot line , except for shade
structures or carports not over 400 square feet in floor area , terraces and
steps not over three feet high above the natural grade , paved areas, and
fences or walls.
В В В В В В D.В В В Accessory structures , except for accessory dwelling units , shall
not exceed 12 feet in height, unless attached to a principal structure . If
attached to the principal structure , maximum height permitted shall be the
same as for the principal structure .
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11890, 12/7/2021; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/
2023; Am. Ord. 12131, 10/22/2024; Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
6.6.4.В В В NONRESIDENTIAL USES
В В В The building used for an accessory use to a nonresidential use shall comply
with the following:
В В В A.В В В An accessory building shall not be occupied as a dwelling, except as a
caretakerвҖҷs facility;
В В В B.В В В The area of a site occupied by an accessory use or building shall be
included as part of the lot coverage calculation; and,
В В В C.В В В The structure used for an accessory use within a nonresidential zone
shall comply with dimensional standards of the principal structure.
(Am. Ord. 11890, 12/7/2021)
6.6.5.В В В URBAN AGRICULTURE USES AND ACTIVITIES
В В В A.В В В Accessory Community Garden
В В В В В В 1.В В В Community gardens are permitted as an accessory use in any zone in
accordance with the standards provided below.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Permitted Activities and Uses
В В В В В В В В В The following activities and uses are permitted in a Community Garden:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Growing and harvesting of agricultural products;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The keeping of food producing animals  is permitted as an
accessory use  to the growing and harvesting of agricultural products in
accordance with Section
6.6.5.E, Keeping of Food Producing Animals . Permission from the property owner
is required and a sign must be posted identifying the person responsible for
caring for the animals and his or her contact information;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Greenhouses and on-site sale of agricultural products grown on-
site are permitted in accordance with Section
6.6.5.D and
6.6.5.F, respectively;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В During an on-site sales event, a temporary booth or canopy may be
located within the front yard setback of the property and is exempt from
Sections
6.6.1.C and
6.6.3.B;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Outdoor activity, including the outdoor display of products for
sale grown on-site , is permitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The growing and harvesting of agricultural products that are not in
a structure are exempt from the perimeter yard requirements of the underlying
zone .
В В В B.В В В Composting
В В В Composting shall be maintained in accordance with applicable sections of the
Tucson Code, Section 4-28 of Animals and Fowl and Section 16-13 of the
Neighborhood Preservation Ordinance.
В В В C.В В В Gardens
В В В В В В 1.В В В Gardens are permitted as an accessory use to any residential or
nonresidential use.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Gardens  not in greenhouses are exempt from this Section
6.6, Accessory Uses, Buildings, and Structures. Further, gardens may be located
in the buildable area extending the full width of the lot between the principal
building and the front street lot line .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Gardens in greenhouses are subject to Section
6.6.5.D.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Vertical gardens mounted on a perimeter wall or fence are permitted
provided they do not affect the integrity of the wall or fence and the
fastening devices do not project or otherwise create a safety hazard to
adjacent property owners.
В В В D.В В В Accessory Greenhouses
В В В Greenhouses are permitted as accessory buildings or structures as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Greenhouses must comply with Section
6.6.2, Accessory Buildings and Structures unless otherwise regulated in this
section.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Except when accessory to a community garden or urban farm,
greenhouses are prohibited in the buildable area extending the full width of
the lot between the principal building and the front street lot line .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Passive Greenhouses
В В В В В В Passive greenhouses shall comply with the dimensional standards of
Section
6.6.2. Greenhouses cooled solely by an evaporative cooler are considered a
passive greenhouse .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Mechanical Greenhouses
В В В В В В Accessory greenhouses are permitted in accordance with Section
4.9.2.B.1.c.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The maximum permitted height of a greenhouse is 12 feet in a
residential zone and in accordance with Section
6.6.4.C in a nonresidential zone .
В В В E.В В В Keeping of Food Producing Animals
В В В В В В 1.В В В This section applies to food producing animals  as defined in
Section
11.4.7, Animals , Food Producing.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The keeping of food producing animals  shall be conducted in
accordance with this Section
6.6.5.E and the Tucson Code, Chapter 4, Animals and Fowl. In the case of a
conflict, the standards of this Section shall control.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В This Section
6.6.5.E does not apply to:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The keeping of livestock; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Animals not defined as food producing animals such as
pigeons, parrots, dogs, cats, and fish.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Uncastrated male miniature goats older than 5 months and male
fowl are prohibited.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The keeping of food producing animals is permitted as an accessory
use to:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Permitted residential use in residential and nonresidential zones
;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Accessory Community Garden per Section
6.6.5.A; or,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Accessory Urban Farm per Section
4.9.2.E.1.b.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Maximum Number of Food Producing Animals Permitted
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Animal Units as defined in Section
11.4.2 are used as a basis for determining the maximum number of food producing
animals permitted.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Animal units are assigned as follows:
В 
Food Producing Animal                         Units per Animal
Chicken                                       1
Duck                                          2
Turkey or Goose                               4
Miniature Goat (female or neutered male only) 5
В 
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The calculation for the maximum number of animals permitted is
the lesser of either two (2) Animal Units per 1,000 square feet of the lot size
or the maximum total Animal Units of the lot size as follows:
В 
Lot Size                Maximum Number of Total Animal Units Permitted
Less than 16,000 sf     24
16,000 sf to 143,999 sf 36
144,000 sf or more      48
В 
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В It is recommended that miniature goats be raised in pairs or
small groups for their well-being.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Exemptions: The following are exempt from this Section
6.6.5.E:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Animal Production, General Farming, or Stockyard Operation
Uses or when an Accessory Shelter or Structure in the RH, SR, SH, RX-1, IR, or
MU zones. These uses must comply with Chapter 4 of the Tucson Code;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Non-profit, agriculture-related organizations, such as the 4-
H Club and Future Farmers of America. These organizations must comply with
Chapter 4 of the Tucson Code; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Rabbits, rodents, and invertebrates, such as worms. These
must comply with Chapter 4 of the Tucson Code.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Requests to increase the number of permitted food producing
animals  are considered in accordance with Section
3.3.4, 100' Notice Procedure.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Location and Setback Requirements
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Food producing animals and shelters are prohibited in the
buildable area extending the full width of the lot between the principal
building and the front street lot line . Food producing animals and their
shelters are permitted in other perimeter yards .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Animal shelters six feet or less in height and sixteen square
feet or less in area are not required to setback from side or rear property
line , but are required to be behind the principal building and at least 20
feet from the dwelling unit on an abutting property.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Animal shelters more than six feet in height and greater than 16
square feet in area must be set back in accordance with Section
6.6.2.D. Shelters shall also be at least 20 feet from the dwelling unit on an
abutting property. Exceptions: A setback is not required from property lines
В abutting В alleys or other types of rights-of-way or easements or when adjacent
to a nonresidential use.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Requests to reduce the setback requirements for animal shelters
are considered in accordance with Section
3.3.4, 100' Notice Procedure, Section
3.11.1, Design Development Option, or Section
6.6.5.E.7,В Existing Shelter Exemption.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The maximum permitted height of an animal shelter is 12 feet.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Food, water, and other items that may attract coyotes, bobcats,
javelinas and other predators must be kept in a secure location.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Existing Shelter Exemption
В В В В В В В В В a. В В В A shelter for food producing animals that has existed prior to
the effective date of the ordinance in a location that does not comply with the
food producing animal В shelter В setback standards set in the ordinance may
apply for an exemption from the affected perimeter yard В setbacks and dimension
standards of this section.
В В В В В В В В В b. В В В To qualify for the exemption the applicant must submit a PDSD
accepted site or plot plan and the shelter must meet the following criteria:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1) В В В There is proof from an aerial or other source of the
shelterвҖҷs existence within the qualifying time.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2) В В В There are no applicable Code Enforcement violations
involving the shelter for the past five years submitted by a property owner
within 300 feet of the subject property.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3) В В В The shelter is accepted as meeting basic construction
standards by PDSD.
В В В В В В В В В c. В В В PDSD shall establish a single fee to cover a single plan review
and inspection.
В В В В В В В В В d. В В В This exemption terminates on January 9, 2018.
В В В F.В В В On-Site Sale of Agricultural Products Grown On-Site
В В В The accessory sale of agricultural products is permitted in accordance with
the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Sales are limited to agricultural products grown on-site , including
produce and the eggs of food producing animals raised on-site ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Outdoor sales of products grown on-site are permitted;
В В В В В В 3.В В В On-site sales are limited to the hours of 7 a.m. to 8 p.m.;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Number and Duration of Sales Events Permitted
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В In the residential, office, institutional reserve (IR), parking
(P), and recreational vehicle (RV) zones and residential uses in nonresidential
zones , the on-site sale of goods is restricted to no more than four advertised
events per calendar year. An event shall not occur on more than three
consecutive days. An вҖңadvertised eventвҖқ is one in which the organizer
advertises the sales event Citywide online, or in any newspaper;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В In the commercial, mixed use, industrial, neighborhood commercial
(NC), rural village center (RVC), and mixed use (MU) zones , there are no
restrictions on the number and duration of sales events provided the sales are
accessory to the growing of agricultural products;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The combination of yard sales and on-site sale of agricultural
products shall not exceed four (4) events per calendar year.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Temporary accessory structures associated with the agricultural
product sales, such as booths and awnings, are exempt from Sections
6.6.1.C and
6.6.3.B and may be located in the buildable area extending the full width of
the lot between the principal building and the front street lot line . The
accessory structures shall be removed at the end of the sales event.
(Ord. 11328, 12/8/2015; Am. Ord. 11732, 2/19/2020)

7.13.В В В EXCAVATING AND GRADING
7.14.В В В HYDROLOGY
7.15.В В В SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING DISPOSAL, COLLECTION, AND STORAGE
7.16.В В В UTILITIES
7.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В This article sets forth the general development standards that apply to
principal and accessory structures and uses in the City . The standards are
intended to encourage high-quality development to enhance the safety,
aesthetics, character, and environmental quality of the community and to
minimize negative impacts between developments .
7.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The standards in this article applies apply to all principal and accessory
structures and uses established in the City of Tucson, unless exempted by the
terms of this article or elsewhere in this code.
7.3.В В В SOLAR CONSIDERATIONS
7.3.1.В В В SOLAR ENERGY COLLECTORS ALLOWED
В В В The use of solar energy collectors for the purpose of providing energy for
heating or cooling is permitted in all zones , whether as part of a principal
structure or as an accessory structure .
7.3.2.В В В SHADOWS FROM MULTISTORY STRUCTURES
В В В Shadows cast from any proposed multistory structure must be taken into
consideration as to their effect on adjacent properties. Where such shadows
adversely affect solar energy systems between the hours of 9:00 a.m. and 3:00
p.m., a plot plan must show that the multistory structure has been reoriented
on the site to mitigate this effect or that other measures have been taken to
minimize the adverse effects of the shading. The development potential of any
property shall not be reduced by compliance with this section. (See
Figure 7.3.2-A.)
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3066.jpg]
Figure 7.3.2-A: Solar Considerations
7.4.В В В MOTOR VEHICLE AND BICYCLE PARKING
7.4.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В This section establishes minimum requirements for motor vehicle and bicycle
parking to ensure that such facilities are consistent with the objectives of
the General Plan . As part of a balanced transportation system, these
regulations are intended to promote public safety and environmental quality.
Specifically, these regulations are intended to:
В В В A.В В В Ensure sufficient off-street motor vehicle and bicycle parking
facilities by establishing minimum parking requirements for land uses ;
В В В B.В В В Reduce excessive off-street parking ;
В В В C.В В В Promote pedestrian safety by separating vehicular use areas from
pedestrian areas and by providing a safe pedestrian path from parking spaces to
destinations;
В В В D.В В В Encourage safe, convenient, and efficient design of motor vehicle and
bicycle parking spaces , circulation, and access areas;
В В В E.В В В Improve air quality by requiring paving of vehicular use areas ; and,
В В В F.В В В Enhance community appearance.
7.4.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The provisions of this division apply to:
В В В A.В В В Proposed development or redevelopment;
В В В B.В В В Changes of use in existing development , except as permitted by Section
3.3.3.G, Zoning Compliance for Site В Improvements in Existence on May 1, 2005;
and,
В В В C.В В В Any expansion of an existing use or any addition of a new use to an
existing development .
7.4.3.В В В GENERAL PROVISIONS
В В В A.В В В Parking Required
В В В В В В 1.В В В Parking is required for every use unless otherwise specified in
Section
7.4.4.B, Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Spaces Required, or 7.4.8.B, Minimum
Number of Bicycle Parking Spaces Required.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Each Land Use Group (Group), except for Residential and Storage, has
a standard minimum parking formula. The standard formula applies to every Land
Use Class (Class) within that Group, except for those Classes specifically
listed in the parking tables.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The Land Use Groups and the Classes within each Group are defined in
Article 11.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Required parking for uses not defined in Article 11 are determined
by the Zoning Administrator.
В В В B.В В В Parking for Individuals with Physical Disabilities
В В В Off-street parking spaces for individuals with physical disabilities must be
provided as required by the City of TucsonвҖҷs adopted Building Code.
В В В C.В В В Change of Approved Vehicular Use Area
В В В Any change of the vehicular use area as shown on the approved site plan must
comply with the requirements of this section.
В В В D.В В В Changes of Use
В В В Parking for changes of use is required as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В When the proposed use is the same as the use it is replacing, the
parking remains the same in accordance with Article 9, Nonconforming Uses ,
Buildings , or Structures.
В В В В В В 2.В В В When the proposed use is different that the use it is replacing, the
parking remains the same provided all of the following criteria are met:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The replacement use must be a permitted use in the current zone ,
except a replacement use may not include a restaurant or bar (Food Service or
Alcoholic Beverage Service uses) or a similar use listed in the applicable Land
Use Group;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The parking intensity for a proposed replacement use, except as
permitted by Section
3.3.3.H, Zoning Compliance for Site В Improvements in Existence on May 1, 2005,
must be the same or a lesser intensity as a prior use on the subject property
as documented by the applicant. The property owner must provide documentation
regarding the prior use as required by the Zoning Administrator;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Existing on-site parking, landscaping , and screening may remain
in their current configuration; however, the Planning and Development Services
Director may require new improvements including paving and striping when a
public safety hazard exists or may be created; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The proposed use must comply with the City of TucsonвҖҷs adopted
Building Code pertaining to accessibility for individuals with physical
disabilities .
В В В В В В 3.В В В When the proposed use is different than the use it is replacing and
the criteria of Section
7.4.3.D.2 cannot be met, the required number of motor vehicle and bicycle
parking spaces shall be in accordance with Sections
7.4.4 and
7.4.8, respectively, except as provided below.
В В В В В В 4.В В В When the change of use is in a tenant space within a shopping
center, mixed use , or multiple use В development , the required number of
bicycle parking spaces is based on the gross floor area , or whichever method
for determining the minimum required number bicycle parking spaces in
accordance with Section
7.4.8, of the tenant space in which the proposed change of use occurs.
В В В E.В В В Expansions
В В В Expansions of existing uses are subject to the following.
В В В В В В 1.В В В If an expansion is less than 25% or if a series of expansions
cumulatively results in less than a 25% expansion in floor area , the
requirements of this section apply only to the proposed expansion. Existing
development on the site is subject to the zoning regulations in effect at the
time of approval of the most recent approved plan for the existing development
. However, if the existing development was approved prior to April 1, 1969, and
there is no approved plan on file with the City , the vehicular use area for
the existing development shall comply with:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The parking, screening, and landscaping requirements in effect at
the time the development permit for the existing use was approved; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The paving and striping requirements of this division.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If an expansion is 25% or greater or if a series of expansions
cumulatively results in a 25% or greater expansion in floor area , the
requirements of this section apply to the entire site .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Expansions as noted in Section
7.4.3.E.1 and 2 are cumulated over time from April 1, 1969, for the application
of motor vehicle parking regulations and from May 9, 1990, for the application
of bicycle parking regulations. Once a development is brought into conformance
with the provisions of this division, subsequent expansions will begin
cumulating as of the date the development was brought into conformance .
В В В F.В В В Calculation of Required Motor Vehicle and Bicycle Parking Spaces
В В В The minimum number of parking spaces required must be calculated based on
the particular characteristics of the use. The following methods shall be used
to calculate the required number of parking spaces.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Based on Fixed Seats
В В В В В В Use the total number of fixed seats to calculate the requirement. If
individual seats are not provided, each 18 linear inches of benches, pews, or
similar seating facilities is considered one seat.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Based on Beds
В В В В В В Use the total number of beds to calculate the requirement.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Based on Bedrooms
В В В В В В Use the total number of bedrooms to calculate the requirement.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Based on Gross Floor Area (GFA)
В В В В В В Use the total GFA of all applicable land uses within the development
В site , plus the area of any outdoor areas necessary to provide the service to
the public or conduct the activity, such as outdoor eating areas or outdoor
areas used for sale of merchandise, to calculate the requirement. The
calculation does not include vehicular use areas , automobile display areas, or
other outdoor areas used for nonpublic purposes. Where such areas are
identified on a development plan but are not defined, the Zoning Administrator
shall determine the extent of the area.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Based on the Number of Residents
В В В В В В Use the total number of residents for which the facility is authorized to
calculate the requirement.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Based on the Number of Students
В В В В В В Use the total number of students for which the facility is designed to
calculate the requirement.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Based on the Number of Employees
В В В В В В Use the shift with the greatest number of employees to calculate the
requirement.
В В В G.В В В Fractional Amounts
В В В When the calculation of required motor vehicle , bicycle parking spaces ,
and EVSE spaces results in a fractional number, a fraction of one-half or more
is adjusted to the next higher whole number, and a fraction of less than one-
half is adjusted to the next lower whole number.
(Am. Ord. 11953, 8/23/2022)
7.4.4.В В В REQUIRED NUMBER OF MOTOR VEHICLE PARKING SPACES
В В В A.В В В Calculation of Required Motor Vehicle Parking Spaces for Multiple or
Mixed Use В Development
В В В The total number of required spaces for a multiple or mixed use В development
is 90% of the sum of the amount required for each separate principal use in
Section
7.4.4.B, Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Spaces Required. The square footage of
Entertainment, Food Service (i.e., restaurants), and/or Alcoholic Beverage
Service (i.e., bars) uses may not be included in the calculation for multiple
or mixed use  parking requirements. The parking requirements for these uses are
calculated individually in accordance with Section
7.4.4.B, Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Spaces Required.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Exceptions
В В В В В В The calculation for a multiple or mixed use В development does not apply
to Shopping Centers, Golf Course, Religious, TravelersвҖҷ Accommodation, and
Lodging uses. Refer to Section
7.4.4.B, Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Spaces Required, for multiple or mixed
use  motor vehicle parking requirements pertaining to these uses.
В В В B.В В В Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Parking Spaces Required
В В В The minimum number of required motor vehicle parking spaces is provided
below in
Table 7.4.4-1.
