2015; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/2023; Am. Ord. 12060, 11/28/2023)
7.4.5.В В В REDUCTIONS AND EXCEPTIONS
В В В A.В В В Individual Parking Plan
В В В В В В 1.В В В The PDSD Director may approve a modification to the number of
required motor vehicle parking space and required number of EVSE parking spaces
on new and existing site through an Individual Parking Plan (IPP).
В В В В В В 2.В В В Applicability
В В В В В В The provisions of this section apply to:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Proposed development and redevelopment of a site ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Changes of use in existing development ; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Any expansion of an existing use or any addition of a change of
use to an existing development .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Permitted Uses and Types of Development
В В В В В В A proposal must include one or more of the following uses or types of
development :
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Combined residential and non-residential development in a single
structure or unified development ;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Projects within 1,320 feet (one-fourth mile) of a transit stop or
public parking facility;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Religious uses where the parking plan will accommodate weekend
and evening use;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Residential care services or housing developments for the elderly
or individuals with physical disabilities ;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Low-income housing ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В f.В В В A project that can accommodate shared parking arrangements for
uses with alternate hours of operation and peak-use times; and/or
В В В В В В В В В В В В g.В В В The parking area of any existing development may continue to
be used in its current configuration except where a public safety hazard
exists, may be created, or adjustments in parking space dimensions are
required.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Individual Parking Plan Requirement
В В В В В В An IPP must include the following information:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Number of required and proposed parking spaces. Indicate the data
source used in establishing the number of proposed parking spaces;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Location of parking spaces, including accessible parking spaces;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Existing and proposed site conditions and uses, including any
available on-street parking ;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Site access and traffic circulation patterns;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Location and distance from the project site to existing
residential neighborhoods;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Neighborhoods adjacent to the site with a Residential Parking
Permit program;
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Availability, location, and distance to alternate modes of
transportation;
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Hours of operation and peak use time (s) of each use;
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Evidence that all required parking for the proposed uses will
either be on-site or at an approved off-site parking location;
В В В В В В В В В j.В В В Existing and proposed shared parking agreements, when applicable.
The shared parking agreement must be prepared in a manner acceptable to the
PDSD Director ;
В В В В В В В В В k.В В В For projects within 400 feet of an R-3 or more restrictive zone
or Historic Preservation Zone, the IPP project must address how the proposal
will not cause a safety hazard, noise, or parking impacts on an adjacent
existing neighborhood. The IPP shall include the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Methods to avoid potential increases in noise and light
intrusion as described in Section
7.4.5.A.5.h, i and j;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Methods to deter vehicular access into adjacent residential
neighborhoods using signage or other means; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Methods to prevent drive-through traffic or habitual parking
within adjacent residential neighborhoods or commercial development .
В В В В В В В В В l.В В В Any other information deemed appropriate by the PDSD Director
including a traffic study.
В В В В В В В В В m.В В В For all restaurants or bars, the applicant is required to conduct
a neighborhood meeting and shall provide notice of the neighborhood meeting in
accordance with Section
3.2.2.C.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Findings for Approval
В В В В В В The PDSD Director may approve an IPP if all of the following criteria are
met in the IPP and documented in findings:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The zone permits the proposed use(s);
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В All parking is provided on-site or at an off-site location per
Section
7.4.6.B.1, Off-Site Parking;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В For multiple or mixed use В projects , the site can accommodate
shared parking arrangements for uses with alternate hours of operation or peak
use times ;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Site access and traffic circulation are not obstructed;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Accessible parking spaces required by the City of TucsonвҖҷs
adopted Building Code have not been reduced or eliminated;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The number of bicycle parking spaces has not been reduced or
eliminated;
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Vehicular access, drive-through traffic, and habitual parking in
adjacent commercial development and residential neighborhoods is deterred;
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Light intrusion onto an adjoining residential use or zone is not
created. Outdoor lighting shall comply with the City of Tucson/Pima County
Outdoor Lighting Code;
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Proposed outdoor seating areas are 100 feet or more from
residentially-zoned properties unless separated by a building . Combined
residential and non-residential development in a single structure or unified
development is exempt from this finding; and,
В В В В В В В В В j.В В В Outdoor loudspeakers or music (live or recorded) are 600 feet or
more from residentially zoned properties.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Review and Approval Procedures
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В An IPP for projects within 400 feet of R-3 or more restrictive
zones or Historic Preservation Zones is reviewed in accordance with Section
3.3.5, 400' Notice Procedure.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В An IPP for projects more than 400 feet from R-3 or more
restrictive zones or Historic Preservation Zones is reviewed in accordance with
Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Amendments
В В В В В В A revised IPP is required when one or more of the following occurs:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A change of use to a more parking intensive use or different
hours of operation or peak use times than the use shown on the last approved
IPP;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В An expansion of a building ;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В An expansion of a use that is more parking intensive than the use
it is partially or entirely replacing; or,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The parking or mitigation methods as set forth in the existing
IPP no longer comply with one or more of the required finding for approval as
provided in Section
7.4.5.A.5.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Violation of an Individual Parking Plan
В В В В В В If a development is operated in a manner that violates the conditions of
the approved IPP, the use may be suspended or terminated in accordance with
Section
10.4, Penalties and Remedies.
В В В B.В В В Downtown Parking District
В В В The following off-street motor vehicle and bicycle parking regulations apply
within the Downtown Parking District as set forth in Section
11.4.5.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Change of Use within an Existing Building
В В В В В В No additional motor vehicle or bicycle parking spaces are required for a
change of use which does not expand the existing building .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Expansions of Existing Development
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В No additional motor vehicle or bicycle parking spaces are
required for the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Expansions that do not involve construction of new building
or the elimination of existing required parking spaces; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Expansions that involve construction of new structures of
less than 1,000 square feet of gross floor area or less than 25% of the
existing gross floor area , whichever is less.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Expansions that involve construction of a new building (s) of
1,000 square feet or more of gross floor area or 25% or more of the gross floor
area of the existing building must provide motor vehicle and bicycle parking
spaces only for the area of expansion.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Expansions that involve the removal of existing required motor
vehicle or bicycle parking spaces must relocate the removed spaces either on
site or in conformance with the provisions of this division.
В В В В В В 3.В В В New Development
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Office Use
В В В В В В В В В Required off-street motor vehicle parking for office use is one space
for every 500 square feet of gross floor area .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Uses Other Than Office
В В В В В В В В В Required off-street motor vehicle parking for all uses other than
office uses in the Downtown Parking District is one space per 400 square feet
of gross floor area .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Residential
В В В В В В В В В Required off-street motor vehicle parking for residential uses in the
Downtown Parking District is one space for each dwelling unit , one space for
each apartment where rent/lease of space is not by the bedroom , or one space
for each bedroom in projects where rent/lease of space is by the bedroom .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Public Area Amenity Incentive
В В В В В В Required off-street В motor vehicle parking spaces for a use may be
reduced by a percentage equal to twice the ratio of open space to GFA up to a
maximum of 8%, if an interior public open space is provided. The interior
public open space may be a roofed atrium, courtyard, plaza, galleria, or
similar area. To qualify for a public area amenity reduction in required
parking, all of the following criteria must be met:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The space is designed to encourage pedestrian activity and public
use;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The space is not, in whole or in part, designated as tenant area;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Not more than 15% of the total area of the space is allocated
toward corridor space;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The space is a minimum of 30 feet wide in any horizontal
direction, with a floor-to-ceiling height of at least 20 feet;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The space is visible and physically accessible directly from a
public right-of-way or public open space and is located no more than one floor
level above or below grade ;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В A minimum of one linear foot of seating is provided for every 30
square feet of interior public open space ; and,
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В A natural lighting source, either direct or indirect, such as
skylights or clerestory windows, is provided for the space.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Motor Vehicle Parking Location
В В В В В В Motor vehicle parking in the Downtown Parking District may be located
within 1,500 feet of the use provided the parking is within the District
boundaries and is approved by the PDSD Director .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Bicycle Parking
В В В В В В Bicycle parking spaces in the Section
11.4.5, Downtown Parking District, and the Section
11.4.7, Fourth Avenue Business District, may be provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В On site . The PDSD Director may allow short-term bicycle parking
to be more than 50 feet from a public entrance (s) based on a finding from the
City of TucsonвҖҷs Bicycle Coordinator that the proposed location is consistent
with best practices pertaining to siting short-term bicycle racks, particularly
in regards to visibility, security, and convenience for bicyclists; or,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В By paying the City parking in-lieu fee in accordance with Section
7.4.5.B.7.
В В В В В В 7.В В В In-Lieu Fee
В В В В В В The off-street parking requirements established by this Section may be
satisfied in whole or in part by paying the City parking in-lieu fee in an
amount established by separate ordinance to be used by the City for the
installation of bicycle parking facilities or the construction of one or more
Downtown public parking facilities.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Design Criteria
В В В В В В All new parking facilities must be designed so that vehicles are not
visible from the adjoining В street level, through incorporation of design
elements such as pedestrian arcades, occupied space, or display space.
В В В C.В В В Reduction Based on On-Street Parking for Certain Residential Uses
В В В On-street parking for single-family dwellings, mobile home dwellings, and
Residential Care Services with ten or fewer residents may be counted on a
space-for-space basis toward the total required amount of parking up to 50% if
the on-street parking is located on the same side of the street as the use and
does not extend beyond the street В frontage of the subject property.
В В В D.В В В Exemption Based on Maintenance of Historic Structure
В В В Non-residential uses with a parking formula of one space per 300 square feet
gross floor area or less intense formula that occupy a structure listed
individually on the National Register of Historic Places or listed as a
contributing property in a National Register Historic District, are exempt from
Section
7.4.4.B, Minimum Number of Motor Vehicle Spaces Required, provided the historic
designation of the contributing structure is maintained.
В В В E.В В В Reduction Based on Additional Electric Vehicle Supply Equipment
В В В В В В 1.В В В General Requirements
В В В В В В В В В The number of required motor vehicle parking spaces may be reduced by
including more EVSE than required in
Table 7.4.11-1: Minimum Required EVSE . The following conditions apply.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The cumulative reduction does not exceed 30% of the required
number of spaces prior to any reduction; and
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The required number of EVSE is based on the total number of motor
vehicle parking spaces provided; and
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Parking spaces for individuals with physical disabilities must be
provided and designed as required by the City of Tucson's adopted Building Code
and the number provided shall be based on the total number of motor vehicle
parking spaces required before any reduction has occurred. This does not apply
to additional accessible electric vehicle (EV) charging spaces as required by
Section
7.4.11.B.2; and
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The amount of parking reduced for additional EVSE in specific
Land Use Group, Class and Types are listed in
Table 7.4.5-1 below.

site Manufacturing         2                        n/a
Location

В 
В В В F.В В В Other Permitted Reductions
В В В В В В 1.В В В General Requirements
В В В В В В The number of required motor vehicle parking spaces may be reduced under
the following conditions:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The project complies with one or more of the permitted reductions
in Section
7.4.5.E.2-8 below;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The cumulative reduction for this sub-section does not exceed 20%
of the required number of spaces prior to any reduction;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If more than one reduction is applied, the parking requirement is
calculated based on the required number prior to any reduction; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The required number of spaces for individuals with physical
disabilities is based on the total number of motor vehicle parking spaces
required before any reduction has occurred.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Reduction Based on Providing Additional Accessible Parking
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For existing development , the number of required parking spaces
may be reduced by two spaces for every one non-required accessible parking
space.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For proposed development , the number of required parking spaces
may be reduced by one parking space for every one non-required accessible
parking space.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Reduction Based on Providing Additional Bicycle Parking
В В В В В В For every six non-required bicycle parking spaces that meet the short or
long-term bicycle parking standards, the motor vehicle parking requirement may
be reduced by one space. Motor vehicle parking spaces may be converted to
bicycle parking spaces pursuant to Section
7.4.9.B.1.g.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Reduction Based on Valet Parking
В В В В В В The Zoning Administrator may approve valet parking as a means of
satisfying a portion of the off-street parking requirements when there is an
assurance of continued operation of valet parking and evidence of an available
area for the valet parking and vehicle stacking spaces . Internal residential
neighborhood streets may not be used for valet parking operations, which
include drop-off, pick-up, parking, and driving access between the valet
parking area and business it serves.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Reduction Based on MS&R Criteria
В В В В В В The number of off-street parking spaces required for any nonresidential
development located on a street designated on the MS&R Plan may be reduced in
accordance with the calculations in Section
5.4.6, MS&R Street В Perimeter Yard and Parking Adjustment.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Reduction Based on Landscaping and Screening Criteria
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The following reductions apply to existing development :
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The number of spaces required may be reduced if the
development is modified to comply with all applicable sections of Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening, except for Section
7.6.4.B.1, Canopy Trees in Vehicular Use Areas; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В For every three non-required canopy trees provided in the
vehicular use area , the motor vehicle parking requirement may be reduced by
one space. The planting area for each tree must comply with the canopy tree in
vehicular use area spatial requirements in accordance with Section
7.6.4.B.1.a.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For comprehensive redevelopment or development of a site , the
motor vehicle parking requirement may be reduced by one space for every four
non-required canopy trees provided in the vehicular use area . The planting
area for each tree must comply with the canopy tree in vehicular use area
spatial requirements in accordance with Section
7.6.4.B.1.a.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Reduction Based on Providing Trash and Recycling Enclosures
В В В В В В When an existing development is modified to comply with the enclosure
requirement for trash and recycling, the number of required parking spaces may
be reduced up to two parking spaces per container enclosure , but not to exceed
10% of the required parking.
В В В G.В В В Diversion of Required Parking
В В В В В В 1.В В В Temporary Diversion
В В В В В В Motor vehicle parking spaces within a vehicular use area may be used
temporarily for storage or display of boats, cars, recreational vehicles, semi-
truck trailers, furniture, or items of any other nature, subject to all of the
following conditions:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The vehicular use area contains more than nine spaces for motor
vehicle parking;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the vehicular use area contains fewer than 250 spaces, no more
than 10% of the total number of vehicle parking spaces is diverted to another
use;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The maximum period of diversion is 15 days . Additional 15 day
extensions up to a maximum of 60 days per year may be granted for reasonable
cause upon written request from the applicant;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Spaces are not diverted to another use more than once in any
calendar month;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The spaces diverted are not designated or designed for use by the
physically disabled; and,
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В The diversion is not for the purpose of dismantling or repairing
vehicles.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Diversion Other Than Temporary
В В В В В В Code required parking spaces for any land use may not be sold, leased, or
otherwise diverted to another use until off-street parking provisions are
secured and provided on another site satisfying all provisions of this section.
Approval is required for any proposed diversion of the vehicular use area .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exception
В В В В В В В В В Any park-and-ride lot set aside to facilitate the use of the mass
transit system may use up to a maximum of 5% of the required vehicular use area
.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11171, 5/20/2014; Am. Ord. 11201, 9/23/
2014; Am. Ord. 11732, 2/19/2020; Am. Ord. 11922, 7/12/2022; Am. Ord. 11953, 8/
23/2022; Am. Ord. 12152, 3/18/2025)
7.4.6.В В В MOTOR VEHICLE USE AREA DESIGN CRITERIA
В В В A.В В В General
В В В The vehicular use area includes the parking spaces, parking area access
lanes (PAALs ), and any areas necessary to provide maneuvering, refuse
collection locations, or loading spaces. Landscaping and screening within the
vehicular use area are considered part of the vehicular use area .
В В В B.В В В Location
В В В Required motor vehicle parking must be located on-site except as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Off-Site Parking
В В В В В В Off-site parking is permitted under the following conditions.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For projects outside the Downtown Parking District , off-site
parking must be located within 600 feet of the project site . For projects in
the Downtown Parking District , off-site parking must be located within 1,500
feet of the land use provided the parking is within the District boundaries and
is approved by the PDSD Director . For purposes of this section, the distance
between required parking and the use it serves is the walking distance measured
along the pedestrian access system from the closest points between the building
or use and the vehicular use area .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The off-site parking location has parking in excess of the
minimum required parking for its use or can demonstrate alternate hours of
operation to the use it serves.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Non-residential uses may not use residentially-zoned property for
off-site parking.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A formal written and signed shared parking agreement between the
owner(s) of the shared parking area and the owner(s) of the shared parking use
in a form approved by the City is required.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The off-site В parking area meets the design criteria provided in
this section.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В When required parking is separated from the principal use by an
arterial or collector street , signage directing customers to the nearest legal
pedestrian crossing is required.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Historic Districts. Land uses within a historic district may
apply the location requirements of this section unless different requirements
are established specific to that historic district .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Parking for Certain Residential Uses
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Individual Residential Lots
В В В В В В В В В Visitor parking spaces on individual residential lots are permitted
under the following conditions:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В At least one visitor parking space is provided on each
residential lot within a project site ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The visitor parking space is at least eight and one half feet
by 18 feet in size; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The designated visitor parking space(s) is shown on the
tentative plat or site plan , whichever is applicable.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В On-Street Parking
В В В В В В В В В Visitor parking for single-family and mobile home dwellings is
permitted on-street if the street is wide enough to accommodate parking on both
sides of the street .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Common Use Area
В В В В В В В В В Visitor parking in common use areas is permitted. Visitor parking
spaces must be within 250 feet of the front or street side yard property lines
of each residential unit.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Tandem Parking
В В В В В В Tandem parking for motor vehicles are allowed only for:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Residential Care Services with four or fewer spaces provided;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Single-family residential development ;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Mobile home dwellings;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Duplexes on individual lots ;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Valet parking ;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Non-residential uses within contributing properties to National
Register historic districts;
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Home Occupations ; or,
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Vehicle Storage or Display, when it is accessory to the principal
use ; or
В В В В В В В В В I.В В В Middle Housing.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Overflow Parking
В В В В В В For uses where the peak parking demand is occasional or seasonal (e.g.,
shopping centers and golf courses), the Zoning Administrator may allow open
space areas to be converted to overflow parking areas for special occasions or
high-demand days . These areas can retain a natural appearance and be off-
limits to vehicles except during these peak periods. Pervious pavement
materials that have a decorative or natural appearance may be used.
В В В C.В В В Areas That May Not Be Counted As Required Parking
В В В The following areas are not counted as required parking:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Spaces in service bays , stacking areas, or car wash bays ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В At gasoline pumps or other hose locations;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Those used for the storage or display of vehicles for sale or rent
to the public; and,
В В В В В В 4.В В В Parking spaces may not be located under, or within five feet of, a
vertical line intersecting the ground and any structural element extending from
a billboard, including, but not limited to, overhangs, cantilevered beams, and
elevated walkways.
В В В D.В В В Motor Vehicle Use Area Dimensions
В В В В В В 1.В В В Motor Vehicle Use Area Dimensions
В В В В В В The minimum parking space, access lane , Parking Area Access Lane (PAAL)
, and driveway dimensions are required as follows.
Figure 7.4.6-A is to be used in conjunction with
Tables 7.4.6-1 &
2 below.

В 
В В В В В В 2.В В В Exceptions and Additional Dimensional Requirements
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Accessible Parking Space Size
В В В В В В В В В Parking spaces for individuals with physical disabilities must be
provided and designed as required by the City of TucsonвҖҷs adopted Building
Code.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Minimum Width Requirement When Adjacent to Barrier
В В В В В В В В В A motor vehicle off-street parking space must have a minimum width of
ten feet when the side(s) of the parking space abuts a vertical barrier over
six inches in height, other than a vertical support for a carport .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Parallel Parking Spaces
В В В В В В В В В A parallel parking space must be eight feet wide and 23 feet in
length, except as follows. A parallel parking space can be reduced to 18 feet
in length if the space is located immediately adjacent to a driveway , access
lane , PAAL , alley , or street intersection and the parking space is designed
to provide maneuvering area on at least one end. On-street parking must be
approved by the Traffic Engineer.
В В В E.В В В Additional Access Lane and Parking Area Access Lanes (PAAL) Design
Criteria
В В В В В В 1.В В В Intersections
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A minimum unobstructed radius of five feet is required for all
PAAL intersections, except as follows. A minimum unobstructed radius of 18 feet
is required where an access lane or PAAL designated as a fire lane or is used
to access refuse and/or recycling collection or loading zones intersects
another access lane or PAAL .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В All intersection radii must be physically defined by curb or
similar material when permanent improvements or fixtures, including landscaping
, are located adjacent to the intersection. In all other instances, the
intersection must be delineated, at a minimum, by paint or similar markings.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Height Clearance
В В В В В В The minimum height clearance along access lanes and PAALs is 15 feet,
except as follows. The minimum height clearance within parking garages may be
less than 15 feet as permitted by the City вҖҷs adopted Building Code.
В В В F.В В В Circulation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Ingress and Egress Locations
В В В В В В Each vehicularingress and egress point to or from a street must comply
with the curb cut regulations as specified in Chapter 25, Streets and Sidewalk,
of the Tucson Code.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Setbacks from Access Lanes and PAALs
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Access lanes and PAALs must be setback at least one foot from:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В An open structure , such as a carport or covered pedestrian
access path as measured from the closest part of the structure or roof
overhang; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В A structure when the access lane or PAAL serves as a drive-
through lane.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Access lanes and PAALs must be setback at least two feet from a
wall, screen , or other obstruction over six inches. The additional area is
necessary to provide clearance for fire, sanitation, and delivery vehicles.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Sight Visibility
В В В В В В Sight visibility at points of ingress into, egress from , or within the
vehicular use area must be provided in accordance with Section
10-01.5.0, Sight Visibility, of the Technical Standards Manual.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Back-Up Spur (See
Figure 7.4.6-B.)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A back-up spur must be provided at the end of a row of parking if
no ingress or egress is provided at that end.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The spur must be a minimum of three feet in depth and have a
three foot radii and a wheel barrier to prevent encroachment onto any
unsurfaced areas.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A minimum distance of three (feet must be provided between the
back of spur and any wall, screen , or other obstruction over six inches in
height .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3070.jpg]
Figure 7.4.6-B: Back Up Spur
В В В G.В В В Striping
В В В Parking spaces must be marked with a four inch wide, white stripe along all
sides, except at the entrance to the stall or where the limits of the space are
defined by other means, such as curbing, except as follows. Striping is not
required when tandem parking is permitted in accordance with Section
7.4.6.B.3, Tandem Parking.
В В В H.В В В Barriers
В В В В В В 1.В В В Barriers, such as post barricades or wheel stop curbing, are
required in a vehicular use area to prevent vehicles from extending beyond the
property lines , to prevent cars from damaging adjacent В landscaping , walls,
or buildings , overhanging adjacent В sidewalk areas , and/or driving onto
unimproved portions of the site .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exception
В В В В В В В В В Barriers are not required to prevent vehicles from overhanging
adjacent В sidewalk areas when the sidewalk is curbed and the applicant can
demonstrate that a clearance space of at least four feet in width is provided
for pedestrian access. When demonstrating compliance, the applicant must
account for a vehicle overhang of two and one-half feet and any other
obstructions in the sidewalk , such as, bicycle parking racks, vending
machines, and merchandise display space.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Barriers may not impede pedestrian circulation and accessible
routes.
В В В В В В 3.В В В When required, wheel stop curbing must be a minimum of two and one-
half feet from the front of the parking space. (See
Figure 7.4.6-C.) Exception: When a tree is located at the edge or between
parking spaces, the minimum required vehicle overhang shall be in accordance
with Section
5-01.3.3, Vehicle Overhangs, of the Technical Standards Manual.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3072.jpg]
Figure 7.4.6-C: Wheel Stop Curbing
В В В I.В В В Surfacing Requirements
В В В В В В 1.В В В All vehicular use areas must be surfaced with one of the following
materials.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Pervious and impervious asphaltic concrete;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Pervious and impervious cement concrete;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A penetration treatment of bituminous material and seal coat of
bituminous binder and mineral aggregate; or,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Alternative surfacing as determined appropriate by the City
Engineer. Exception: Vehicular use areas for residential development with four
or fewer spaces provided, and vehicle storage are exempt from the surfacing
requirements.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The surface must be maintained in a smooth, durable, and well-
drained condition and be kept clear of debris or other accumulated refuse.
В В В J.В В В Lighting
В В В Lighting provided in any vehicular use area or for a bicycle parking
facility must be in compliance with the adopted Outdoor Lighting Code of the
City of Tucson.
В В В K.В В В Use of Street or Alley for Maneuvering Area
В В В A street or alley can not be used for maneuvering directly into or from any
parking space located wholly or partially outside the public right-of-way ,
except as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The following uses are permitted to use a street or alley for access
and maneuvering:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Residential uses (including, but not limited to, single-family
dwellings, mobile home dwellings, duplexes on individual lots, multifamily
development, and Residential Care Services) that provide four or fewer off-
street motor vehicle parking spaces;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Home occupations ; or,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Non-residential uses within contributing properties to a National
Register Historic District.
В В В В В В 2.В В В An alley , when used for access, must be a minimum of 20 feet wide,
free of obstructions, and surfaced with a dust control method that is
acceptable to the TDOT City Engineer.
В В В В В В 3.В В В A street may be used for maneuvering directly into or from a parking
space, provided the parking space is located completely within the right-of-way
and the design of the parking layout is approved by the TDOT City Engineer.
В В В В В В 4.В В В These exceptions are not applicable on MS&R designated streets as
provided in the MS&R Plan .
В В В L.В В В Screening and Landscaping Requirements
В В В В В В 1.В В В Vehicular use areas shall be developed in accordance with Section
7.6.4.B, Vehicular Use Areas.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/2023; Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/
2025)
7.4.7.В В В MOTOR VEHICLE STACKING REQUIREMENTS
В В В A.В В В Requirement
В В В The minimum vehicle stacking capacity required is as follows.
В 
TABLE 7.4.7-1: MINIMUM VEHICLE STACKING REQUIREMENTS
Use                                      Minimum Vehicle Stacking Capacity (per
                                         drive-through lane)*
Automotive Washing (Self-Service)        1 vehicle space
Automotive Washing (Full-Service) and
Food Service where there are separate    4 vehicle spaces
points of service for ordering and pick-
up
All other uses                           3 vehicle spaces
* The space at the point of service counts as one vehicle space.
В 
В В В B.В В В Design Criteria
В В В В В В 1.В В В Each stacking space must be a minimum of nine feet in width and 18
feet in length.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The stacking area for drive-through lanes must not cross on-site
pedestrian access.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Stacking spaces may not impede on-site traffic circulation and
ingress to and egress from the project site .
В В В В В В 4.В В В Drive-through lanes must be striped, marked, or otherwise clearly
delineated.
7.4.8.В В В REQUIRED NUMBER OF BICYCLE PARKING SPACES
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В The purpose of this section is to encourage the use of bicycles by providing
safe and convenient places to park bicycles. These regulations further the City
вҖҷs goal of being a bicycle friendly community by ensuring that the necessary
facilities are in place to accommodate cyclists.
В В В B.В В В Minimum Number of Bicycle Parking Spaces Required
В В В В В В 1.В В В The number of short and long-term bicycle parking spaces required
for each Land Use Group, Class and Type is listed in
Table 7.4.8-1 below.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exceptions
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В No long-term bicycle parking is required on a site where
there is less than 2,500 square feet of gross floor area .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Bicycle Parking In-Lieu Fee. The required number of bicycle
parking spaces may be satisfied partially or completely by paying the City
В bicycle parking in-lieu fee in an amount established by separate ordinance to
be used by the City to install bicycle parking and associated improvements in
the right-of-way . The in-lieu fee may not be used if there are vehicular use
areas , plazas, exterior courtyards, or other open areas on the site , other
than required landscaping , large enough, separately or in combination, to
accommodate all or a portion of the required bicycle parking.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В When there are existing bicycle racks in the right-of-way ,
the PDSD Director may allow a modification to the required number of bicycle
parking spaces based on a finding from the City вҖҷs Bicycle Coordinator that the
number of existing racks will adequately serve the proposed use and other
nearby uses the racks currently serve.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В When the requirements of this Section are required due to an
expansion in accordance with Section
7.4.3.E, Expansions, the PDSD Director  may grant a modification to the
required number of bicycle parking spaces  based on a finding from the City вҖҷs
Bicycle  Coordinator or designee that a reasonable attempt has been made to
provide all or a portion of the required bicycle parking in a manner that that
the bicycle  parking does not create a safety hazard for pedestrians, cyclists,
and motorists and is clearly visible from adjacent В sidewalks , drives, and/or
public entrances .

7.4.9.В В В BICYCLE PARKING DESIGN CRITERIA
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В These standards ensure that required bicycle parking is designed so that
bicycles may be securely locked without undue inconvenience and will be
reasonably safeguarded from intentional or accidental damage.
В В В B.В В В General Criteria
В В В В В В 1.В В В Parking and Maneuvering
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Bicycle parking may not impede on-site pedestrian access. A
clearance space of at least four feet in width must be provided for pedestrian
access.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Each bicycle parking space must be accessible without moving
another bicycle.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Adequate maneuvering space must be provided behind all outdoor
bicycle parking facilities .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The bicycleВ parking area must be hard surfaced and maintained in
a smooth, durable, and well-drained condition. Stabilized decomposed granite is
an acceptable surface material for bicycleВ parking areas .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Outdoor bicycleВ parking areas must be lighted so that they are
thoroughly illuminated and visible from adjacent В sidewalks , parking lots , or
buildings during hours of use.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Bicycle parking facilities will be maintained in good condition
and kept clear of trash and debris.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Vehicular Use Areas . Short- and long-term bicycle parking are
permitted in vehicular use areas provided the parking area is separated from
vehicular parking and drive areas by a barrier or is located a sufficient
distance from vehicular uses areas to prevent damage to the parked bicycles.
Examples of acceptable barriers include curbs, bollards, concrete planters,
landscape buffers, or other suitable barrier devices. Striping in combination
with other barrier devices is permitted.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Bicycle Racks
В В В В В В Bicycle racks must comply with all of the following criteria: (See
Figures 7.4.9-B and C for illustrative examples of these criteria.)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Bicycle racks must be securely anchored to the ground, floor,
wall, or ceiling;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The bicycle frame and one wheel can be locked to the rack with a
high security, U-shaped lock if both wheels are left on the bicycle;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A bicycle six feet long can be securely held with its frame
supported so that the bicycle cannot be pushed or fall in a manner that will
damage the wheels or components;
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A single rack is designed and located to accommodate two
bicycles;
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Racks must not have sharp edges that can be hazardous to
pedestrians, particularly individuals with visual disabilities. Artistic
bicycle racks are acceptable provided they meet the criteria herein;
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Each required short-term bicycle parking space must be at least
two feet by six feet;
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В A bicycle rack must be a minimum of two and one half feet from a
wall or other obstruction; and,
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В An access aisle at least five feet wide must be provided between
two rows of bicycle parking. The aisle width is measured between the lengthwise
dimensions (i.e., six feet) of the bicycle parking spaces between the two rows.
В В В C.В В В Short-Term Bicycle Parking
В В В The purpose of short-term bicycle parking is to encourage shoppers,
customers, messengers, and other visitors to use bicycles by providing a
convenient and readily accessible place to park bicycles.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Short-term bicycle parking must be provided in racks per Section
7.4.9.B.2 or lockers per Section
7.4.9.D.2.d.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Location
В В В В В В Short-term bicycle parking must be:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Within 50 feet of each public entrance to a building as measured
along the most direct pedestrian access route (See
Figure 7.4.9-A below).
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3074.jpg]
Figure 7.4.9-A: Bicycle Parking Location
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Exception
В В В В В В В В В В В В The PDSD Director may allow short-term bicycle parking to be more
than 50 feet from a public entrance (s) based on a finding from the City of
TucsonвҖҷs Bicycle Coordinator or designee that the proposed location is
consistent with best practices pertaining to siting short-term bicycle racks,
particularly in regards to visibility, security, and convenience for
bicyclists.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Located outside the building (s);
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Clearly visible from the adjacent В sidewalks , drives, and/or a
public entrance (s); and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Where buildings have more than one public entrance or a site has
more than one building , short-term bicycle parking must be distributed so that
at least one short-term bicycle parking space is within 50 feet of each public
entrance .
В В В D.В В В Long-term Bicycle Parking
В В В The purpose of long-term bicycle parking is to provide employees, students,
residents, commuters and others who generally stay at a site for several hours,
a secure and weather-protected place to park bicycles. Although long-term
parking does not have to be provided on-site , the intent of these standards is
to allow bicycle parking to be within a reasonable distance in order to
encourage bicycle use. Long-term bicycle parking facilities must comply with
the following criteria:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Location
В В В В В В Long-term bicycle parking must be located on-site or offsite within 300
feet of the building . Long-term bicycle parking for multiple or mixed use
В developments and shopping centers should be distributed proportionately among
the uses;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Security
В В В В В В To provide security, long-term bicycle parking must have controlled
access. Examples include, but are not limited to:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Inside residential units;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Inside buildings provided the bicycle parking does not create a
safety hazard or impede pedestrian circulation and in an area that is visible
from employee work areas or in a locked room;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В In a bicycle room or an area enclosed by a fence that is eight
feet high or connected floor-to-ceiling accessed by key, smartcard, or other
secure method; or,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В In bicycle lockers that fully enclose the bicycle, resist
tampering, are securely anchored, and constructed of durable materials, such
as, but not limited to, steel. These lockers may be leased (keyed or smartcard)
lockers or on-demand lockers (self-lock or smartcard) lockers.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Weather Protection
В В В В В В Long-term bicycle parking must be covered to provide weather protection
and may be provided inside buildings , under roof overhangs or awnings, in
bicycle lockers , or within or under other structures . Where long-term bicycle
parking is not within a building or locker, the cover must be permanent and at
least seven feet above the floor or ground.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Signage
В В В В В В Where long-term bicycle parking is not within a building and a key, code,
or secure method is needed to access the parking facility, a sign must be
provided instructing cyclists how access may be obtained.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Lighting
В В В В В В Long-term bicycle parking not within a building must be lit by overhead
lighting.
В В В E.В В В Bicycle Parking Figures
В В В В В В 1.В В В Examples of Acceptable Bicycle Rack Design
В В В В В В The following bicycle racks comply with the criteria of Section
7.4.9.B.2 and are provided for illustrative purposes only. Other bicycle rack
designs may be used provided they comply with Section
7.4.9.B.2.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3076.jpg]
Figure 7.4.9-B: Bike Rack Design
В В В В В В 2.В В В Examples of Bicycle Parking Layouts
В В В В В В The following bicycle parking layouts comply with the criteria of Section
7.4.9.B.2 and are provided for illustrative purposes only. Other layouts may be
used provided they comply with Section
7.4.9.B.2.
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-3078.jpg]
Figure 7.4.9-C: Bicycle Parking Layouts
7.4.10.В В В PARKING DESIGN MODIFICATION REQUEST (PDMR)
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В This section is established to provide an administrative process through
which specific parking design regulations of the UDC may be modified. This
procedure is not intended to delete or waive UDC regulations, but is intended
to allow design flexibility in UDC compliance and alternative design solutions
within the intent of the regulation.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В The following requirements may be considered for a modification under this
section:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Number of motor vehicle parking spaces ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Dimensional and location requirements of Section
7.4.6 (Motor Vehicle Use Area Design Criteria) including, but not limited to
off-site  parking location, parking space width, access lane  and PAAL width,
and back-up spur depth; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Number of bicycle parking spaces .
В В В C.В В В Review and Approval Procedures
В В В В В В 1.В В В Applications must include property ownership information, a site
plan , and other information deemed by the PDSD Director as necessary to
evaluate the request.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Review of PDMR requests is processed in accordance with Section
3.3.3, PDSD Director Approval Procedure.
В В В D.В В В Findings for Approval
В В В The PDSD Director may approve a modification as provided by this section if
all of the following applicable criteria are met and documented in findings.
В В В В В В 1.В В В The modification does not result in the deletion of an UDC
requirement.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The modification is not a request previously denied as a variance.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The modification is not to a condition of approval for a rezoning,
Special Exception Land Use , variance, or Individual Parking Plan.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The modification does not adversely impact adjacent properties or
development .
В В В В В В 5.В В В The modification does not impede sight visibility at points of
ingress into, egress from , or within the vehicular use area for either
vehicular or pedestrian traffic or otherwise create or increase a safety
hazard.
В В В В В В 6.В В В The modification provides design alternatives to better integrate
the development into the design character of the immediate neighborhood.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Except as provided below, a PDMR may modify a dimensional
requirement by no more than 10%.
В В В В В В 8.В В В A modification may be granted for decreasing the required motor
vehicle parking by 20% or less, but in no case more than five spaces. The
modification in off-street В motor vehicle parking spaces is for the purpose of
improving the site design in a manner which will further the intent of the
PDMR. This includes improvements such as, but not limited to, enhancement of
landscaping , pedestrian facilities, or bicycle provisions beyond the
requirements of the UDC .
В В В В В В 9.В В В The modification does not decrease the minimum parking space
dimension to less than eight feet in width. The number of parking spaces with
decreased dimensions may not exceed 10% of the total number of parking spaces
provided. The parking spaces with decreased dimensions must be marked as
вҖҳcompactвҖҷ.
В В В В В В 10.В В В The modification does not decrease the parking area access lane or
driveway dimension by more than 5% of the minimum width required.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exceptions:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В In instances when the access is a fire lane, the width of a
parking area access lane or driveway may not be reduced.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The parking area access lane or driveway may be decreased by
more than 5% for residential development of five units or less and with
approval of Tucson Fire
В В В В В В 11.В В В The back-up spur dimensional requirements may be modified when the
last space in a bay of parking is wider than eight and one-half feet or when
the PDSD Director determines that the proposed modification will not create a
safety hazard.
В В В В В В 12.В В В A modification may be granted for decreasing the required bicycle
parking by 10% or less, but in no case shall the modification result in there
being less than two bicycle parking spaces . The modification in the number of
bicycle parking spaces is for the purpose of improving the site design in a
manner which will further the intent of the PDMR. This includes improvements
such as, but not limited to, enhancement of landscaping or pedestrian
facilities beyond the requirements of the UDC .
(Am. Ord. 11732, 2/19/2020; Am. Ord. 11996, 3/21/2023)
7.4.11.В В В REQUIRED NUMBER OF ELECTRIC VEHICLE SUPPLY EQUIPMENT (EVSE).
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В The purpose of this section is to encourage the use of electric vehicles
by providing equitable and convenient access to electric vehicle charging
infrastructure. These regulations support the City's climate strategy to reduce
greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector by ensuring that the
necessary facilities are in place to accommodate electric vehicles.
В В В B.В В В Minimum Required EVSE В 
В В В В В В 1.В В В The percentage of total required motor vehicle parking spaces that
must also have access to at least Level 2 EVSE for specific Land Use Group,
Class and Types are listed in
Table 7.4.11-1 below. The required EVSE parking spaces shall be based upon the
total number of parking spaces provided following any approved parking
reductions or modifications.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Four percent (e.g., one in 25 spaces) but no less than one of
electric vehicle (EV) charging spaces, in any given parking facility, must meet
accessible parking space design standards. Such spaces shall not serve to meet
the required number of accessible parking spaces required per the International
Building Code.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Retail Trade Uses and the following Commercial Use Classes:
Alcoholic Beverage Service, Commercial Recreation, Entertainment, Food Service,
Personal Services and Technical Services, with less than 50 total required
motor vehicle parking spaces prior to any reduction are exempt from Minimum
Required EVSE .
В В В В В В 4.В В В The number of total EVSE required parking spaces may be reduced by
installing Level 3 charging stations. Each installed Level 3 charging station
meets the requirement for three EV charging spaces.
В В В В В В 5.В В В In the case of a proposed expansion on an existing site, the EVSE
requirements shall be based upon the number of parking spaces provided as part
of the expansion and not the total number of parking spaces provided on site.

(Ord. 11953, 8/23/2022; Am. Ord. 12152, 3/18/2025)
7.4.12.В В В ELECTRIC VEHICLE PARKING DESIGN CRITERIA
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В These standards ensure that required electric vehicle charging spaces are
designed so that electric vehicle drivers may easily find and utilize EVSE
without undue inconvenience.
В В В B.В В В General Criteria
В В В В В В 1.В В В EVSE Siting
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В When siting EVSE , the charging equipment must not interfere with
other uses and access on site.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В EVSE shall be installed so that EV charging is feasible in the
number of spaces required.
В В В C.В В В EV Installed В 
В В В В В В The purpose of EV Installed В EVSE is to provide easy access for EV
charging to a wide range of the general public. This is the most visible and
significant investment in EVSE infrastructure. The installation of a charging
station allows an EV driver to charge a vehicle without additional equipment.
The installation of a networked station allows for managed charging access,
monitoring, billing, and remotely accessed updates for the user and property
manager.
В В В В В В 1.В В В EV Installed Stations must be securely anchored to the ground,
floor, wall, or ceiling.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Stations shall be installed to minimize tripping hazards and draping
of the charging cord.
В В В В В В 3.В В В EV parking spaces with access to installed stations shall be
identified with signage and pavement markings including an EV symbol and
regulatory instructions such as "No Parking Except for Electric Vehicle
Charging."
В В В В В В 4.В В В Installed EVSE stations shall be accessible for EV charging during
business hours at a minimum.
В В В D.В В В EV Ready В В 
В В В В В В The purpose of EV Ready В EVSE is to provide lower cost ready access to EV
charging in locations with familiar or semi-regular users. Location-specific
management strategies can be utilized to monitor or bill for charging access.
Users may be required to provide their own charging cables to connect to an
available outlet, or management may opt to provide this component. EV Ready
spaces provide significant cost savings over retrofits if they are upgraded to
EV Installed stations in the future.
В В В В В В 1.В В В The outlet near the EV Ready parking space shall be permanently and
visibly marked as "EV Ready ."
В В В В В В 2.В В В This marking shall be changed if upgraded to EV Installed .
В В В В В В 3.В В В In the service panel or subpanel directory, the branch circuit shall
be identified as "EV Ready ."
В В В В В В 4.В В В Information for how to access and charge at EV Ready parking spaces
through site- specific management plans shall be clearly visible in the parking
lot.
В В В E.В В В EV Capable В 
В В В В В В The purpose of EV Capable conduit is to provide cost effective 'future
proofing' for future EV charging at a site.
В В В В В В 1.В В В The raceway termination location near the EV Capable parking space
shall be permanently and visibly marked as "EV Capable ."
В В В В В В 2.В В В This marking shall be changed if upgraded to EV Ready or EV
Installed.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The service panel or subpanel circuit directory shall identify the
overcurrent protective device space(s) reserved for future electrical vehicle
charging as "EV Capable ".
(Ord. 11953, 8/23/2022)
7.5.В В В OFF-STREET LOADING
7.5.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В This section establishes requirements for the safe receipt or distribution
of materials or merchandise by vehicle to provide for:
В В В A.В В В An off-street space or berth for temporary parking of a vehicle while
loading or unloading merchandise, cargo, or materials;
В В В B.В В В Expediting moving traffic and lessening street congestion by minimizing
traffic conflicts between streets and the loading areas for various land use
activities;
В В В C.В В В Adequate loading areas for new land uses and for expansion of existing
land uses ;
В В В D.В В В A safer pedestrian environment by providing separate and distinct
spaces from the pedestrian areas for loading areas ;
В В В E.В В В Safe, convenient, and efficient ingress-egress for loading areas ; and,
В В В F.В В В Safe and efficient on-site vehicular circulation systems by providing
separate loading areas from the vehicular traffic lanes.
7.5.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The provisions of this section apply to:
В В В A.В В В New development ;
В В В B.В В В Changes of use locating in existing development , as required in
Section
7.5.3.D, Changes of Use.
В В В C.В В В Any expansion of an existing land use or any addition of a new use to
an existing development , as required in Section
7.5.3.E, Expansions.
7.5.3.В В В GENERAL PROVISIONS
В В В A.В В В Land Use Groups and Land Use Classes
В В В The specific Land Use Groups and Land Use Classes listed in Sec
7.5.5, Required Loading Areas, are defined in Article 11, Definitions and Rules
of Construction.
В В В B.В В В Calculations for Loading Areas
В В В The calculation for the required number of loading areas is based on the
proposed land use and the size of the project as provided in Section
7.5.5, Required Loading Areas. The size of the project  is the gross floor area
of the use, including any outdoor area dedicated  to the use, but excluding
vehicular use areas
В В В C.В В В Change of Approved Vehicular Use Area
В В В Whenever zoning approval has been granted for a vehicular use area which
includes loading areas , the approval is conditioned upon the continued
compliance with the provisions contained in the approved plan. Any change to
the vehicular use area as shown on the approved plan must comply with the
requirements of this section and be reapproved.
В В В D.В В В Changes of Use
В В В Whenever the use of an existing development is changed to a different use
which requires a loading area or loading areas of greater size under this
section than were required for the prior use, additional loading areas or a
loading area of greater size, whichever is applicable, must be provided.
В В В E.В В В Expansions
В В В Any expansion of an existing use or any addition of a new use to an existing
development which results in an expansion in gross floor area is subject to the
following requirements:
В В В В В В 1.В В В If an expansion or a series of expansions cumulatively results in
less than a 25% expansion in gross floor area , the requirements of this
section apply only to the expanded use.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If an expansion or a series of expansions cumulatively results in a
25% or greater expansion in gross floor area , the requirements of this section
apply to the entire site .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Expansions are cumulated over time from April 1, 1969. Once a
development is brought into conformance with the requirements of this section,
subsequent expansions will begin accumulating as of the date the development
was brought into conformance .
7.5.4.В В В DESIGN CRITERIA
В В В All loading areas , including any non-required loading areas , must comply
with the following requirements.
В В В A.В В В Location and Use Requirements
В В В The location and use of a loading area are as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В A loading area must be located on the same site or lot as the use
they serve.
В В В В В В 2.В В В A loading area required for a billboard must be located immediately
adjacent to the billboard and outside the area defined by a vertical line
intersecting the ground and any structural element extending from the
billboard, including, but not limited to, overhangs, cantilevered beams, and
elevated walkways.
В В В В В В 3.В В В A loading area may be co-located within the approach area for
dumpster containers and the stacking spaces of drive-through aisles when safety
and access issues are appropriately addressed and delivery and solid waste
collection schedules are coordinated so as not to conflict with one another.
В В В B.В В В Access Requirements
В В В The access requirements to a loading space are as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В A street may be used for access and maneuvering into and out of a
loading area provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The street is used for access to only one loading area on that
site .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The street is not shown on the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R)
Plan .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The street is not a local residential street .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The street does not abut a residential zone within the same block
.
В В В В В В 2.В В В An alley may be used for access and maneuvering into and out of a
loading area provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The alley is used for access to only one loading area on that
site .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The alley is a minimum 20 feet wide.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The alley is surfaced in a manner acceptable to the City
Engineer.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The alley does not abut a residential zone within the same block
.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The maneuvering area must be designed so that there is safe access
into and out of a loading area . The size and configuration of the maneuvering
area must be based on AASHTO (American Association of State Highway and
Transportation Officials) standards for turning radii and distances required of
the vehicles for which the loading area is designed.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The access route to a loading area must have an overhead clearance
of 15 feet.
В В В C.В В В Screening and Landscaping Requirements
В В В The screening and landscaping of a loading area must be in accordance with
Section
7.6, Landscaping and Screening Regulations.
В В В D.В В В Lighting Requirements
В В В The lighting of a loading area , if present, must be in conformance with
adopted City of Tucson Outdoor Lighting regulations.
В В В E.В В В Surfacing Requirements
В В В The surfacing of a loading area must be in accordance with Section
7.4.6.I, Surfacing Requirements.
В В В F.В В В Striping Requirements
В В В A loading area must be striped in accordance with Section
7.4.6.G, Striping, Surfacing Requirements, to distinguish it from motor vehicle
parking spaces  and other uses on the site .
В В В G.В В В Dimensional Requirements
В В В The minimum size of a loading area , exclusive of access and maneuvering
area, must be:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Type A: 12 feet by 35 feet;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Type B: 12 feet by 55 feet; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Overhead Clearance is 15 feet.
7.5.5.В В В REQUIRED LOADING AREAS
В В В The number of loading areas listed for each Land Use Group is applicable for
all Land Use Classes within that Land Use Group, unless a Land Use Class is
listed specifically stating otherwise.
