7.6.В В В LANDSCAPING AND SCREENING
7.6.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В The purpose of this section is to establish standards for the installation
and maintenance of landscaping and screening according to recognized xeriscape
principles and to provide for the protection of native vegetation . The
standards are intended to accomplish the following:
В В В A.В В В Implement Mayor and Council policy to conserve energy, water, and other
natural resources through the use of xeriscape В landscaping principles; to
promote air quality; to improve community aesthetics; and to protect the public
health, safety, and general welfare as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Help achieve City water conservation goals through the use of
drought-tolerant plantings and xeriscape principles in landscape design;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Reduce air pollution and dust by encouraging the use of vegetation
for air filtration and absorption of carbon dioxide and production of oxygen;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Reduce the heat and glare radiated by the built environment;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Reduce soil erosion by slowing storm water runoff;
В В В В В В 5.В В В Assist in ground water recharge; and,
В В В В В В 6.В В В Limit the use of allergenic, pollen-producing plants.
В В В B.В В В Establish or retain a neighborhood character by providing design
standards to:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Buffer the potential negative effects that more intensive land uses
may have upon adjacent В land uses ; and,
В В В В В В 2.В В В Where development is subject to neighborhood or area plan standards,
incorporate the adopted landscape policies of neighborhood or area plans to the
extent they are consistent with the provisions of this section.
В В В C.В В В Contribute to and enhance the economic welfare of the City and the
quality of life of citizens and visitors through the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Promote the image of the southwestern desert environment;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Create an attractive appearance along City В streets ; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Carry out the intent of design standards for development along
Scenic Routes and Gateway Routes .
7.6.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The provisions of this section apply to the following:
В В В A.В В В All new development ; and,
В В В B.В В В Expansion of existing development , as provided below:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Buildings Greater Than 10,000 Square Feet
В В В В В В On sites where the gross floor area of the existing building (s) is more
than 10,000 square feet, expansion in square footage of land area, floor area ,
lot coverage , or vehicular use area as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the expansion is less than 25%, the standards of this section
apply only to the proposed expansion. Existing development on the site is
subject to the zoning standards in effect at the time the existing development
received zoning approval.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the expansion is 25% or greater or if expansions as of
February 15, 1991, cumulatively result in a 25% or greater expansion in land
area, floor area , lot coverage , or vehicular use area , the standards of this
section apply to the entire site .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Buildings 10,000 Square Feet or Less
В В В В В В On sites where the gross floor area of the existing building (s) is
10,000 square feet or less, expansions in square footage of land area, floor
area , lot coverage , or vehicular use area as follows.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В If the expansion is less than 50%, the standards of this section
apply only to the proposed expansion. Existing development on the site is
subject to the zoning standards in effect at the time the existing development
received zoning approval.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If the expansion is 50% or greater or if expansions as of
February 15, 1991, cumulatively result in a 50% or greater expansion in land
area, floor area , lot coverage , or vehicular use area , the standards of this
section apply to the entire site .
7.6.3.В В В EXCEPTIONS
В В В The provisions of this section do not apply to the following.
В В В A.В В В Single-family В dwelling units  or duplexes on separate lots , prior to
January, 01, 2026 except commonly owned areas in SectionВ 
8.7.3 , Flexible Lot Developments (FLD); or,
В В В B.В В В Approved plats and plans developed in the City prior to February 17,
1991 if:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The site is developed in accordance with the approved plat or plan;
and,
В В В В В В 2.В В В Development complies with the standards of Section
7.6.4.A, Use of Drought-Tolerant Vegetation.
В В В C.В В В Residential development with four or fewer units per site is exempt
from Sections
7.6.4-
7.6.6.
(Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
7.6.4.В В В LANDSCAPE STANDARDS
В В В A.В В В Use of Drought-Tolerant Vegetation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Except as otherwise provided by this section, all plant material
used for landscaping must be selected from the adopted plant list located in
Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Areas of reseeding or hydroseeding after grading are subject to the
Native Seed List standards of Section
5-02.6.0, Exhibit I, of the Technical Standards Manual.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Plants not listed in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual cannot be used within the Scenic
Corridor Zone (SZC) where the use of native vegetation is required in the
buffer area adjacent to a Scenic Route in accordance with Section
5.3.4.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Plants not listed in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual may be used as follows:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Existing Plants
В В В В В В В В В Existing trees and shrubs may be incorporated in a landscaped area if
their locations do not conflict with the plant location standards of this
section;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Protected Riparian Areas
В В В В В В В В В Within Protected Riparian Areas pursuant to an approved mitigation or
restoration plan; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Oasis Allowance
В В В В В В В В В An oasis is an area where non-drought tolerant landscaping designs are
permitted. Plants not listed in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual may be used in an oasis if the
plants are grouped in separately programmed irrigation areas according to their
water requirements. The location and maximum area on a site that may be used
for oasis areas, including those located in a street landscape border , is
determined as follows.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Multifamily Residential Uses
В В В В В В В В В В В В Up to 5% of the site , or one 100 square feet per dwelling unit ,
or 8% of the required open space , whichever is greater.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В All Other Uses
В В В В В В В В В В В В Equal to, but not more than, 2.5% of the site .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Locational Standards
В В В В В В В В В В В В The following factors should be considered when determining the
location of the oasis area.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В Providing optimum exposure for site users by selecting
areas near main buildings , pedestrian facilities, and active use areas; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Selecting areas that incorporate outdoor seating or
assembly spaces.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Street Landscape Border
В В В В В В В В В В В В Oasis areas may be located in the street landscape border only if:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В The oasis areas do not total more than 5% of the area of
the street landscape border ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В The non-drought tolerant plants used in the oasis area are
flowering bedding plants.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Turf Areas of 10 Acres or Greater
В В В В В В В В В В В В Turf areas of ten acres or more are regulated by the state . The
Arizona Department of Water Resources shall be notified of cases where proposed
turf or other high water uses exceed ten acres .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Exceptions for Oasis Limitations
В В В В В В В В В В В В The oasis area limitations in Section
7.6.4.A.4.c and turf area restrictions in this section do not apply to:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В Public parks and botanical gardens ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Outdoor recreation facilities , whether under public or
private ownership, for public use, schools, day care centers;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В The playing areas of golf courses;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (d)В В В Cemeteries; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (e)В В В Mobile home spaces within mobile home parks.
В В В B.В В В Vehicular Use Areas
В В В The standards in this Section
7.6.4.B apply to all developments that provide more than four motor vehicle
parking spaces , except as provided in Section
7.6.4.B.1.c, Exemptions.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Canopy Trees in Vehicular Use Areas
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В General Standards
В В В В В В В В В Within a vehicular use area , one canopy tree is required for each
four motor vehicle parking spaces or fraction thereof.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The canopy trees must be evenly distributed throughout the
vehicular use area . Every parking space must be located within 40 feet of the
trunk of a canopy tree (as measured from the center of the tree trunk).
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Fifty percent of the trees required for landscape borders
located within ten feet of the paved portion(s) of a vehicular use area may be
counted towards both the minimum parking lot В canopy tree standard and the
landscape border canopy tree standard.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В An unpaved planting area, which must be a minimum of 34
square feet in area and four feet in width, must be provided for each canopy
tree .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В In development where it is necessary to bring the existing
vehicular use areas into conformance with these regulations, gaps are allowed
in the tree coverage requirement of even distribution, provided that at least
the minimum required number of trees is placed within or adjacent to the
vehicular use area with coverage which is as uniform as possible. Canopy trees
В adjacent to the vehicular use area may count toward meeting this requirement,
provided the trees meet the criteria listed in Section
7.6.4.B.1.a(1).
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Alternative Standard
В В В В В В В В В In lieu of the number of canopy trees required by subsection a. above,
the applicant may provide the number of canopy trees needed to create a shade
pattern caused by the trees at maturity and buildings that covers 50% of the
paved area within the vehicular use area from 9:20 a.m. to 3:20 p.m. Mountain
Standard Time on June 21st.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Exemptions
В В В В В В В В В The following are exempt from Section
7.6.4.B.1.a, General Standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Duplexes on individual lots with four or fewer spaces
provided;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Home occupations ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Mobile home dwellings;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Non-residential uses within contributing properties to a
National Register Historic District are exempt from Section
7.6.4.B;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Residential Care Services with four or fewer spaces provided;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Single-family dwellings;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Vehicle storage ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Structurally covered or underground garages ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (9)В В В For expansion of an existing development that is located on a
lot of record on February 17, 1991, that is 10,000 square feet or less in size,
the existing vehicular use areas are exempt from the canopy tree standard, if
the existing vehicular use area is in compliance with the zoning standards in
effect at the time the existing vehicular use area was developed; or,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (10)В В В For expansion of an existing development , the existing
vehicular use areas are exempt from the canopy tree standard if the existing
vehicular use area is subject to site plans approved between February 15, 1991,
and August 2, 2004.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Plant Protection
В В В В В В Areas where plants are susceptible to injury by vehicular or pedestrian
traffic must be protected by appropriate means, such as curbs, bollards, or low
walls.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Planter Area
В В В В В В For each tree required by this code, a planter area with a minimum
unpaved area of 34 square feet and a four-foot minimum width is required.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The planter area must have a raised border four inches high to
prohibit the tires of the vehicle from encroaching onto the planter. Standard
wheel barriers are acceptable but not encouraged, as they can be easily moved
and could allow damage to the tree.
В В В C.В В В Landscape Borders
В В В There are two types of landscape borders: street landscape borders and
interior landscape borders .
В В В В В В 1.В В В All Landscape Borders
В В В В В В The following apply to all landscape borders.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В One canopy tree is required for every 33 linear feet of landscape
border or fraction thereof, excluding vehicular ingress or egress points.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A minimum of one canopy tree is required within a required
landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Trees may be planted at varying distances apart.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Street Landscape Borders
В В В В В В To enhance the visual appearance of the streetscape, a street landscape
border is required in accordance with
Table 7.6.4-1 along the street В frontage of a site as follows, except as
provided in Section
7.6.4.C.4, Exceptions to the Landscape Border Standards:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Minimum Width
В В В В В В В В В Street landscape borders must be a minimum of ten feet wide as
measured from the street В property line . On streets designated as Major
Streets and Routes (MS&R), the street landscape border must be measured from
the future MS&R right-of-way line as determined by Section
5.4.4, Establishment of MS&R Right-of-Way Lines and Gateway Routes.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Residential Subdivisions
В В В В В В В В В Street landscape borders for residential subdivisions of eight or more
lots shall conform to the following standards.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Street landscape borders are only required along the exterior
boundaries of subdivisions . Landscape borders are not required along front
yard street frontages.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Walls, fences, or other screening must be placed behind the
landscape border.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The landscape border must be recorded as common area and
maintained by the homeowners association (HOA) for the subdivision . The
subdivision CC&Rs shall reference and require compliance with the maintenance
standards in Section
7.6.9, Maintenance. The PDSD Director  may allow the recording of a public use
easement with the subdivision В plat  in cases where the standards of this
section are the only reason for the creation of an HOA. The public use easement
shall require the abutting  property owner to install and maintain a landscape
border in accordance with the standards in this section.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Street landscape borders fronting on local streets may be
reduced to a minimum of five feet.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Located on Site
В В В В В В В В В Street landscape borders must be located entirely on site , except
that, if approved by the City Engineer or designee, up to five feet of the
required ten foot width may be placed within the adjacent В right-of-way area or
within the Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) right-of-way area on MS&R streets .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Inorganic Ground Cover
В В В В В В В В В Except as otherwise provided by this section, the area between the
right-of-way line and sidewalk and the area between the sidewalk and the curb,
if not covered with vegetation, must be covered with an appropriate inorganic
ground cover, such as decomposed granite.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Vegetative Ground Cover
В В В В В В В В В Fifty percent or more of the area of the street landscape border must
be covered with shrubs or vegetative ground cover. The required ground coverage
must be achieved within two years from the date of planting.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Scenic Route
В В В В В В В В В Street landscape borders on property with street В frontage on a
designated Scenic Route are subject to the standards of Section
5.3.4,В Scenic Route Buffer Area. A buffer area in accordance with Section
5.3.4, Scenic Route Buffer Area, is required on property with street В frontage
on a designated Scenic Route .
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Landscaping on Adjacent В Sites
В В В В В В В В В Existing drought tolerant vegetation on adjacent В sites must be
considered in design to prevent abrupt changes in plant types and to maintain a
visual continuity along street frontages.
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Structural Overhangs
В В В В В В В В В Covered parking canopies or other structural canopies, such as those
used in service stations, may not overhang into street landscape borders in
order to avoid conflicts between the structures and crowns of trees.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Interior Landscape Borders
В В В В В В Interior landscape borders are required as a buffer and visual transition
along the common property lines between adjacent В land uses and zones as
follows, except as provided in Section
7.6.4.C.4, Exceptions to the Landscape Border Standards.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Interior landscape borders are required as determined in
Table 7.6.4-1, which ranks land uses and zones based upon their land use
intensity and the impact a use will have on adjacent В land uses .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The minimum required width of an interior landscape border is the
lesser of ten feet or the width of the building setback required by the UDC for
the proposed building or use.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Where motor vehicle parking spaces or parking area access lanes
(PAALs) are located next to the property line or where the interior landscape
border conflicts with a utility easement, an interior landscape border is not
be required if all of the following requirements are met:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В An equivalent number of trees are planted elsewhere on the
site between the building (s) and the property line , or if the use on the site
does not include buildings , the required canopy trees are located between the
principal use and the property line ;
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The trees are evenly distributed over the site ; and
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В The minimum planting area required in Section
7.6.4.B.3, Planter Area, is provided for each canopy tree .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Detention/retention facilities may be incorporated into the
interior landscape border if they are designed in accordance with Section
7.6.6.C.1, Storm Water Runoff.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Exceptions to Landscape Border Standards
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Downtown Parking District
В В В В В В В В В Within the Downtown Parking District as defined in Section
11.4.5, Definitions - D, the standards for landscape borders are as provided as
follows.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В A street landscape border , measured from the development
side of the future sidewalk location, must be maintained along all public
right-of-way frontages of vehicular use areas and parking structures .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В A street landscape border is not be required if the ground
level story of a building containing a parking structure is not used for motor
vehicle parking or if the parking structure is completely screened and enclosed
by a masonry wall.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В An interior landscape border is required only if the adjacent
В zone or land use is residential.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Expansions
В В В В В В В В В For expansions of an existing development located on a lot of record
on February 17, 1991, 10,000 square feet or less in size, the development is
subject only to the following landscape border standards.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В On streets other than a Scenic or Gateway Route (refer to
Sections
5.3 and
5.5), the width of a street landscape border must be a minimum of five feet.
With the permission of the City Engineer or designee, up to one-half the street
landscape border width may be located in the public right-of-way or in the
Major Streets and Routes (MS&R) right-of-way area .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Interior landscape borders are not be required in existing
vehicular use areas , if the existing vehicular use area was developed in
compliance with the zoning standards in effect at the time of development .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Interior Landscape Border
В В В В В В В В В Interior landscape borders are not required as follows.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Around interior pads within a site or between adjacent В sites
with common driveways , shared motor vehicle parking, or vehicular access
easements or between two adjacent nonresidential uses of equal intensity as
determined by
Table 7.6.4-I.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Where an alley , drainageway, or other right-of-way ten feet
or more in width physically separates the site from an adjacent property.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Where, prior to February 15, 1991, an open space area was
provided as a buffer between the site and an adjacent less intensive use or
zone , if the open space area is:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В At least ten feet wide; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Restricted in perpetuity to natural or landscaped open
space use through dedication to the public, deed restriction, or covenant
running with the land.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Between two similar uses, whether or not the uses are within
the same zoning classification.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Street Landscape Border
В В В В В В В В В Street landscape borders are not required along street frontages where
the landscaping standards of Section
5.3.4, Scenic Route Buffer Area, apply.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Historic Preservation Zone (HPZ)
В В В В В В В В В The Planning and Development Services Department Director may grant a
complete or partial exception to the landscape border standards for development
within a HPZ. The exceptions may be granted if, after completion of the HPZ
development review required by the HPZ, the PDSD Director determines that the
standards are not compatible with the character and design elements of the HPZ.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Planned Area Development (PAD)
В В В В В В В В В For development В adjacent to a Planned Area Development (PAD) zone ,
the landscaping standards are those which are required for development
В adjacent to residential zones , except where an adjacent PAD District has been
developed or planned for nonresidential uses. In that case, the landscaping
standards are based on the adjacent В land use.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Community Garden and Urban Farm Uses are exempt from the
landscape border requirements of Section
7.6.4.C, Landscape Borders.

В 
В В В D.В В В Use of Turf
В В В В В В В В В 1.В В В Installation of ornamental turf in commercial, industrial, and
institutional developments, and common areas of residential development is
prohibited.
В В В В В В В В В 2.В В В Ornamental turf is defined as:
В В В В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Grass areas with any single dimension of eight feet or less.
В В В В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Grass areas exceeding a 4:1, or 25%, slope.
В В В В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Grass areas that are not accessible by paved pathways and/or
are restricted by physical barriers that prohibit accessibility.
В В В В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Grass areas installed closer than ten feet to a street and/or
in front entryways to residential neighborhoods or subdivisions unless
associated with active recreational activities.
В В В В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Grass areas that are not utilized for active recreational
purposes.
В В В В В В В В В 3.В В В Except as provided by Section
7.6.4.A.4.c(6), Exceptions for Oasis Limitations, turf  areas that do not meet
any of the criteria provided in Section
7.6.4.D.2 are allowed only within an oasis and may only be 50% of the total
oasis allowance .
В В В E.В В В Plant Cover/Dust Control
В В В All disturbed, grubbed, graded, or bladed areas not otherwise improved must
be landscaped, reseeded, or treated with a layer of inorganic or organic ground
cover to help reduce dust pollution.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Ground surfaces in planting beds, planters, medians, or tree
understory within a landscaped area that are not covered with shrubs, accent
plants, vines, ground cover, or other vegetation from the listed in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual must be treated with an inorganic
ground cover.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Unless maintained as undisturbed natural desert , all portions of a
site not occupied by buildings , structures , vehicular use areas , oasis
areas, pedestrian circulation areas, or required landscape elements must be
landscaped with vegetation from the list located in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual, reseeded with a native seed mix, or
treated with an inorganic ground cover and maintained in a clean condition. All
portions of a site including common areas, natural areas, and revegetated or
landscaped areas are to continuously be maintained to remove populations of
buffelgrass (Pennisetum ciliare).
В В В В В В 3.В В В Unless maintained as undisturbed natural desert , future building
pads within a phased development must be temporarily landscaped with vegetation
from the list located in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual, reseeded with a native seed mix, or
treated with an appropriate inorganic ground cover and maintained in a clean
condition. All portions of a site including common areas, natural areas, and
revegetated or landscaped areas are to continuously be maintained to remove
populations of buffelgrass (Pennisetum ciliare).
В В В В В В 4.В В В If vegetative ground cover is not intended, a minimum two-inch layer
of organic or inorganic material (i.e., decomposed granite, rock mulch, or
other material) is required as ground cover under and around the vegetation in
landscaped areas to help cool soil areas, reduce evaporation, and retard weed
growth. Existing areas of undisturbed native vegetation retained on the site
are not required to have the soil mulched or amended.
В В В F.В В В Use of the Public Right-of-Way
В В В Nonrequired landscaping may be placed in the public right-of-way , if the
following standards are met:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The landscaping is approved by the City Engineer or designee and
complies with the City EngineerвҖҷs standards on construction, irrigation,
location, and plant type;
В В В В В В 2.В В В All vegetation complies with the standards of Section
7.6.4, Landscaping Standards; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В The landscaping does not interfere with the use of the sidewalk .
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013; Am. Ord. 11127, 11/6/2013; Am. Ord. 11328, 12/8/
2015; Am. Ord. 12005, 6/6/2023; Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
7.6.5.В В В SCREENING STANDARDS
В В В The purpose of screening is to provide visual barriers, noise reduction, and
to provide privacy.
В В В A.В В В When Required
В В В Screening for individual land uses and zones must be provided as determined
in
Table 7.6.4-1 and in addition to the required landscape borders. Screening is
not required between similar uses in accordance with
Table 7.6.4-1.
В В В B.В В В Screening Materials
В В В В В В 1.В В В Walls
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В All walls required by this section must be of masonry material or
masonry with a stucco or textured finish.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Decorative openings may be incorporated into the upper 20% of an
otherwise solid masonry wall located along a street В frontage .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Earth Berms
В В В В В В The slope of an earth berm, used alone or in combination with a retaining
wall, may not exceed one foot of rise for every three feet of linear distance.
The width of an earth berm must be self stabilizing.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Hedges and Other Vegetation Screens
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Plants used for screening purposes may not be smaller than a five
gallon can size when planted. The plants may be aligned, or unevenly spaced,
but must provide a continuous screen at maturity within two years.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Vegetative screens shall be planted in areas not less than three
feet in width.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Fences
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Wood fences shall provide a continuous opaque В screen .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Nonwood fences, such as chain link, may not be used along a
street В frontage along a Gateway Route or within the SCZ.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Nonwood fences shall have wooden slat inserts, or a similar
material with equal or better durability, to provide opacity.
В В В C.В В В Location
В В В В В В 1.В В В Along interior lot lines of the site , the required perimeter
screens must be located on the property line , unless the screen is provided
between the property line and the use and a landscaped area of a minimum width
of 20 feet is provided between the screen and the property line . In situations
where a utility or drainage easement runs along a property line and the width
of easement lying within a site is not used as part of the site , then the
required screen may be located at the easement line.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Whether or not required by this section, screens along a street
В frontage must be located on the development side of the street landscape
border so that they do not obstruct the view of the street landscape border
from the street .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Screens may be located within the street landscape border , if the
following standards are met.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Minimum Width
В В В В В В В В В The street landscape border is a minimum of ten feet wide.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Vegetative Screens
В В В В В В В В В Hedges and other vegetative screens may not extend more than three
feet into the street landscape border . If, based on the growing
characteristics of the type of plant used, the ultimate width of the vegetative
screen will be greater than three feet, the vegetative screen must be
sufficiently set back from the landscape border to accommodate the wider
growth.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Non-vegetative Screens
В В В В В В В В В Fences or walls constructed in a single continuous line may not extend
into a street landscape border more than the actual width of the fence or wall.
Where a fence or wall incorporates offsets or similar design features, a screen
may extend a maximum of three feet into the street landscape border . (See
Figure 7.6.10-H, Screening Standards - Variations for Walls.)
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Earth Berms
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Where earth berms are used, the crest of the berm must be
located on site and no closer than one foot from the street В property line or,
on sites subject to the MS&R zone , the MS&R right-of-way line .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В If berms are used in conjunction with vegetation or a
retaining wall to achieve the screen , the vegetation or wall has to occur at
the area of the landscape border farthest away from the property line in
accordance with Section
7.6.5.C.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Bermed areas, front and back sides, must be landscaped to
comply with minimum standards when located within the street landscape border
area. Vegetation also minimizes erosion .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В If the toe of the berm slope abuts a sidewalk or pedestrian
area, design precautions must be implemented to prevent water from washing
debris, dirt, rocks, etc., onto the sidewalk or eroding the pedestrian path.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В When berms are used, design solutions are to be provided
accomplishing screening standards if the berm cannot satisfy the standards
(i.e., at the ends where the berm slopes or if the berm is cut to place a
tree).
В В В D.В В В Height Measurement
В В В The height of a screen required under
Table 7.6.4-1 is measured as follows.
В В В В В В 1.В В В The height of a screen В adjacent to a property line or along a
street В frontage is the vertical distance measured on the development side of
the screen from the design grade at the base of the screen to the top of the
screen .
В В В В В В 2.В В В For all structures that are not buildings (e.g., ground mounted
mechanical equipment) that are visible from the street В frontage , the screen
В height is measured from the design grade at the base of the structure to its
highest point.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The maximum permitted height of a free-standing screen is six feet.
В В В E.В В В Sight Visibility Triangle
В В В The maximum permitted height of a screen is 30 inches within a sight
visibility triangle (see Section
10-01.5.0, Sight Visibility, of the Technical Standards Manual). Trees may be
planted within these areas in accordance with Section
5-01.0.0, Landscaping and Screening Standards.
В В В F.В В В Phased Development
В В В Screening for phased development is required as follows:
В В В В В В 1.В В В The perimeter screening element along the property lines must be
installed during development of the first phase; or
В В В В В В 2.В В В Where the undisturbed natural desert is maintained in areas to be
developed in subsequent phases, a temporary screen may be erected around the
perimeter of the initial phase, subject to the following:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Temporary screening may be an opaque wood fence, a chain link
fence with wood slats, or any equivalent type of screen ; and,
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Temporary screening must be replaced by a permanent screen if
construction of the subsequent phases is not started within two years of the
date the original phase received a certificate of occupancy.
В В В G.В В В Exceptions to Screening Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В The required screen along a street В frontage for vehicular use and
outdoor display areas may be lowered to 30 inches if the site is located:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В On a street that is not designated as an MS&R; and
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Across the street from nonresidential uses or unimproved,
nonresidentially zoned parcels .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Where a building wall abuts a street landscape border , the building
may serve as the required screen .
В В В В В В 3.В В В The PDSD Director may grant a partial or complete exception to the
screening standards for development within a Historic Preservation Zone . The
exceptions may be granted if, after completion of the historic district
В development review required by Historic Preservation Zone , the PDSD Director
determines that the standards are not compatible with the character of the
historic district .
В В В В В В 4.В В В A partial or complete exception may be granted to the screening
standards for uses with extensive landscaped open space , including parks,
cemeteries, or golf courses.
В В В В В В 5.В В В For development В adjacent to a Planned Area Development (PAD), the
screening standards are those that are required for development В adjacent to
residential zones , except where an adjacent PAD has been developed or planned
for nonresidential uses. In that case, the required screening standards are
based on the adjacent В land use.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Community Garden and Urban Farm Uses are exempt from the screening
requirements of Section
7.6.5, Screening Standards.
(Am. Ord. 11328, 12/8/2015)
7.6.6.В В В USE OF WATER
В В В The following standards conserve water and assist in carrying out xeriscape
design principles.
В В В A.В В В Water Conservation Design
В В В Landscape plans shall incorporate water-conserving designs. A water-
conserving design must take into account soil and drainage factors and
microclimates, includes grouping of plants with similar water standards and use
of efficient irrigation systems, and attempts to preserve on-site vegetation.
В В В B.В В В Use of Reclaimed Water
В В В В В В 1.В В В Reclaimed water must be used in new and expanded development in
accordance with the adopted Mayor and Council Water Policies and other
applicable state and local standards.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The availability of a reclaimed water service does not confer any
right to plant turf areas except as permitted under this Section
7.6.4.D, Use of Turf.
В В В C.В В В Stormwater Runoff
В В В В В В 1.В В В Storm water detention/retention basins not integrated with paved
vehicular use areas must be designed in accordance with the Storm Water
Detention Retention Manual.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Grading , hydrology, and landscape structural plans must be
integrated to make maximum use of site storm water runoff for supplemental on-
site irrigation purposes. The landscape plan shall indicate use of all runoff,
from individual catch basins around single trees to basins accepting flow from
an entire vehicular use area or roof area.
В В В D.В В В Ornamental Water Features
В В В В В В 1.В В В Ornamental water features, such as fountains or ponds, may be used,
if all the following conditions are met.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The total water surface area does not exceed 1% of the net site
area of the development .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The water feature is located within the oasis area.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The sum of the square feet of water surface area and the square
feet of turf area does not exceed the total allowed square feet for the oasis
allowance .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Water in moving water features is recycled through the feature.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The water feature is designed to prevent water seepage or
leaking.
В В В В В В 2.В В В This section does not apply to swimming pools or spas.
В В В E.В В В Irrigation
В В В В В В 1.В В В A water-conserving irrigation system is required for all new
landscape plantings. For sites that are larger than one acre and on which the
gross floor area of all buildings is more than 10,000 square feet, an
underground irrigation system is required.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Storm water and runoff harvesting to supplement drip irrigation are
required elements of the irrigation system for both new plantings and preserved
vegetation.
В В В В В В 3.В В В An irrigation plan must be submitted together with the landscape
plan required in Section 7.6.10.B, Review Procedures, and must comply with the
standards below.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В System Specifications
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The source of irrigation water, whether potable or reclaimed,
must be indicated on the irrigation plan.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Rigid irrigation pipes and flexible drip system irrigation
lines under paved surfaces must be contained in pipe sleeves.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Pipes or lines carrying water under constant pressure must be
buried to a minimum depth of 24 inches. Those that are under intermittent
pressure must be buried to a minimum depth of 18 inches.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Nonpressure polyethylene lines must be buried to a minimum
depth of eight inches. Nonpressure PVC lines must be buried to a minimum depth
of 12 inches.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Turf , shrub, and bubbler heads closer than 12 inches to any
paved surface must be pop-up heads.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Turf , shrub, and bubbler heads located within 24 inches of
any paved surface must be installed with a system that allows the riser
assembly to absorb impacts and return to normal position.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Spray irrigation systems may be used only in the oasis area
for turf . These areas must be served by low precipitation rates. Matched
precipitation rate heads should be used.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Temporary spray irrigation systems may be used to establish
hydroseeded areas. All other plant material, excluding turf , must be irrigated
with drip irrigation or low-flow bubblers. Drip irrigation is encouraged. The
systems will be reviewed on a case-by-case basis.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (9)В В В Trees and shrubs outside of areas of spray irrigation must be
irrigated with low-flow bubblers or emitters.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (10)В В В Unless required under the rainwater harvesting ordinance,
rain-sensing and moisture-sensing devices are encouraged.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В System Design and Layout
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Irrigation systems must be designed with attention to
prevailing winds, degree of slope , type of soil, soil percolation
(infiltration), season, and type of vegetation to be watered. These design
constraints, and any others that may be specific to the site , must be clearly
indicated on all irrigation documents.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Spray irrigation systems shall be designed to provide 100%
head-to-head coverage of the area of irrigation to avoid dry spots and
overspray onto any paved or unplanted surface.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Areas of different water requirements, such as the oasis,
trees and shrubs, the vehicular use area , landscape borders, and native
vegetation areas, must be controlled by separate remote control valves.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В The design of irrigation systems and irrigation schedules
should attempt to match application rates with infiltration rates in order to
minimize runoff and reduce evaporation.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Sprinkler heads and nozzles within each control valve should
have matching precipitation rates.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Spray irrigation systems may not be installed in planting
strips less than ten feet wide. Strip spray heads are not be allowed.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Irrigation timers must be set for irrigation cycles between
8:00 p.m. and 8:00 a.m. between May and September to minimize water loss due to
evaporation.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Rain-sensing and moisture-sensing devices are encouraged.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Temporary irrigation proposals may be reviewed on a case-by-case
basis.
7.6.7.В В В COMPLIANCE WITH NEIGHBORHOOD PLANS
В В В Where a development is subject to neighborhood or area plan standards,
landscape plans must incorporate, to the greatest extent possible, the
landscape, screening, and design provisions of the adopted plan. In case of a
conflict between this section and the provisions of a neighborhood or area
plan, this section shall apply.
7.6.8.В В В NEW SINGLE FAMILY, MIDDLE HOUSING, AND ACCESSORY DWELLING UNITS
В В В В В В A.В В В The following landscaping is required for all new single family
residential, middle housing development , and accessory dwelling units . These
requirements do not apply to new single family residential dwellings within
subdivisions of 5 or more units.
В В В В В В В В В 1.В В В One canopy tree is required for every 1,000 square feet of gross
floor area of the development project.
В В В В В В В В В 2.В В В Vegetative groundcover is required within the front yard setback
per the calculation below.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (X Linear feet of Street Frontage) - (Y Curb cut width) x (5
Foot Depth) = Z Square Feet of vegetative groundcover
В В В В В В В В В В В В An example, using a parcel with 115 feet of street frontage and a
15-foot curb cut:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (115 Linear Feet of Frontage) - (15 curb cut) X (5 Foot Width) =
500 Square Feet of vegetative groundcover required within the front yard
setback.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Parcels with less than 30 linear feet of street frontage may place
the required groundcover in alternate locations indicated on the site plan.
В В В В В В В В В В В В Irregular-shaped lots, including flag lots, will use the street
frontage parallel to their parcel (see Figure 7.6.2.A).
В В В В В В В В В 3.В В В Plants must be low to very low water use with a hardiness rating
of 15 degrees Fahrenheit or lower and non-invasive. Options are listed in
Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual. Exceptions to these criteria or
plants that are not listed in Section
5-02.5.0 of the Technical Standards Manual, may be approved by the PDSD
Landscape Architect.
В В В В В В B.В В В Required landscaping must be located as follows:
В В В В В В В В В 1.В В В A minimum of fifty percent of the required canopy trees must be
located within the front yard setback area.
В В В В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Exception: If site constraints prevent new tree placement in a
front yard, five shrubs may be substituted per required tree. Required trees
must still be placed elsewhere on the site . Shrubs used as a substitute plant
material for the required tree shall range from three feet in height by three
feet in width, or larger. Shrubs must follow the relevant spacings from utility
lines in section 5-01.5.11of the Technical Standards Manual, but can be planted
one radius of their mature width from foundations, enclosed sidewalks,
sidewalks, and curbs.
В В В В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Exception: The tree required for an accessory dwelling unit
may be placed anywhere on site .
В В В В В В В В В 2.В В В Trees may be planted at varying distances apart.
В В В В В В В В В 3.В В В Required landscaping is not restricted to the first 10 feet onto
the property from the existing or future right-of-way line.
В В В В В В В В В 4.В В В Newly planted trees must be placed at a minimum distance from
existing or proposed City of Tucson-owned or private utility-owned lines, as
found in section 5-01.5.11 of the Technical Standards Manual. Tree plantings
are not permitted in El Paso Gas Easements or under overhead power lines.
В В В В В В В В В 5.В В В Trees shall be placed a minimum of ten feet from the center of
the tree trunk to any enclosed structure.
В В В В В В В В В 6.В В В Trees shall be placed a minimum of five feet from the back of the
sidewalk or curb. If this standard cannot be met, then root barriers are
required and must be illustrated on the submitted construction plans.
В В В В В В В В В 7.В В В Trees must be planted in a six-to-nine-inch depressed planting
area of at least 50 square feet (four-foot radius, or alternatives as proposed)
and at least ten feet away from a building foundation.
В В В В В В В В В 8.В В В Existing, established trees on property, at least 4 inches trunk
diameter at chest height, located at least ten feet from the foundation or with
an unobstructed dripline at maturity, shall count toward this requirement
(Figure 7.6.2.B). The root systems of existing trees used for this requirement
must be protected from soil compaction during construction using perimeter
protective fencing around the dripline . Established trees are not required to
have a depressed planting area. Existing shrubs and vegetative groundcover may
also be used toward the required landscaping .
[https://export.amlegal.com/media/bc207d22edb16588f0bd0d32c4fc7186a85be661/
IMAGES/0-0-0-16623.gif]
В В В Figure 7.6.2-B:
В В В Obstructed and unobstructed dripline s on various size trees, located 10
feet from an existing structure
В В В В В В C.В В В Vegetation shall be established, maintained, and sustained
throughout its life in support of the City's tree count, climate, and
walkability goals. One of the following two options may be used:
В В В В В В В В В 1.В В В Drip or bubbler irrigation with backflow protection. A simple
irrigation system can be part of the domestic water supply for the residence.
В В В В В В В В В 2.В В В Alternate methods, such as rainwater harvesting, hand watering,
and greywater systems, as proposed by the property owner and approved by the
PDSD Landscape Architect.
В В В В В В D.В В В Proposed landscaping and related items can be included in a
residential plot plan; a Landscape Plan is not required.
(Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
7.6.9.В В В MAINTENANCE
В В В All required landscaping , irrigation systems, walls, screening devices,
curbing, and detention basin landscape improvements on the site or within the
abutting В right-of-way shall be maintained as shown on the approved plans. The
property owner is responsible for proper maintenance to achieve permanent,
safe, and successful landscaping as required by this section. Failure to
maintain the improvements or landscaping required by this section constitutes a
violation of the UDC .
В В В A.В В В Plant Materials
В В В В В В 1.В В В Any plant material in areas of required landscaping that does not
survive must be replaced with an equivalent size and species within 30 days .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Plant material must be pruned as necessary to control size but not
to disrupt the natural growth pattern or characteristic form of the plant
except as necessary to achieve height clearance for visibility and pedestrian
passage or to achieve a continuous opaque hedge if required.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Clipping and pruning of the plant material are required to allow
maximum shading while preserving surveillance opportunities and preventing
obstructive overhang into walks, curb areas, drives, and line of sight
triangles as required by the Unified Development Code .
В В В В В В 4.В В В All dead or removed plant material must be replaced with plant
material acceptable under the standards of the xeriscape В landscaping
standards. Replacement material must be a minimum 15-gallon size for trees and
five gallon size for shrubs.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Regular landscape maintenance shall ensure water efficiency and
include, but not be limited to, pruning, mulching, weeding, litter removal,
aerating and dethatching turf areas, and fertilizing nonnative plant materials,
as necessary.
В В В B.В В В Inert Materials
В В В В В В 1.В В В Exterior improvements , such as benches, walls, or ramadas, must be
of durable materials appropriate to the desert climate. Material with integral
coloring or stucco is preferred for seating fixtures and other permanent
improvements .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Inert materials used in the landscaping areas must be of a nature to
withstand the extremes of the desert climate and minimize heat gain or
reflected heat.
В В В В В В 3.В В В The inert materials must be replaced as needed due to displacement
or erosion .
В В В C.В В В Planting Areas
В В В В В В 1.В В В Inert materials must be used in inaccessible, narrow, or hard to
maintain sections of the planting areas.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Landscaped areas must be kept free of trash, weeds, debris, and dead
plant material and must be maintained in a clean and neat fashion.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Use of river rock is discouraged adjacent to building windows.
В В В D.В В В Landscape Removal
В В В В В В 1.В В В Substantial modifications, as determined by the PDSD Director , and/
or removal of plant materials or other landscape elements shall require review
and approval of a revised landscape plan by PDSD.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The removal or destruction of landscape material that has been
installed according to an approved landscape plan and not replaced constitutes
a violation of the UDC .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Minor modifications of the landscaping occurring as a result of
routine maintenance, necessary replacement of elements because of damage or
disease, or other causes is not subject to review by PDSD.
В В В E.В В В Screening and Wall Maintenance
В В В В В В 1.В В В Paint and stucco must be renewed on the surfaces of fences and walls
as needed.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Wooden slats in chain link fences must be replaced as needed to
retain the opacity of the screen .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Any screening device that has deteriorated to the point where it
does not serve as a screen must be replaced by the property owner. Vegetation
used to meet the screening standards may not be replaced with new vegetation
after two years past the installation. Replacement must be with another option
for screening, such as a fence or a wall, in order to achieve an immediate
screen . This standard does not apply to the occasional replacement of single
plants within a vegetative screen .
В В В F.В В В Irrigation System Maintenance
В В В Irrigation systems must be maintained and replaced as necessary to continue
to conserve water. Detection and repair of leaky or broken pipes, valves, and
fittings and malfunctioning and/or misaligned heads, emitters, and bubblers
must be part of a regular maintenance program for the site .
В В В В В В 1.В В В Automatic Irrigation Controllers are recommended to be reset a
minimum of four times a year (spring, summer, fall, and winter) to adjust for
plant water requirements that vary according to the season.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Whenever possible, repair of irrigation equipment must be done with
the originally specified materials or their equivalent.
(Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
7.6.10.В В В ADMINISTRATION
В В В A.В В В Design and Review Procedures
В В В The applicant for development approval is advised to schedule a presubmittal
conference with the PDSD and Tucson Water Department staff with reference to
specific design or review procedures.
В В В B.В В В Review Procedures
В В В Landscape plans are reviewed in the same manner and concurrent with the
applicable site plan review required for development approval. Comments on the
landscape plans and requests for corrections or resubmittals are made in
conjunction with responses to the applicable tentative plat , or site plan .
For development located along Scenic Routes and Gateway Routes , the additional
review procedures set forth in Section
5.3 and Section
5.5, respectively, apply.
В В В C.В В В Site Inspection and Enforcement
В В В В В В 1.В В В Inspections
В В В В В В All landscaping required by this section must be inspected by the PDSD
prior to a final certificate of occupancy being issued by the City or prior to
the building being occupied.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Enforcement
В В В В В В With the permission of the owner or tenant, City staff may enter a site
or any part of a site for the purpose of performing inspections to verify
compliance with the standards of this section.
В В В D.В В В Variances
В В В The Design Review Board (DRB) shall review all variance requests from the
Landscaping and Screening Standards as provided in Section
2.2.6.C.5 and forward a recommendation in accordance with Section
2.2.6.B.6.
(Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
7.7.В В В NATIVE PLANT PRESERVATION
7.7.1.В В В INTENT
В В В A.В В В TucsonвҖҷs setting is in the Sonoran Desert, a unique biological
community known as the Arizona Uplands found only in southern Arizona and
limited areas of northern Mexico. Some of the plants and animals living in this
area are found nowhere else in the world. One of the most distinctive plants is
the Saguaro cactus (Carnegiea gigantea), a visual symbol synonymous with Tucson
and the Sonoran Desert. The Saguaro, along with certain other Sonoran genus and
species, is extremely slow growing and not easily transplanted with success.
В В В B.В В В Development in the Tucson area has decreased the number of these unique
native plants resulting in the loss of a natural resource. City -wide goals and
policies for preservation of the native plants found in the Sonoran Desert have
been established in the Vision: A Guide for the Future of the City of Tucson,
adopted in 1989 and in the General Plan , Section 2, Vegetation and Wildlife,
adopted in 1992. This section addresses the preservation of native Upland
vegetation that provides much of the visual character of the Sonoran Desert and
supports wildlife.
В В В C.В В В In conjunction with development , buffelgrass invasion is spreading
along roadways , wash systems and into the undisturbed Sonoran Desert.
Buffelgrass (Pennisetum ciliare) is converting a fire-resistant desert to
flammable grassland. In 2005, the state of Arizona placed buffelgrass on the
Arizona Noxious Weed List prohibiting its entry or sale in the state .
Buffelgrass is a wildfire risk to desert ecosystems threatening life, property,
tourism and the regional economy. Buffelgrass, as it burns, can kill the
Saguaro cactus, other native vegetation and is detrimental to desert wildlife
species including the desert tortoise and mule deer.
В В В D.В В В This section is intended to encourage preservation-in-place of healthy
native plants through sensitive site design that minimizes the disruption of
areas within the site containing native plants, while allowing for salvage and
transplanting plants on the site that are likely to survive. The section also
provides for replacement of plants that are removed for development at ratios
to assure site revegetation and to compensate for probable loss of transplanted
and replacement plants. Mitigation credits are offered to encourage
preservation-in-place . Mature trees and Saguaros should be given particular
consideration for preservation-in-place . When preservation-in-place is not
possible, emphasis should next be given to plant salvage for use elsewhere on
the site , and lastly, if plants are removed from the site , the revegetation
concept should emphasize the recreation of the natural character and plant
distribution similar to the undisturbed vegetation on and adjacent to the
subject site .
7.7.2.В В В PURPOSE
В В В These standards provide for the preservation, protection, transplanting, and
replacement of existing designated native plants including cacti, succulents,
trees, and shrubs through the establishment of comprehensive procedures,
requirements, and standards that protect the public health, safety, and general
welfare by:
В В В A.В В В Preserving a sense of place through the potential enhancement of the
communityвҖҷs appearance from public streets and between incompatible land uses ;
В В В B.В В В Maintaining property values, the quality of life, and lifestyles valued
and enjoyed by the community through the preservation of the unique Sonoran
vegetation;
В В В C.В В В Contributing to economic development through the maintenance of a
regional identity that attracts tourism and new businesses, while promoting
business retention and expansion;
В В В D.В В В Improving air quality through the preservation of mature vegetation
that removes carbon monoxide and filters dust and particulates from the air;
В В В E.В В В Promoting water conservation through retention of existing drought-
tolerant vegetation that requires no supplemental irrigation;
В В В F.В В В Assisting in climate modification and reducing energy costs through the
use of native vegetation to shade buildings , streets , sidewalks , and other
outdoor areas;
В В В G.В В В Retaining vegetative features of habitats that are important to native
wildlife species; and,
В В В H.В В В Stabilizing desert soils by minimizing soil erosion through
preservation of or revegetation with native plants.
7.7.3.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В The provisions of this section apply to all development as listed below.
В В В A.В В В New Development
В В В All new development proposed in the City of Tucson shall comply with the
standards of this section.
В В В B.В В В Expansions of Existing Development
В В В В В В 1.В В В If the expansion is less than 25%, the standards of this section
apply only to the proposed expansion area.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the expansion is 25% or greater or if expansions after March 24,
1997, cumulatively result in a 25% or greater expansion in land area, floor
area , lot coverage , or vehicular use area , the standards of this section
apply to the proposed expansion area and the remaining undeveloped site area.
В В В C.В В В Concurrent Applicability of Other Related Sections
В В В The standards of this section; the Hillside Development Zone (HDZ) (Section
5.2); the Environmental Resource Zone (ERZ) (Section
5.7); the Watercourse Amenities, Safety, and Habitat (WASH) ordinance (Section
29 of the Tucson Code); the Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ) (Section
5.3); and the Landscape and Screening Standards (Section
7.6) are all calculated separately. Plants or areas preserved or planted for
the purposes of compliance with one standard may be considered for the purposes
of compliance with this section.
В В В D.В В В Exceptions
В В В The provisions of this section do not apply to the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Single-family residential development on lots recorded before March
24, 1997, or single-family residential development on a lot within a
subdivision that is subject to an approved Native Plant Preservation Plan after
the sale and development of a principal structure on that lot ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Single-family residential subdivisions for which a tentative plat
has been submitted or approved prior to July 1, 1997, per Article 8,
Subdivision Standards, provided the plat  is recorded within one year from the
approval date of the final plat , infrastructure  is in place, and 50% of the
lots  are developed within five years after July 1, 1997;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Projects on a site that does not contain any plant on the Protected
Native Plant List as demonstrated by the applicant and subject to approval
through procedures established at the Planning and Development Services
Department (PDSD). This application can be submitted prior to an applicable
review process and approved for an exception on the site . The approved
exception is valid for up to one year from the date of the approval; or,
В В В В В В 4.В В В Projects on a site or parcel that contains Protected Native Plants
that will not be substantially impacted by development on the site as
demonstrated by the applicant per this section and subject to approval through
procedures established at the PDSD, provided that construction occurs per the
approved plan.
7.7.4.В В В GENERAL PROVISIONS AND STANDARDS
В В В A.В В В Submittal Requirements
В В В A Native Plant Preservation Plan in accordance with Section
2-12.0.0 of the Administrative Manual is required for all projects under the
applicability of this section according to the methodology selected by the
applicant as outlined in Section
7.7.5, Plant Preservation Standards. If buffelgrass is present, populations are
to be mapped and included as part of the Native Plant Preservation Plan  or the
Environmental Resource Report . Continued buffelgrass eradication and
monitoring methodology, including common areas, is required for developed and
undeveloped portions of a site .
В В В В В В 1.В В В If the Plant Inventory Methodology is selected, a Native Plant
Inventory , an Analysis, and a Plant Preservation and Salvage Plan in
accordance with Section
2-11.4.4 of the Administrative Manual must be submitted.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the Plant Appraisal Methodology is selected, a plant appraisal in
accordance with Section
7.7.5.A.2 based on a Native Plant Inventory for each plant to be removed from
the site in accordance with Section
2-11.4.1, Native Plant Inventory, of the Administrative Manual must be
submitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В If the Set Aside Methodology is selected, an Environmental Resource
Report in accordance with Section
7.7.5.A.3 and Section
2-11.4.5, Set Aside Submittal, of the Administrative Manual must be submitted.
В В В B.В В В Plan Approval Prior to Site Modifications
В В В В В В 1.В В В No grubbing, grading , construction, or salvaging of any plants on
the site may take place prior to the submittal and approval of the required
Native Plant Preservation Plan . Protected Native Plants designated in the
Technical Standards Manual may not be destroyed, damaged , salvaged,
transplanted, or removed from the site except in accordance with the approved
Plan. Once a Native Plant Preservation Plan has been approved, a salvage-only
permit may be issued. The salvage-only permit may be issued upon completion of
one of the following conditions:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В For rezoning or annexation cases, ordinance adoption by the Mayor
and Council;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В For tentative plats or development plans, approval from all City
departments and public and semipublic agencies that review the plat or plan;
and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В For site plans , an official submittal to the PDSD with all site
plan review fees paid in full.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The salvage-only permit does not allow site grubbing, grading , or
construction of any kind, other than the salvaging of those materials as
indicated on the approved Native Plant Preservation Plan . No protected native
plants , even if indicated on the approved Native Plant Preservation Plan for
removal or destruction, may be destroyed or removed from the site until a
grading permit has been issued for the project .
В В В C.В В В Plan Approval Prior to Permitting
В В В The Native Plant Preservation Plan must be submitted prior to or
concurrently with any plan(s) required for development approval, including a
grubbing/grading plan, landscape plan , site plan , or tentative plat . No
permits may be issued prior to submittal and approval of the Native Plant
Preservation Plan .
В В В D.В В В Professional Expertise
В В В Preparation of all elements of the Native Plant Preservation Plan and on-
site monitoring as required under Section
7.7.5.C.5, On-Site Monitoring, must be performed by a plant professional, such
as:
В В В В В В 1.В В В An arborist certified by the International Society of Arboriculture;
В В В В В В 2.В В В A landscape architect; or,
В В В В В В 3.В В В A horticulturist, biologist, or botanist with a minimum B.A. or B.S.
in an appropriate arid environment natural resource field.
В В В E.В В В Plant Disposition
В В В The Native Plant Preservation Plan must indicate the disposition of
Protected Native Plants in excess of those needed to meet the standards of this
section and other applicable standards. It is encouraged that all Protected
Native Plants that are not proposed to remain on-site be salvaged and
transplanted off-site . The Plan shall affirm, by a statement on the Plan,
conformance with the requirements of the Federal Endangered Species Act and the
Arizona Native Plant Law. Applicants are advised that required salvage permits
that must be obtained from the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service for the
applicable Endangered and Threatened genus and species and required
notification that must be provided to the Arizona Department of Agriculture
prior to site modification. Compliance with this section does not constitute
conformance with state or federal regulations.
7.7.5.В В В PLANT PRESERVATION STANDARDS
В В В A.В В В Plant Preservation Methodologies
В В В One of the following four methodologies is required when preparing a Native
Plant Preservation Plan for Protected Native Plants on the project site . The
applicant must state on the plans and/or in the report which methodology has
been chosen.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Plant Inventory Methodology
В В В В В В The Plant Inventory Methodology is based upon a Native Plant Inventory of
all Protected Native Plants in accordance with Section
2-11.4.1.A of the Administrative Manual. The viability and transplantability of
Protected Native Plants must be rated according to Section
7.7.5.A.1.b and must be used to determine the numbers and locations of plants
required for preservation. The minimum number of Protected Native Plants
required for preservation is based upon a percentage of the Viable Protected
Native Plants inventoried on-site . In addition, this methodology provides for
mitigation through the planting of replacement plants for Protected Native
Plants that are damaged , destroyed, or removed from the site . The number of
replacement plants required for mitigation is based upon the number of Viable
Protected Native Plants that are removed from the site . The preservation-in-
place of Protected Native Plants may be credited toward the number of
mitigation plants required. Refer to
Table 7.7.5-1 and the Native Plant Preservation Worksheet, Section
2-12, Exhibit I, of the Administrative Manual. The provisions of this
subsection may be combined, or used in conjunction, with the Plant Appraisal
Methodology or the Set Aside Methodology, in accordance with Section
7.7.5.A.4, Combined Methodologies.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Minimum Protected Native Plant Preservation Standards
В В В В В В В В В The minimum required standards for the preservation of Protected
Native Plants in the Plant Inventory Methodology are as follows.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Endangered Species and вҖңCrestedвҖқ Saguaros
В В В В В В В В В В В В One hundred percent of Federal Endangered Species and вҖңCrestedвҖқ
Saguaros must be preserved-in-place or salvaged and transplanted on-site .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Saguaros and Ironwoods
В В В В В В В В В В В В At least 50% of all Viable Saguaros and Ironwood trees must be
preserved-in-place or salvaged and transplanted on-site .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Other Protected Native Plants
В В В В В В В В В В В В At least 30% of each genus and species of other Viable Protected
Native Plant must be preserved-in-place or salvaged and transplanted on-site .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Native Plant Viability and Transplantability Status
В В В В В В В В В The Native Plant Viability and Transplantability Status must be
determined for each native plant of the minimum size and must be used to
determine numbers and locations of plants required for preservation.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Plant Viability Standards
В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant viability is based upon plant health, age, and form. Plants
rated Low are not considered Viable and are not required to be assessed
according to the Transplantability Standards. Plants rated Medium or High are
considered viable and must be assessed under the Transplantability Standards.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В High
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A high plant viability rating must be assigned to plants meeting
the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (i)В В В Health
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant health is good to excellent with no major infestations
of pests or apparent diseases;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (ii)В В В Age
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant age is young or mature with a likely chance of long
survival; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iii)В В В Form
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant is relatively undamaged with a healthy branching habit.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Medium
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A medium rating must be assigned to plants that do not meet all
of the standards for a high rating but have sufficient merit, in the opinion of
the qualified professional conducting the inventory, to warrant preservation.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В Low
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A low plant viability rating must be given to plants meeting any
one or more of the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (i)В В В Health
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant health is poor. Generally the result of severe
infestations of pests or diseases or a lack of water over time;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (ii)В В В Age
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant is in a state of decline, suggesting a low probability
of lengthy survival; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iii)В В В Form
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant form and character is severely damaged . For trees,
this may include new branches from large, old, dead trunks or weak branching
habit.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Plant Transplantability Standards
В В В В В В В В В В В В Plant transplantability standards are based upon plant genus and
species, size, soils, context, and topography. The following five categories
must be inventoried to determine the ability to salvage the Viable plants that
will not be preserved-in-place. Plants rated Low for transplantability should
not be considered for salvage and transplant. Plants rated medium or high that
are not preserved-in-place should be considered for salvage and transplant on-
site or off-site .
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (a)В В В High
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A high rating for transplantability is assigned to viable plants
that also meet the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (i)В В В Genus and Species
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Has a high survival rate for reestablishment after
transplant;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (ii)В В В Size
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Overall plant dimensions are suitable for transplanting based
upon the genus and species;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iii)В В В Soils
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Can be excavated, are cohesive, and seem capable of
supporting the rootball system;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iv)В В В Topography
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Permits access with the appropriate equipment needed to
remove plants and their rootball systems; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (v)В В В Context
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Adjacent plants do not pose a likely interference with root
systems or interfere with plant removal.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (b)В В В Medium
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A medium Transplantability rating is assigned to plants that do
not meet all of the standards for a high rating but do have sufficient merit,
in the opinion of the qualified professional conducting the inventory, to
warrant transplanting.
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (c)В В В Low
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В A low rating for Transplantability is assigned to plants that
also meet the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (i)В В В Genus and Species
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Has a low survival rate for reestablishment after transplant;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (ii)В В В Size
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Overall plant dimensions are not suitable for transplanting
based upon the genus and species;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iii)В В В Soils
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Too rocky, sandy, or shallow to excavate a cohesive rootball
system;
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (iv)В В В Topography
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Seriously limits access to the specimen by the appropriate
equipment (i.e., steep slopes , rock barriers); and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В (v)В В В Context
В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В В Adjacent plants interfere with removal or present likely
conflicts with the rootball system.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Preservation Credits
В В В В В В В В В The preservation-in-place of certain Protected Native Plants ,
including those that are subject to the minimum standards of Section
7.7.5.A.1.a, Minimum Protected Native Plant Preservation Standards, are
credited toward the mitigation  standards of this section as determined by the
size of the Protected Native Plant  as listed in
Table 7.7.5-1, Protected Native Plant Preservation Credits and Mitigation
Standards. The preservation credits shall be in the form of the number of
plants that may be used to offset the number of additional plants of that same
genus and species required, based on the mitigation  standards, Section
7.7.5.A.1.d. Generally, the preservation of larger Protected Native Plants
results in a greater credit toward the mitigation  standards. For example, the
preservation-in-place  of one 12-foot Saguaro results in a credit of three
Saguaros.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Mitigation Standards
В В В В В В В В В Viable Protected Native Plants harmed by on-site salvage and
transplant, off-site removal, or damage or destruction during development must
be mitigated by replacement with a plant or plants of the same genus and
species in conformance with
Table 7.7.5-1. Plants used for mitigation may either be nursery grown plants,
plants that are transported to the site in conformance with the state Native
Plant Protection Act, A.R.S. В§В§3-901 et seq., or plants salvaged from the site
that are not required for the minimum preservation standards of Section
7.7.5.A.1.a(2), Saguaros and Ironwoods, or (3), Other Protected Native Plants .
Salvage and transplant on-site of Protected Native Plants that are in excess of
the minimum standards in Section
7.7.5.A.1.a(3) do not require any further mitigation . Each native plant that
is salvaged and transplanted on-site to meet the minimum standards of Section
7.7.5.A.1.a(2) or (3) must be supplemented with one additional plant of the
same genus and species as listed in
Table 7.7.5-1. Each Viable Protected Native Plant that is removed from the site
, damaged , or destroyed during development must be replaced on-site with an
additional number of plants of the same genus and species as determined by
Table 7.7.5-1.
