Table 4 - Portable Maximum Sign Area Allotment
В В В B.В В В Portable Signs - General Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В Ground and Wall Mounted General Standards
В В В В В В В В В These standards apply to ground mounted and wall -mounted portable
signs for a premise . The signs may be attached by cords to posts and other
supports and include posters, banners , A-frames , and other similar portable
signs types. Ground-mounted includes affixed to the ground, placed on the
ground, or attached to a perimeter wall or fence that faces the street .
В В В В В В 2.В В В No illumination is permitted.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Ground-Mounted General Standards
В В В В В В В В В Maximum area per sign in a residential zone is 16 square feet and in a
non-residential zone 32 square feet unless otherwise regulated by another
portable sign type standard.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum Height: Ten feet.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Minimum Setback : Ten feet.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Maximum Number on a Street В Frontage : No more than four signs
shall be allowed for the first 300 feet of street В frontage . An additional
sign may be allowed for each additional 75 feet of a street В frontage but not
to exceed a total of eight signs for a street В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Materials: A ground-mounted portable sign shall be made of
similar materials such as non-pliable or taut materials. A sign shall not be a
ground-mounted banner made of a pliable fabric such as cloth with multiple
supports unless stated otherwise in
Article 7A.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Spacing of ground-mounted portable signs at a premise shall
consist of the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Lots with a street В frontage of 150 feet or greater may have
up to four 32 square foot В signs per each 150 feet with at least a minimum of a
30 foot distance between the individual signs . Two of the four signs whose
sign areas combined do not exceed 32 square feet may have a distance between
each other that is closer than 30 feet. An individual sign under 32 square feet
in sign area and not grouped with another sign must be at a minimum a distance
of 15 feet from another sign .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Lots with a street В frontage less than 150 feet but greater
or equal to 75 feet may have up to two 32 square foot В signs with a 30 foot
distance between the signs . If the two signs combined sign area does not
exceed 32 square feet the signs may be placed at a distance between each other
that is closer than 30 feet. An individual sign under 32 square feet in sign
area and not grouped with another sign must be at a minimum a distance of 15
feet from another sign .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Lots with a street В frontage less than 75 feet may have one
sign up to 32 square feet in sign area.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Wall -Mounted General Sign В Standards
В В В В В В В В В A wall -mounted portable sign may not exceed 32 square feet on an
arterial street , 16 square feet on a collector street or eight square feet on
a local street . Wall -mounted banners are not allowed on a dwelling unit.
В В В C.В В В Portable Sign - Additional Standards
В В В В В В 1.В В В A-frame В Signs
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В An A-frame В sign is an on-site non-illuminated sign temporarily
authorized for one year used to advertise the location, goods or services
offered on the premises . The A-frame В sign must be made of a durable, rigid
material such as, but not limited to, wood, plastic or metal.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A-Frame В signs are prohibited from any form of illumination,
animation, movement and miscellaneous attachments including, but not limited
to, balloons, ribbons, speakers etc.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A-frame В signs are not permitted in the public right-of-way
except where a temporary revocable easement has been granted for their
placement.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Maximum Height and Width
В В В В В В В В В В В В The A-frame В sign when placed in an open position must not exceed a
height of 30 inches from ground level to the top of the sign and be no more
than 30 inches wide.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Maximum Number
В В В В В В В В В В В В One per business. The A-frame В sign must be removed from the street
at the close of business.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Districts
В В В В В В В В В В В В A-frames В signs are regulated by district : they are permitted in
the commercial and industrial zones , planned area development В district ,
pedestrian business district and historic district subject to subsection below.
Subject to the other restrictions under this section, A-frames В signs are
allowed in all districts to advertise those businesses immediately adjacent to
and affected by road or water construction.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Historic District
В В В В В В В В В В В В An A-frame В sign may be permitted in a historic district after
review and approval by the planning and development services director , the
applicable historic district advisory board and the Tucson Pima County
Historical Commission.
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Decal Required
В В В В В В В В В В В В A decal issued by the City for placement of any A-frame В sign shall
be displayed on the upper right hand corner of each visible advertising face.
An A-frame В sign authorized to be located in the City's public right-of-way
shall display a decal of a different color than signs not authorized to be in
the public right-of-way .
В В В В В В В В В i.В В В Permission Required
В В В В В В В В В В В В The permission of the property owner for use of the sign is also
required.
В В В В В В В В В j.В В В Sign Maintenance
В В В В В В В В В В В В The A-frame shall be properly maintained.
В В В В В В В В В k.В В В Application Process
В В В В В В В В В В В В By signing and submitting the application the applicant verifies
the following:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В The property owner or management company is in compliance
with their current lease agreement in regard to the permissibility of A-frame
В signs ; and,
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В The applicant is responsible to research and verify right-of-
way information to ensure that the proposed location of the A-frame is on
private property and is not located in the City's public right-of-way .
В В В В В В В В В l.В В В Site Inspection
В В В В В В В В В В В В A site inspection is not required as part of the application and
permit process for A-frame В signs that are not located in the public right-of-
way . For A-frame В signs located in the public right-of-way , the zoning
administrator may require site inspection or additional inspections of the sign
В standards to ensure proper placement.
В В В В В В В В В m.В В В Refunds
В В В В В В В В В В В В No refunds of application or annual permit fees will be given under
any circumstance.
В В В В В В В В В n.В В В Placement
В В В В В В В В В В В В The A-frame В sign must be located at least two feet back from the
face of the curb. No A-frame В sign shall be located in a median, driving lane,
parking aisles or spaces. An A-frame В sign is not permitted in the City's
public right-of-way without a temporary revocable easement. An A-frame В sign
shall be placed so that a minimum four feet is left clear for pedestrian
passage on all sidewalks and walkways. An A-frame В sign shall only be placed at
grade level and shall not be placed on walls, boulders, planters, vehicles,
other signs or any other type of structure .
В В В В В В В В В o.В В В Temporary Revocable Easement
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В A license to permit the placement of a temporary A-frame
В sign in the public right-of-way may be granted administratively by the zoning
administrator upon the written terms and conditions as are required by the
zoning administrator and contained in the written license agreement. The
applicant will submit a written application upon a form to be supplied by the
office of the zoning administrator. The zoning administrator will then approve
or deny such application. A license to permit an A-frame В sign in the public
right-of-way shall not be granted until all other applicable permit
requirements contained in this Code are met.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В If at any time after a license is granted, any portion of the
public right-of-way occupied and used by the licensee may be needed or required
by the City or the licensee fails to maintain the permitted sign so as to block
pedestrian traffic, site visibility, or as described by such specific terms and
conditions set forth by the zoning administrator, any license granted pursuant
to this section may be revoked by the City and all rights there under are
terminated. The licensee shall and will promptly remove all property belonging
to the licensee from the public right-of-way area upon receipt of written
notice of revocation. If removal is not accomplished by the licensee within the
time specified in the notice, the City will cause the sign to be removed and
stored and the cost thereof shall be charged to the licensee. Notwithstanding
the foregoing, any A-frame В sign placed in violation of the provisions of this
section is deemed to be a public nuisance and subject to removal by the City.
В В В В В В В В В p.В В В Removal of Sign
В В В В В В В В В В В В In addition to the penalties described below, the zoning
administrator may remove any A-frame В sign that is placed in violation of the
sign В standards . The zoning administrator may immediately remove, without
prior notice, any A-frame В sign illegally placed in the right-of-way or site
visibility triangle, or that for any reason presents an immediate hazard. For
any A-frame В sign otherwise illegally placed, the zoning administrator may
remove the sign after providing reasonable notice to the responsible party В 
В В В В В В 2.В В В Commercial Flags, Feather Banners , and Banner Flags
В В В В В В В В В In commercial and industrial zones , in lieu of the Portable Sign area
allotment, an applicant may use four commercial flags, feather banners , or
banner flags using two colors with one additional portable sign .
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.10.4В В В TEMPORARY SIGNS
В В В A.В В В Temporary Signs
В В В В В В The following sign types are permitted for a limited period of time and
are not calculated as part of the portable sign area allotment.
В В В В В В 1.В В В A Fixed balloon sign is permitted as a temporary sign under the
following standards :
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В One balloon sign 2' in diameter or larger is permitted per
establishment. Balloons smaller than 2' in diameter are considered small fixed
balloons and are regulated by Section
7A.10.4.A.3;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A fixed balloon sign is prohibited from having mechanical or
electronic movement or animation of any kind;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A permit for a fixed balloon will be issued for a period of 90
days.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The permitted fixed balloon may be displayed for up to 10
consecutive days at a time.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В A fixed balloon is allowed in commercial and industrial zones
only.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Temporary Banner
В В В В В В В В В A wall -mounted banner may be permitted under the following standards
:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum number: One banner per street В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Size: 90 sq. ft. maximum.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Allowed in a commercial and industrial zone only.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A temporary wall -mounted banner is not calculated as part of the
portable sign area allotment for a premise or building on a premise.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В All corners must be attached to the building В wall or facade or a
temporary construction structure during periods of remodeling or road
construction.
В В В В В В В В В f. В В В Allowed in 90-day increments. This banner cannot be used for
more than a cumulative total of 90 days in any calendar year, except that if
this banner is used to temporarily advertise business activities during the
course of construction, permit may be renewed until construction has been
completed.
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В The temporary banner is subject to Section
7A.2 Non-commercial Speech Substitution.
В В В В В В В В В h.В В В Removal: Upon completion of construction or as stated on the
permit for installation of the banner.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Propellers, Hula Strips, Small Fixed Balloons, and Pennants
В В В В В В В В В Propellers, hula strips, small fixed balloons, and pennants may be
permitted under the following standards:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Allowed in 90-day increments. The propellers, hula strips, small
fixed balloons, and pennants cannot be used for more than a cumulative total of
180 days in any calendar year.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Propellers, hula strips , small fixed balloons, and pennants are
subject to Section
7A.2 В Non-commercial Speech Substitution.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Political Election Signs
В В В В В В В В В Shall comply with A.R.S. В§ 16-1019.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum area: 32 square feet in commercial and industrial zones
and 16 square feet in rural and residential zones .
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.10.5В В В HERITAGE LANDMARK SIGNS (HLS)
В В В A.В В В Definitions
В В В В В В 1.В В В Heritage landmark sign (HLS). A sign that has conditional or final
designation as a heritage landmark sign . HLS are listed on the City of Tucson
Heritage landmark Sign Registry. There are three types of HLS: classic,
transitional, and replica.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Classic HLS. A heritage landmark sign originally installed prior to
1961.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Transitional HLS. A heritage landmark sign originally installed
between 1961 and 1974 inclusive.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Replica HLS. An accurate reconstruction of an original sign that no
longer exists. The sign to be replicated must have been originally installed
prior to 1961.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Heritage landmark sign (HLS) character defining features. Physical
features of an HLS such as materials, technologies, structure , colors, shapes,
symbols, text, font/typography and/or art that have cultural and historical
significance and are integral to overall sign design.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Heritage landmark sign (HLS) concentration. A minimum of three
previously designated HLS, or signs meeting the criteria for designation,
within 2,640 linear feet (1/2 mile) as measured along the center line of a
street , including turning in any direction at the intersection of a street to
connect with another designated HLS or sign meeting the criteria for
designation, together with an additional 440 linear feet (1/16 mile) beyond the
terminus HLS. A replica HLS cannot be used as part of the number of HLS in the
calculation of a HLS concentration.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Heritage landmark sign (HLS) registry. The official list of
designated historic landmark В signs within the City of Tucson.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Heritage landmark sign (HLS) treatment plan. A detailed description
of an HLS including its character defining features, condition, location, and
maintenance, and, as applicable, proposed restoration, adaptive reuse,
relocation, and, replication.
В В В B.В В В Purpose
В В В В В В 1.В В В The Heritage landmark Sign regulations are intended to provide for
the preservation of the City of Tucson's unique character, history, and
identity, as reflected in its historic and iconic signs , and
В В В В В В 2.В В В To restore the sense of place that existed within the central
business district and in areas of the City with concentrations of surviving
historic signs , and
В В В В В В 3.В В В To protect the community from inappropriate reuse of nonconforming
and/or illegal signs .
В В В C.В В В Heritage Landmark Sign (HLS) Designation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Requests for HLS designation shall be initiated by the sign owner
and supported by an HLS treatment plan.
В В В В В В 2.В В В "As is" HLS designation. An existing sign which will not be
restored/repaired, adaptively reused, or relocated, and retains sufficient
integrity and character-defining historic features, is in working order, and
has an acceptable appearance, may obtain HLS designation "as is", upon approval
of the treatment plan.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Conditional HLS Designation
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The decision to approve or deny an HLS treatment plan that meets
each of the HLS designation guidelines is rendered per the administrative
process herein.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The decision to approve or deny an HLS treatment plan that does
not meet each of the HLS designation guidelines is rendered per the legislative
process herein.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Approval of an HLS treatment plan shall constitute conditional
HLS designation.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Final HLS Designation
В В В В В В В В В Final HLS designation shall be contingent upon issuance of a sign
permit in compliance with an approved HLS treatment plan, and final inspection
of the sign within five years of conditional HLS designation. Issuance of a
permit is not required for "as is" designation.
В В В В В В 5.В В В All signs designated (conditional or final) as heritage landmark
signs shall be listed in the City of Tucson Heritage landmark Sign Registry.
В В В D.В В В HLS Designation Guidelines
В В В В В В Classic, transitional, and replica HLS shall be reviewed for compliance
with the following guidelines:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Technical guidelines:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The sign shall include or have once included exposed integral
incandescent lighting, or exposed neon lighting.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The sign shall use materials and technology representative of its
period of construction.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The sign shall be non-rectangular or non-planar.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The sign shall be a permanent sign , including but not limited to
a detached, projecting, wall , or roof sign .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В The sign is structurally safe or can be made safe without
substantially altering its historical appearance.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Cultural/historical/design guidelines:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The sign shall exemplify the cultural, economic, and historic
heritage of Tucson.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The sign shall exhibit extraordinary aesthetic quality,
creativity, and innovation.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The sign is unique; or was originally associated with a chain or
franchise business that is either a local or regional chain or franchise only
found in Tucson or the southwestern United States; or there is scholarly
documentation to support its preservation; or it is a rare surviving example of
a once common type.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В The sign shall retain the majority of its character defining
features. If character-defining features have been altered or removed, the
majority are potentially restorable to their historic function and appearance.
В В В E.В В В HLS Performance Requirements
В В В В В В Classic, transitional, and replica HLS shall comply with the following
requirements as applicable:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Restoration/repair
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Restoration/repair of a classic HLS shall be consistent with a
documented appearance that existed prior to 1961.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Restoration/repair of a transitional HLS shall be consistent with
a documented appearance that existed between 1961 and 1974 inclusive.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Restoration/repair of a replica HLS shall be consistent with a
documented appearance that existed prior to 1961.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Restoration/repair shall not add typographical or other elements
which result in an increase in the size of the restored/repaired sign .
В В В В В В 2.В В В Adaptive Reuse (Change of Copy)
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Adaptive reuse of a replica HLS is prohibited.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Text changes shall not result in changes to character defining
text.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Text changes shall match or be compatible with existing text in
material(s), letter size, font/typography, and color.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Relocation
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В When relocated, detached HLS shall be setback at least 20 feet
from the back of curb (edge of pavement if there is no curb), no more than 40
feet from the future right-of-way line of the street , and a distance at least
two times the height of the sign from any property with a non-commercial use.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В If relocated to another premise , the HLS shall display
conspicuous text or a plaque, using a template provided by the City of Tucson,
that indicates that the sign has been relocated, the date of relocation, and
the original location.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The scale and design of the sign to be relocated shall be
compatible with existing HLS in the vicinity of the proposed location.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Replica HLS
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В A replica HLS shall be consistent with a documented appearance
that existed prior to 1961.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В A replica HLS may only be installed on the premises where it
originally existed.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В A replica HLS shall display conspicuous text or a plaque, using a
template provided by the City of Tucson, that indicates the sign is a
contemporary reproduction, and the date of reproduction.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В A replica HLS shall utilize historical materials and
technologies, or visually matching contemporary materials and technologies.
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В A replica HLS shall not replicate an existing sign .
В В В В В В 5.В В В The sign shall not be an off-site sign as defined in the sign
В standards .
В В В В В В 6.В В В The sign shall not have been previously, altered, removed and
reinstalled or replaced pursuant to Section
7A.9В Non-conforming Signs.
В В В F.В В В Content of HLS Treatment Plan
В В В В В В An HLS treatment plan shall include the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Completed application form.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Existing and proposed elevation of the proposed HLS showing height
and area of the sign .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Description and age of construction materials and type of
illumination.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Dimensioned site plan , with the existing and proposed land use ,
graphically showing existing and proposed location and setbacks for the
proposed HLS and any other existing or proposed signs on the premises ,
existing and proposed site В improvements , and adjacent В streets .
В В В В В В 5.В В В Photographs of the existing sign and photo simulation(s) of the
completed sign as viewed from the street and other significant vantage points
as appropriate, together with photographs of the existing site conditions.
Photographs must be sufficient to demonstrate the sign's dimensions,
construction materials used including electrical and any types of illumination
which is or was used.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Date of original construction and installation, and the address
where the proposed HLS was first installed.
В В В В В В 7.В В В List of character defining features.
В В В В В В 8.В В В Documentation of the authenticity of the proposed HLS including
approved permits, site plans , elevations, and dated photographs, and age of
existing materials, as available.
В В В В В В 9.В В В A narrative describing compliance with each of the HLS designation
guidelines and all applicable HLS performance requirements.
В В В В В В 10.В В В Maintenance program.
В В В В В В 11.В В В List of parts and materials to be replaced.
В В В В В В 12.В В В Mitigation measures to reduce the impact on residential uses within
300 feet of the proposed HLS.
В В В G.В В В Review of HLS Treatment Plan
В В В В В В HLS treatment plans shall be submitted to the Planning and Development
Services Department for review.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Pre-submittal conference. Prior to submitting an HLS treatment plan,
an applicant may, but is not required to meet with City staff responsible for
administration of the HLS program. Comments supplied by City staff during the
conference are advisory and do not constitute approval of any proposed
application.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Neighborhood meeting. A neighborhood meeting is encouraged for a
proposed HLS relocation, or for a proposed construction of a HLS replica sign .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Initial review. Initial review of an HLS treatment plan or revised
treatment plan will be for completeness, compliance with HLS designation
guidelines, and compliance with applicable HLS performance requirements. No
later than ten days after submittal, the zoning administrator will issue a
determination as to whether the request meets each of the HLS technical
designation guidelines.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Where an applicant produces physical evidence or documentation
sufficient to prove that a proposed HLS included intermittent lighting features
(e.g. flashing, blinking, chasing or sequentially lit elements which create the
appearance of movement) or moving parts, such sign elements may be repaired or
restored conditioned upon a determination by Tucson Department of
Transportation (TDOT) that no negative safety issues will result.
В В В В В В 5.В В В If the subject property is within a historic preservation zone
(HPZ), the treatment plan shall be forwarded to the HPZ advisory board for
review and recommendation prior to being forwarded to the Tucson-Pima County
Historical Commission (T-PCHC) Plans Review Subcommittee .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Review of the treatment plan shall include an analysis of applicable
policies of the Tucson general plan .
В В В В В В 7.В В В The treatment plan shall be forwarded to the T-PCHC Plans Review
Subcommittee for review of the treatment plan for compliance with HLS Cultural/
historic/design designation guidelines and applicable HLS performance
requirements. Notice of the treatment plan and subcommittee meeting shall also
be provided to the registered neighborhood association in which the proposed
sign would be located; to property owners immediately adjacent to the proposed
location; and to any persons who have submitted a written request to the
director to be notified of HLS applications.
В В В В В В 8.В В В The T-PCHC shall forward a recommended list of character defining
features, including all character defining text, and a recommendation to
approve or deny the treatment plan, to the Planning and Development Services
Department.
В В В В В В 9.В В В Decision
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Administrative: The planning and development services director
will prepare a written decision to approve or deny the treatment plan within
ten days of receiving the T-PCHC Plans Review Subcommittee recommendation.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Legislative: An appeal from the Planning and Development Services
Director's decision shall be conducted in accordance with Section
3.9.2, Mayor and Council Appeal Procedure. In granting approval, the mayor and
council must find that preservation of the sign will contribute to Tucson's
unique character, history, and identity.
В В В H.В В В Review of Permits for HLS
В В В В В В All permits for the installation, repair /restoration, adaptive reuse,
relocation, or replication of HLS shall be consistent with an approved HLS
treatment plan.
В В В I.В В В Maintenance
В В В В В В All maintenance activities relating to HLS shall be consistent with an
approved HLS treatment plan.
В В В J.В В В Demolition
В В В В В В Demolition of HLS shall be consistent with an approved treatment plan for
relocation or subject to a maximum 30 day waiting period to facilitate salvage
of the sign . The sign owner shall allow reasonable access to the sign to
facilitate documentation and salvage activities.
(Ord. 11508, 12/5/2017; Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.11.В В В SPECIAL DISTRICTS
The sign В standards in historic, pedestrian and scenic corridor districts take
precedence over the sign В standards listed above in Section
7A.10В Sign Types and General Standards. All the below referenced districts,
zones  and provisions are superseded when the sign is regulated by Section
7A.7В Sign Design Options.
7A.11.1.В В В HISTORIC DISTRICT
В В В A.В В В Location
В В В В В В Historic districts include property established as historic preservation
zones pursuant to Section
5.8В Historic Preservation Zone В and designated with the preface "H" which is
added to the assigned residential, office, commercial, or industrial zone
designation, i.e., R-1 becomes HR-1. For purposes of the sign В standards ,
historic districts are treated as specific mapped districts and are not treated
as overlay zones . Note: historic preservation zone  maps are located on the
official City of Tucson's zoning maps .
В В В B.В В В Regulation of Signs
В В В В В В All signs located in a "H" Historic Preservation Zone are regulated under
Section
5.8.9.M, Signs, and subject to review under 5.8.8, Design Review Required.
В В В C.В В В Maximum Area of Any Sign
В В В В В В Eight square feet in a residential zone ; 40 square feet in a commercial
zone . The sign shall be appropriately sized, result in minimal damage to
historic fabric, and avoid adverse effects on qualities that make a building
eligible for listing in the National Register of Historic Places.
В В В D.В В В Historic District Advisory Board Approval
В В В В В В All sign permit applications must be approved by the appropriate historic
district advisory board.
В В В E.В В В No signs may extend above the top of the nearest facade , eaves or
firewall of a building or structure .
В В В F.В В В Illumination
В В В В В В Signs within the historic district may be illuminated by remote light
sources that are shielded from adjacent properties.
В В В G.В В В Prohibited Features
В В В В В В Visible bulbs, neon tubing, luminous paints, and plastics are prohibited,
except as provided in Section
5.8.9  HPZ Design Standards В and approved in accordance with Section
5.8.8В Design Review Required.
В В В H.В В В Permitted Signs
В В В В В В 1.В В В Signs generally permitted and sign types listed in Section
7A.10, except as modified by this subsection for this district , and signs
exempt under Section
7A.8В Exempt and Prohibited Signs.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Awning signs .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Banners , building and curbside.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Freestanding signs , monument and low profile only.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum number: One per premises .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Freestanding signs that include or consist of a three-dimensional
representation of a figure or object are prohibited.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Portable signs .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В May be permitted in this district only after review and approval
by the planning and development services director , the applicable historic
district advisory board and the Tucson Pima County Historical Commission.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Use is subject to the provisions of Section
7A.10.3В Sign Types and General Standards.
В В В В В В 6.В В В Projecting signs .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Allowed use: Limited to commercial uses only.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum height: 20 feet from grade (pedestrian surface) to top of
sign or two feet below the parapet , whichever is more restrictive.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Minimum clearance : Eight feet between grade and bottom of sign .
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Maximum projection from building : Five feet.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Temporary signs .
В В В В В В 8.В В В Wall signs .
В В В В В В 9.В В В Canopy signs , limited to properties zoned HO-2, HO-3, HNC, HC-1,
HC-2, HC-3, HOCR-1 and HOCR-2.
В В В В В В 10.В В В Historic landmark В signs (HLS), all types. The first HLS on a
premise does not count toward the maximum total sign area.
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.11.2 .В В В PEDESTRIAN BUSINESS DISTRICT
В В В A. В В В Location
В В В В В В The pedestrian business district includes property as shown in Figure 1:
Pedestrian Business District Downtown; Figure 2: Pedestrian Business District
Fourth Avenue; Figure 3: Pedestrian Business District Park Avenue; and Figure
4: Pedestrian Business District Mercado.
