В В В B.В В В Intent
В В В В В В Signs in the pedestrian business district should provide clear and
understandable identification for buildings , businesses, and parking. Signs on
historic buildings should be carefully designed and located to respect the
visual integrity of the historic architecture, including building scale,
proportions, surface texture and decorative ornamentation.
В В В C.В В В Maximum Total Sign Area
В В В В В В Three square feet per foot of street В frontage .
В В В D.В В В Permitted Signs
В В В В В В 1.В В В Signs generally permitted and sign types listed in Section
7A.10, except as modified by this subsection for this district , and signs
exempt under Section
7A.8В Exempt and Prohibited Signs.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Awning signs .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Banners , building and curbside.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Freestanding signs , low profile and monument type only.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum number: One per building per street В frontage where a
building В facade is set back at least ten feet from a public right-of-way , or
one per street В frontage for a surface parking lot where parking is the primary
use of the property.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum area: 20 square feet per sign .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Parking lots: Where used to identify a commercial parking
facility, each freestanding sign must display the standard Parking I.D. symbol.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Maximum height: 12 feet above grade.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Parking lot signs .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Projecting signs .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Allowed for commercial uses only.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum area: 20 square feet.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Maximum height: 20 feet from grade (pedestrian surface) to top of
sign or two feet below the parapet , whichever is more restrictive.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Minimum clearance : Eight feet between grade and bottom of sign .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Maximum projection from building : Five feet.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Portable signs .
В В В В В В 8.В В В Temporary signs .
В В В В В В 9.В В В Access point signs .
В В В В В В 10.В В В Wall signs . Maximum size: 30% of the area of each wall .
В В В В В В 11.В В В Heritage landmark signs (HLS), all types. The first HLS on a
premise does not count toward the maximum total sign area.
В В В В В В 12.В В В Canopy signs .
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.11.3.В В В SCENIC CORRIDOR ZONE (SCZ) DISTRICT
В В В A.В В В Location
В В В В В В The scenic corridor zone (SCZ) district includes any portion of property
or parcels within 400 feet, measured in any direction, of the future right-of-
way lines of a scenic route , as designated on the Major Streets and Routes
(MS&R) Plan map. If any portion of a development is within the SCZ district ,
the entire development will be treated, for sign purposes only, as though it
were entirely within the SCZ district .
В В В B.В В В Maximum Total Attached Sign Area
В В В В В В 1.В В В For commercial or industrial uses: 1.25 square feet per foot of
building В frontage with a minimum allowance of not less than 25 square feet and
a maximum of 250 square feet per tenant . Signs must be oriented toward a
scenic route , arterial street , collector street , or the interior of the
premises .
В В В В В В 2.В В В For multifamily complexes: 20 square feet per street В frontage .
В В В C.В В В Unified Development Code Compliance
В В В В В В All signs in this District shall comply with applicable provisions of the
Unified Development Code and must be approved through the applicable review
process.
В В В D.В В В Colors
В В В В В В All signs shall use colors that are predominant within the surrounding
landscape, such as desert and earth tones, as required in the scenic corridor
zone provisions of the Unified Development Code .
В В В E.В В В Permitted Signs
В В В В В В 1.В В В Signs generally permitted and sign types listed in Section
7A.10, except as modified by this subsection for this district , and signs
exempt under Section
7A.8В Exempt and Prohibited Signs.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Awning signs .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Freestanding signs , monument and low profile only.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum number per premises :
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Scenic route : One for the first 450 feet of scenic route
street frontage . A second sign is permitted for a street frontage from 451
feet to 850 feet. A third sign is permitted for a street В frontage from 851
feet to 1250 feet. This rule applies for each subsequent 400 feet of scenic
route В street В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Arterial street : One for the first 450 feet of arterial
street В frontage . A second sign is permitted for a street В frontage of 451
feet to 700 feet. A third sign is permitted for a street В frontage of 701 feet
to 950 feet. This rule applies for each subsequent 250 feet of arterial street
В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Collector Street : One for the first 450 feet of collector
street В frontage . A second sign is permitted for a street В frontage of 451
feet to 700 feet. A third sign is permitted for a street В frontage of 701 feet
to 950 feet. This rule applies for each subsequent 250 feet of collector street
В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum area:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Multifamily residential uses: 20 square feet per street
В frontage .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Commercial or industrial uses: 35 square feet per sign if
located within the SCZ buffer, 50 square feet per sign if located outside the
SCZ buffer.
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Maximum height: Ten feet.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Location:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Scenic route : Maximum height signs shall be located no less
than 7.5 feet behind the leading edge of the SCZ buffer and within 50 feet of
the right-of-way line. Signs may be located one foot closer to the leading edge
of the SCZ buffer for each foot (below the maximum) they are reduced in height.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В All other streets : Within 20 feet of the right-of-way line
and at least 150 feet from the centerline of the scenic route .
В В В В В В В В В e.В В В Freestanding signs that include or consist of a three-dimensional
representation of a figure or object are prohibited.
В В В В В В В В В f.В В В Lighting: Sign panels shall be opaque . Light shall be emitted
through individual translucent letters and/or symbols only, or individual
letters and/or symbols may be halo illuminated. Unused tenant panels shall be
opaque and designed to match the rest of the sign .
В В В В В В В В В g.В В В Within SCZ buffer digital sign copy and exposed neon signs are
prohibited.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Access point sign (medical uses).
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Maximum area: Eight square feet.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum height: Four feet to top of sign .
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В Permitted: Only if no frontage on collector or arterial street .
В В В В В В 5.В В В Portable signs .
В В В В В В 6.В В В Access point sign .
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Within the scenic corridor 30 foot landscape buffer the following
shall apply:
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Minimum site area : Ten acres.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Maximum area: Three square feet; tenant identification or
logo not to exceed one square foot .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Maximum number: One per vehicular entrance.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Location: Within 20 feet of the entrance.
В В В В В В 7.В В В Wall signs .
В В В В В В 8.В В В Canopy signs .
(Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.11.4.В В В PLANNED AREA DEVELOPMENT (PAD) DISTRICT
В В В A property owner within a PAD district shall use the sign В standards of the
zone upon which the PAD regulation is modeled or may submit a master sign
program. Billboards are prohibited in a PAD district .
(Ord. 11508, 12/5/2017)
7A.12.В В В APPEALS
All appeals shall be heard in accordance with Section
3.10В Board of Adjustment Appeals and Variances. The applicant may appeal a
Board of Adjustment decision to the Mayor and Council per Section
3.9.2В Mayor and Council Appeals Procedure or to the Superior Court.
(Ord. 11508, 12/5/2017; Am. Ord. 11803, 12/8/2020)
7A.13.В В В SIGN DESIGN REVIEW COMMITTEE
A sign design review committee is hereby created to assist the planning
commission by reviewing and recommending amendments to the sign В standards and
recommending design options in compliance with Section
7A.7В Sign Design Options to the planning and development services director .
The committee's authority and organization shall be in accordance with Section
2.2.12В Sign Design Review Committee.
(Ord. 11508, 12/5/2017)
ARTICLE 8: LAND DIVISION, LAND SPLIT , AND SUBDIVISION STANDARDS
Section
8.1.В В В PURPOSE
8.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
8.3.В В В LAND DIVISION
В В В 
8.3.1.В В В Land Split
8.4.В В В SUBDIVISIONS
В В В 
8.4.1.В В В Block Plat
В В В 
8.4.2.В В В Residential and Non-Residential Condominium and Condominium Conversion
В Plat
В В В 
8.4.3.В В В Minor Subdivision
В В В 
8.4.4.В В В Tentative Plat
В В В 
8.4.5.В В В Final Plat
8.5.В В В EXPIRATION DATES
В В В 
8.5.1.В В В Tentative Plat
В В В 
8.5.2.В В В Final Plat
8.6.В В В GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR SUBDIVISIONS
В В В 
8.6.1.В В В General Provisions
В В В 
8.6.2.В В В Subdivision Improvements and Assurances
В В В 
8.6.3.В В В Survey Monuments Required
В В В 
8.6.4.В В В Permits for Model Homes
В В В 
8.6.5.В В В Land Clearing and Grading
В В В 
8.6.6.В В В Plat В Abandonment
8.7.В В В SUBDIVISION DESIGN STANDARDS
В В В 
8.7.1.В В В Parks, Recreational Facilities, Fire Stations, and School Sites
В В В 
8.7.2.В В В Phased Subdivisions
В В В 
8.7.3.В В В Flexible Lot Development (FLD)
8.1.В В В PURPOSE
В В В The purpose of this article is to:
8.1.1.В В В Assure that all land divisions, land spits, and subdivisions are in
conformance  with the City вҖҷs regulations regarding land development ;
8.1.2.В В В Assure a coordinated vehicular and pedestrian circulation system;
8.1.3.В В В Establish minimum standards for land divisions and subdivisions ;
8.1.4.В В В Assure that all lots  or parcels  are provided with infrastructure
В improvements ;
8.1.5.В В В Provide an expedient and consistent review process;
8.1.6.В В В Obtain accurate survey and permanent public record of the boundaries
of lots  created by the division of lands and subdivision В plats ;
8.1.7.В В В Facilitate the conveyance of land by reference to an accurate legal
description by means of a recorded subdivision В plat ; and,
8.1.8.В В В Provide a convenient method of describing property being conveyed.
8.2.В В В APPLICABILITY
В В В This article applies to all proposed land divisions, land spits, and
subdivisions of land as defined in this code, with the following exceptions:
8.2.1.В В В The sale or exchange of parcels  of land to or between adjoining
property owners if such sale or exchange does not create additional lots ;
8.2.2.В В В The partitioning of land in accordance with other statutes regulating
the partitioning of land held in common ownership;
8.2.3.В В В The leasing of apartments, offices, stores, or similar space within a
building  or mobile home/RV park, or mineral, oil, or gas leases;
8.2.4.В В В Land divisions for defining cemetery plots or columbarium units; and,
8.2.5.В В В Although exempt from this article, these types of land divisions may
still require review and approval by the PDSD staff and outside review agencies
in accordance with other articles of the UDC .
8.3.В В В LAND DIVISION
8.3.1.В В В LAND SPLIT
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В According to A.R.S. В§9-463.01(T), the City may regulate a land split as
defined in Section
11.4.13 within its jurisdictional limits. The state law gives the City
authority to determine the division lines, area, and shapes of the parcels .
Neither a tentative plat nor a final plat is required but the resulting tracts
or parcels shall conform to applicable zoning requirements and other government
codes and ordinances. The purpose of this section is to establish a review
process for land splits . This review:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Assures that newly created tracts or parcels are of sufficient size
to be developed and meet the requirements of applicable zoning classification;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Assures that the newly created tracts or parcels have locations for
building pads that are protected from flood and erosion hazards as defined in
the Floodplain Ordinance (Tucson Code Chapter 26) and Standards Manual for
Drainage Design and Floodplain Management in Tucson, Arizona; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Assures that all tracts or parcels resulting from a land split shall
have adequate access as specified by Section
7.8, Access, of the UDC , Sections
7-01.0.0, Pedestrian Access, and
10-01.0.0, Street Technical Standard, of the Technical Standards Manual and
City of Tucson Drainage Manuals.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В A proposed land split shall be submitted to PDSD for review and approval as
provided in this section.
В В В C.В В В Pre-application Conference Recommended
В В В A pre-application conference is encouraged, but not required.
В В В D.В В В Application Required
В В В Submittal of an application to the PDSD is required in order to process the
request. The application must be in accordance with Section
2-08.0.0, Land Split Applications, of the Administrative Manual. Applications
are reviewed for completeness in accordance with Section
3.2.3, Application Requirements.
В В В E.В В В Review
В В В Land splits are reviewed by the PDSD for compliance with the following
requirements:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Does not constitute a subdivision as defined in Section
11.4.20 which would require compliance with platting requirements of Section
8.4;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Results in tracts or parcels which conform to the minimum lot size
requirements of the propertyвҖҷs zone ;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Provides access to the proposed tracts or parcels in accordance with
Section
7.8, Access, of the UDC , Sections
7-01.0.0, Pedestrian Access, and
10-01.0.0, Street Technical Standard, of the Technical Standards Manual, and
the City of Tucson Floodplain Ordinance and Drainage Manuals;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Results in all existing buildings complying with the perimeter yard
requirements of the propertyвҖҷs zone ; and,
В В В В В В 5.В В В Results in tracts or parcels of at least the minimum size required
to build in accordance with the applicable zoning including any natural,
environmental and floodplain requirements in accordance with the City of Tucson
Floodplain Ordinance and Drainage Manuals.
В В В F.В В В Decision
В В В В В В 1.В В В The PDSD Director decides whether to approve or deny an application.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The PDSD will notify the applicant if the land split has been
approved.
В В В G.В В В Recordation Required
В В В After approval, the deeds for the land split shall be recorded at the Pima
County RecorderвҖҷs Office by the applicant/owner or his designated agent.
8.4.В В В SUBDIVISIONS
8.4.1.В В В BLOCK PLAT
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В The block plat process allows a subdivider to divide a parcel of land into
ten or fewer lots without the necessity of a tentative plat, subject to the
requirements of this section as allowed for by A.R.S. В§9-463.01(U). A block
plat may be used to abandon a previously recorded plat. Although a tentative
plat is not required, additional information shall be submitted as needed, to
review the project for compliance with all governmental regulations and good
engineering practices. Additional information that may need to be provided
include a geotechnical report, grading plan, drainage report, phasing plan,
title report, or other documents that the City deems necessary to review for
compliance with all applicable standards.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В Any proposed block plat , as defined in Section
11.4.3, shall be submitted to PDSD for review and decision as provided below.
В В В C.В В В Pre-application Conference Required
В В В A pre-application conference in accordance with Section
3.2.1 is required.
В В В D.В В В Application, Review, Decision, and Recordation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Block plats shall be prepared, reviewed for compliance with Section
8.4.1.E, Criteria, and considered for approval in accordance with Section
8.4.5, Final Plat, with the exception that a pre-application is required.
В В В В В В 2.В В В After approval of the block plat by the Mayor and Council, the block
plat must be recorded with the Office of the Pima County Recorder.
В В В E.В В В Criteria
В В В Block plats must:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Result in ten or fewer lots;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Result in lots /blocks that conform to the minimum lot size
requirements of the propertyвҖҷs zone ;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Provide access to the proposed lots in compliance with Section
7.8, Access, of the UDC  and Sections
7-01.0.0, Pedestrian Access, and
10-01.0.0, Street Technical Standard, of the Technical Standards Manual;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Results in all existing buildings complying with the perimeter yard
requirements of the propertyвҖҷs zone ;
В В В В В В 5.В В В Conform to all City , state , and federal drainage requirements;
В В В В В В 6.В В В Result in lots of at least the minimum size required to build in
conformance with the applicable zoning including any natural, environmental and
floodplain requirements;
В В В В В В 7.В В В Not be for a Flexible Lot Development; and,
В В В В В В 8.В В В When used to abandon a plat, the disposition of the public right-of-
way and utility easements created by the abandoned plat is provided.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013)
8.4.2.В В В RESIDENTIAL AND NON-RESIDENTIAL CONDOMINIUM AND CONDOMINIUM CONVERSION
В PLAT
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В The purpose of this section is to establish procedures and requirements for
proposed condominium В projects and the conversion of existing structure(s) into
condominiums as allowed by A.R.S. Title 33, Chapter 9.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В A proposed condominium В project or condominium conversion that meets the
definition of a subdivision , as defined in Section
11.4.20, shall be submitted to the PDSD for review and decision as provided
below.
В В В C.В В В Application, Review, Decision, and Recordation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Under Arizona Revised Statutes, A.R.S. В§ 9-463.02(A), a subdivision
В plat is required when a condominium or similar project contains four or more
parcels .
В В В В В В 2.В В В A tentative and final plat is required; however, the approved site
plan for the existing structure/development may serve as the tentative plat if
approved by the Director .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Tentative plats of a proposed condominium are prepared, reviewed,
and considered for approval in accordance with Section
8.4.4, Tentative Plat.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Final plats of the proposed condominium or condominium conversion n
are prepared, reviewed, and considered for approval in accordance with Section
8.4.5, Final Plat.
В В В В В В 5.В В В A final plat may be submitted concurrent with the tentative plat in
accordance with Section
3.2.3.B, Sequential or Concurrent Review Procedure.
В В В В В В 6.В В В After approval by the Mayor and Council, the final condominium or
condominium conversion В plat must be recorded with the Office of the Pima
County Recorder.
(Am. Ord. 11200, 9/23/2014)
8.4.3.В В В MINOR SUBDIVISION
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В A minor subdivision may be accomplished with less documentation and it
provides a more streamlined process than required for subdivisions. Although a
tentative plat is not required, additional information shall be submitted, as
needed, to review the project for compliance with all governmental regulations
and good engineering practices. Additional information that may need to be
provided include a geotechnical report, grading plan, drainage report, phasing
plan, title report, or other documents that the City deems necessary to review
for compliance with all applicable standards. The purpose of this section is to
establish procedures and requirements for minor subdivision В plats .
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В A subdivision meeting the following criteria is considered a minor
subdivision :
В В В В В В 1.В В В The number of proposed lots is ten or less;
В В В В В В 2.В В В All utility services are available at the subdivision В site
boundary;
В В В В В В 3.В В В All proposed lots have street В frontage or perpetual access
easements of a minimum width and maximum length complying with requirements
established by Section
7.8, Access, of the UDC  and Section
10-01.0.0, Street Technical Standards, of the Technical Standards Manual;
В В В В В В 4.В В В All streets forming the boundary of the subdivision are fully
improved, except for sidewalks that may be improved as part of the project ;
В В В В В В 5.В В В The subdivision has no special topographic conditions, such as
slopes greater than 10%;
В В В В В В 6.В В В The property is not located within a 100-year regulatory floodplain
area or erosion hazard setback ;
В В В В В В 7.В В В The property has all weather access in accordance with the City of
Tucson Floodplain Ordinance and Design Manuals, or Section
10-02.4.4, Street Drainage, of the Technical Standards Manual;
В В В В В В 8.В В В The proposed subdivision В site does not have special development
requirements, or special requirements have been reviewed and special
development requirements have been determined. These special development
requirements may include, but are not limited to, the Hillside Development Zone
(HDZ); the Environmental Resource Zone (ERZ); the Scenic Corridor Zone (SCZ);
and the Watercourse Amenities, Safety, and Habitat (WASH) ordinance; and,
В В В В В В 9.В В В The minor subdivision cannot be used for the creation of Flexible
Lot Development (FLD).
В В В C.В В В General Provisions
В В В В В В 1.В В В Minor subdivisions shall conform to the design standards provided in
Section
8.6 , General Requirements for Subdivisions, and Sections
8.7.1 and
.2 of the Subdivision Design Standards.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Up to two single-family model homes may be authorized for
construction prior to recordation of the final plat, provided:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Sale of a lot or occupancy of an individual structure as a
residence does not occur until after recordation of the final plat and the City
вҖҷs release of assurances for improvements , and
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The location of each structure is based on the minor subdivision
В lot configuration approval for final plat recording.
В В В D.В В В Pre-application Conference Required
В В В A pre-application conference in accordance with Section
3.2.1 is required.
В В В E.В В В Application, Review, Decision, and Recordation
В В В В В В 1.В В В Minor subdivisions are prepared, reviewed for compliance with all
applicable requirements, and considered for approval in accordance with Section
8.4.5, Final Plat, with the exception that a pre-application is required;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Any reviews that are normally conducted during the tentative plat
process, such as those involving drainage statements or reports, shall instead
be conducted as part of the final plat process; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В After approval by the Mayor and Council, the minor subdivision must
be recorded with the Office of the Pima County Recorder.
8.4.4.В В В TENTATIVE PLAT
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В The tentative plat sets forth the technical mapping of the project including
its density , intensity, land uses , pedestrian and bicycle ways, trails,
parks, open space , and future lots , streets , and drainage patterns
established for the site . The purpose of this section is to establish
procedures and requirements for tentative plats . If the subdivision proposed
is a Flexible Lot Development, the subdivision shall additionally comply with
Section
8.7.3.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В Any proposed subdivision , as defined in Section
11.4.20, shall be submitted to PDSD for review and decision as provided in this
section.
В В В C.В В В Pre-application Conference Recommended
В В В A pre-application conference in accordance with Section
3.2.1 is recommended, but not required.
В В В D.В В В Neighborhood Meeting Required for Flexible Lot Developments Only
В В В For Flexible Lot Development Project tentative plats only, the applicant is
required to conduct a neighborhood meeting and provide notice of the meeting in
accordance with Section
3.2.2, Neighborhood Meeting.
В В В E.В В В Application Required
В В В В В В 1.В В В Submittal of an application to the PDSD is required in order to
process the request. The application must be in accordance with Section
2-06.0.0, Development Package, of the Administrative Manual. Applications are
reviewed for completeness in accordance with Section
3.2.3, Application Requirements.
В В В В В В 2.В В В A final plat may be submitted concurrent with the tentative plat in
accordance with Section
3.2.3.B, Sequential or Concurrent Review Procedure.
В В В В В В 3.В В В For Flexible Lot Development Projec t tentative plats only, a mailed
notice of application is required in accordance with Section
3.2.4.B, Mailed Notice.
В В В F.В В В Review
В В В В В В 1.В В В PDSD shall coordinate the formal technical review of all tentative
plats to ensure the plats are prepared in accordance with the applicable codes,
ordinances, and standards.
В В В В В В 2.В В В On receipt of the application, PDSD shall distribute copies of the
tentative plat to CDRC members for review and comment.
В В В В В В 3.В В В CDRC shall review the tentative plat for compliance with all
pertinent codes and legal requirements, including, but not limited to, codes
regulating streets , sidewalks and public transportation access, zoning,
floodplain and drainage, grading , fire, water, environmental services, Pima
County wastewater, and utility company regulations.
В В В В В В 4.В В В If CDRC finds that the tentative plat requires revisions then:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The plat will be returned to the subdivider with a letter listing
specific deficiencies and required revisions;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The subdivider shall resubmit a revised tentative plat and a
letter which responds to each deficiency in detail and explains any revisions
made by the subdivider to the plat;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The subdivider continues to resubmit the tentative plat until
such time as the CDRC recommends approval of the tentative plat and it is
forwarded to the PDSD Director ; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Any necessary resubmittals will not change or extend the
expiration times commencing at acceptance of a tentative plat, as provided in
this article.
В В В В В В 5.В В В The zoning of the property shall permit the proposed use, and any
changes to zoning shall have been adopted prior to the approval of the
tentative plat.
В В В G.В В В Decision
В В В В В В 1.В В В If the requirements for tentative plats have been met, then the CDRC
, by its designated representative, shall send a letter to the applicant
notifying the applicant that the plat has been forwarded to the PDSD Director
with a recommendation for approval.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The PDSD Director , or designee, shall approve the tentative plat
once all the final documents, as detailed in the CDRC decision letter, are
submitted for his or her signature.
В В В H.В В В Significance of Tentative Plat Approval
В В В Approval of the tentative plat shall constitute authorization for the
subdivider to proceed with the preparation of the final plat.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Once a tentative plat is approved, the layout and design under which
approval of the tentative plat is granted shall not be changed without
concurrence of both the reviewing departments and agencies and subdivider prior
to the expiration date of the tentative plat.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Approval of a tentative plat does not guarantee final acceptance of
streets for dedication.
В В В I.В В В Tentative Plats Approved Prior to the Effective Date of the UDC
В В В A final plat application for which a tentative plat was approved prior to
the effective date of this UDC may be processed in accordance with the
tentative plat approval and applicable terms of the ordinance in place at the
time of tentative plat approval, even if the application does not comply with
one or more standards set forth in this UDC . Tentative plat approvals granted
prior to the effective date of this UDC may be extended no more than once, and
for no longer than one year.
8.4.5.В В В FINAL PLAT
В В В A.В В В Applicability
В В В В В В 1.В В В Proposed subdivisions, including minor subdivisions , block plats ,
and condominium В plats , require the review and approval of a final plat.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The subdivider shall construct all required subdivision improvements
, at no expense to the City , prior to recordation of the final plat, or the
subdivider may post assurances as detailed in Section
8.6.2, Subdivision Improvements and Assurances.
В В В B.В В В Application Required
В В В В В В 1.В В В Submittal of an application to the PDSD is required in order to
process the request. The application must be in accordance with Administrative
Manual (AM) Section
2-07.0.0, Final Plat , Block Plat , Minor Subdivision , and Condominium В Plat.
Applications are reviewed for completeness in accordance with Section
3.2.3.
В В В В В В 2.В В В The final plat shall substantially conform to the approved tentative
plat.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Assurances in accordance with Section
8.6.2,В Subdivision Improvements and Assurances, is also required, when
applicable, for review and consideration for approval by the City Engineer.
В В В В В В 4.В В В A final plat may be submitted concurrent with the tentative plat in
accordance with Section
3.2.3.B, Sequential or Concurrent Review Procedure.
В В В C.В В В Review
В В В В В В 1.В В В The CDRC reviews the final plat for compliance with all pertinent
codes and legal requirements, including, but not limited to, codes regulating
streets , sidewalks and public transportation access, zoning, floodplain and
drainage, fire, water, environmental services, Pima County wastewater, and
utility company regulations. Additionally, the City Engineer reviews the
assurances in accordance with Section
8.6.2, Subdivision Improvements and Assurances.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the CDRC finds that the final plat requires revisions, then:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В The plat will be returned to the subdivider with a letter listing
specific deficiencies and required revisions;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The subdivider shall resubmit a revised final plat and a letter
which responds to each deficiency in detail and explains any revisions made by
the subdivider to the plat;
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В The subdivider continues to resubmit the final plat until such
time as the CDRC recommends approval of the final plat and it is forwarded to
the PDSD Director ; and,
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Any necessary resubmittals will not change or extend the
expiration times commencing at acceptance of a final plat, as provided in this
article.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Upon completing their review, the CDRC shall forward a
recommendation whether to approve the final plat.
В В В D.В В В Decision
В В В В В В 1.В В В Approval and Decision
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Upon recommendation of approval by the CDRC and submission of all
required items as detailed in the CDRC approval letter, the PDSD Director shall
sign and then forward the final plat to the City Engineer for consideration and
signature;
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Pursuant to Section
8.6.2.A.3, assurances must be approved by the City Engineer prior to approval
of the final plat; and,
В В В В В В В В В c.В В В If the plat is dependent on the adoption of a rezoning ordinance,
the City's approval and signature must not occur until after the Mayor and
Council consideration of the rezoning ordinance.
В В В В В В В В В d.В В В Upon approval and signature of the final plat by the PDSD
Director and the City Engineer, the PDSD shall provide a notice of the decision
to the applicant and any party requesting such notice.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Recordation
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В When a subdivider has opted not to post assurances and in
accordance with Section
8.6.2.A, a subdivider shall construct all required subdivision improvements at
no expense to the City prior to the recordation of the final plat.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В The City ClerkвҖҷs Office shall forward the final plat documents to
the Office of the Pima County Recorder for recordation after approval of the
final plat by City staff persons. If the use of the property proposed through
the plat is dependent on the adoption of a rezoning ordinance, the recordation
shall occur after the effective date of any change in zoning.
(Am. Ord. 12219, 12/16/2025)
8.5.В В В EXPIRATION DATES
8.5.1.В В В TENTATIVE PLAT
В В В A.В В В Tentative Plat Expiration
В В В В В В 1.В В В The tentative plat approval expires three years from the date of
approval if a final plat has not been approved and recorded within this period.
В В В В В В 2.В В В When a tentative plat is approved with phases, all phases except
phase one expire four years from the date of approval. A final plat for phase
one must be approved and recorded within three years of tentative plat
approval. A final plat for all other phases must be approved and recorded
within four years of tentative plat approval.
В В В В В В 3.В В В If the subdivider fails to develop the platted lots or subdivision
В infrastructure and intervening UDC changes make the lots unbuildable (i.e.,
nonconforming), then the subdivider is required to file an amended, conforming
plat.
В В В B.В В В Tentative Plat Approval Extension
В В В A subdivider may request an extension of the tentative plat approval period.
The extension may be granted based on the following:
В В В В В В 1.В В В For non-phased subdivisions, up to a one-year time extension may be
granted provided there have been no changes in City regulations applicable to
the proposed subdivision since the approval of the tentative plat; or,
В В В В В В 2.В В В For phased subdivisions, up to a one-year time extension for each
phase of a tentative plat being platted and recorded in phases, provided there
have been no changes in City regulations applicable to the proposed subdivision
since the approval of the tentative plat phase under consideration.
8.5.2.В В В FINAL PLAT
В В В A.В В В Final Plat Approval Period
В В В A final plat shall be approved and recorded within three years of the
tentative plat approval date. A final plat is subject only to the ordinances in
effect at the time of the tentative plat application except where an ordinance
specifically states that it applies to pending applications.
8.6.В В В GENERAL REQUIREMENTS FOR SUBDIVISIONS
8.6.1.В В В GENERAL PROVISIONS
В В В The following requirements apply to all subdivisions.
8.6.2.В В В SUBDIVISION IMPROVEMENTS AND ASSURANCES
В В В Assurances are required when the assurable infrastructure is not completed
at the time a final plat is recorded. Assurances provide security that the
subdivider will complete necessary improvements . The following requirements
also apply to the subdivider (s) or developers when acceptable substitutions of
assurances are used.
В В В A.В В В Requirement
В В В В В В 1.В В В A subdivider shall construct all required subdivision improvements
at no expense to the City prior to the recordation of the final plat.
В В В В В В 2.В В В If a subdivider posts assurances to guarantee the completion of the
required assurable infrastructure , the form and the amount of the assurance
shall conform to the requirements of this section and the Administrative Manual
and is satisfactory to the City AttorneyвҖҷs Office as to form, sufficiency, and
manner of execution.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Assurances that all required assurable infrastructure will be
completed as approved by the City shall be provided by the subdivider in
accordance with Section
2-09.0.0, Assurances, of the Administrative Manual before a final plat is
forwarded to the Mayor and Council for approval. The PDSD Engineering
Administrator is authorized to execute on behalf of the Mayor and Council
agreements with the subdivider  to ensure that subdivision В assurances  are
provided by the subdivider  in accordance with Section
2-09.0.0 , Assurances, of the Administrative Manual. The PDSD Director  is
authorized to release assurances  when appropriate.
В В В B.В В В Acceptable Forms of Assurances
В В В The following are acceptable forms of assurances . For phased subdivisions,
assurances may be provided separately for the improvements necessary for each
phase.
В В В В В В 1.В В В Completion/Performance Bond
В В В В В В A subdivider may post a completion/performance bond for financial
assurances executed by a surety company licensed to do business in the State of
Arizona in an amount approved by the PDSD Engineering Administrator. The PDSD
Engineering Administrator may sign the bond instrument on behalf of the City ,
and the City Attorney shall approve same as to form.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Escrow Account
В В В В В В A subdivider may establish an escrow account with the City or a bank or
similar financial institution acceptable to the City .
В В В В В В 3.В В В Letter of Credit
В В В В В В A subdivider may provide an irrevocable letter of credit in a sum
approved by the PDSD Engineering Administrator from a bank or other financial
institution or person acceptable to the City . The PDSD Engineering
Administrator is authorized to sign the agreement on behalf of the City , and
the City AttorneyвҖҷs Office shall approve same as to form.
В В В В В В 4.В В В Third Party Land Trust
В В В В В В A subdivider may vest ownership of the property constituting the
subdivision in a Third Party Trustee, with direction that the property in trust
will not be released for sale by the Trustee unless and until the City agrees
to the release. The City will thereafter agree to a full or partial release of
trust property as provided in this section and Section
2-09.0.0, Assurances, of the Administrative Manual. The PDSD Engineering
Administrator is authorized to sign the agreement on behalf of the City  and
the City Attorney shall approve same as to form. The Mayor and Council approval
of the third party  land trust is required if the subdivider  deviates from the
City -approved third party  land trust documents.
В В В В В В 5.В В В Alternative Forms of Assurances
В В В В В В The PDSD Director or designee may accept alternate methods to assure the
completion of subdivision improvements where acceptable to the PDSD Director
and the City AttorneyвҖҷs Office in form and substance. Personal checks are not
accepted. Mayor and Council approval of the alternate assurance method,
agreement, or form(s) is required prior to the approval of the final plat.
В В В C.В В В Amount of Assurances
В В В Except for third party land trust agreements, the PDSD Director or designee
may require a monetary amount for assurance of subdivision improvements not to
exceed one and two- tenths times the estimated cost of construction of the
required improvements .
В В В D.В В В Inspection and Acceptance of Improvements for Partial and Final
Releases
В В В В В В 1.В В В The PDSD Director or designee shall provide for inspection of
required improvements .
В В В В В В 2.В В В If the PDSD Director or designee finds upon inspection that any of
the required improvements have not been constructed per City approved
documents, or have not been constructed in accordance with the City вҖҷs
construction standards and specifications, the subdivider shall be responsible
for completing or replacing such improvements in accordance with the list of
deficiencies, the City accepted specifications, and approved plans, as outlined
in this article and Section
2-09.0.0, Assurances, of the Administrative Manual.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Project completion documentation is required to be submitted for
final release as outlined in the PDSD Engineering established procedures in
Section
2-09.0.0, Assurances, of the Administrative Manual.
В В В В В В 4.В В В The City will not accept the required improvements or release or
reduce any assurance for a subdivided project requiring sewer, water, or
transportation improvements , until the PDSD Director or designee has received
statements from the Director or designee of Tucson Water or other water company
serving the property, the Tucson Department of Transportation, and the Pima
County Regional Wastewater Reclamation Department that such improvements have
been satisfactorily completed. Upon such approval and recommendation by the
City Manager, the Mayor and Council shall accept the improvements for
dedication in accordance with established procedure.
В В В E.В В В Partial Release of Assurance
В В В В В В 1.В В В Partial Release of Subdivision Improvements for Third Party Land
Trust
В В В В В В Where a Third Party Land Trust is provided as assurance for completion of
improvements , a partial release of a portion of the subdivision may occur,
prior to the completion of all improvements , provided the partial release is
proportional to the level of completion of improvements . Requests for release,
inspections, and approvals shall be in accordance with Section
2-09.0.0, Assurances, of the Administrative Manual.
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Partial Release of Residential Subdivision В Assurances for Third
Party Land Trust
В В В В В В В В В Up to 75% of the lots held in a third party trust in any particular
phase may be released prior to completion and acceptance of the common-element
improvements (e.g., basins, drainage facilities, sewer lines, water lines,
street В improvements , etc.). Any lot (s) released shall be served by the
completed common-element improvements . Once the common-element improvements
have been accepted, partial releases greater than 75% or a final release may be
granted only when all of the assurable subdivision improvements have been
completed, inspected, accepted by the appropriate agency, and the project
closure documents have been submitted and accepted by the PDSD Engineering
Administrator.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Commercial Subdivisions for Third Party Land Trust Release
В В В В В В В В В Up to 60% of the lots in any particular phase may be released prior to
the completion and acceptance of the common-element improvements (e.g., basins,
drainage facilities, sewer lines, water lines, street В improvements , etc.).
Any lot (s) released shall be served by the completed common-element
improvements . Once the common-element improvements have been accepted, partial
releases greater than 60% or a final release may be granted only when all of
the assurable subdivision improvements have been completed, inspected, accepted
by the appropriate agency, and the project closure documents have been
submitted and accepted by the PDSD Engineering Administrator.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Reduction or Partial Release of Monetary Assurances
В В В В В В Where a bond, letter of credit, or other monetary assurance is provided
the amount of the assurance may be reduced upon partial completion of
construction or actual dedication of one or more improvements , but, only in a
ratio that the dedicated improvement(s) bears to the total improvements
indicated on the plat. Where a subdivider completes and offers to dedicate the
required improvements for a portion of the final plat, monetary assurances for
such improvements may be reduced only where the improvements can be used and
maintained independently of remaining improvements required for the entire
plat. For example, temporary cul-de-sacs should be provided for incomplete
streets ; water, sewer, and electric facilities should be capable of
independent operation; and adequate access for public safety vehicles shall be
provided. In no case may more than 75%, or 65% for commercial projects , of the
total monetary value of assurances be released prior to completion of all
improvements .
В В В F.В В В Substitution of Assurances
В В В Where a Third Party Land Trust is provided as assurance for completion of
improvements , a substitute assurance may be submitted for review. The
substitute trust shall include those portions of the subdivision to be covered.
Additional substitute assurances may be needed to be in place so that all of
the assurable infrastructure that needs completion is covered. The Third Party
Trust or other types of assurances may be entirely substituted by another form
of assurance using cost estimate procedures based on the remaining improvements
with contingency and mobility costs. Acceptance of substitute assurances is
solely at the discretion of the PDSD Director . The number of substitutes for
any given subdivision may be limited as determined by the PDSD Director .
В В В G.В В В Full Release of Assurable Infrastructure
В В В A final release may be granted only when all of the assurable subdivision
improvements have been completed, inspected, accepted by the appropriate
agency, and the project closure documents have been submitted and accepted by
the PDSD Engineering Administrator.
В В В H.В В В Additional Assurances
В В В Notwithstanding assurances provided, if the PDSD Director , in his sole
discretion reasonably determines that grounds exist that could result in
commercial insecurity that the required improvements will be completed, he or
she may demand additional financial assurances . The neglect or refusal of the
subdivider to provide such additional assurances within 30 days shall result in
the immediate suspension of the issuance of any permits or further permits
unless and until further assurance is provided.
В В В I.В В В Expiration or Lapse of Assurances
В В В If an assurance expires, the PDSD Director shall suspend the issuance of any
permit(s) until such time when assurances have been provided. If the PDSD
Director determines that any applicable assurances have lapsed, become subject
to a bankruptcy or otherwise become ineffective, the PDSD Director reserves the
right to issue a stop work order, stop release of additional lots , and utilize
the financial assurances to complete the assurable items, or apply monetary
penalties as prescribed by law.
8.6.3.В В В SURVEY MONUMENTS REQUIRED
В В В The subdivider shall place survey monuments of appropriate type and design
delineating the external boundary of the parcel being subdivided, public
streets , and all public street intersections within or adjacent to the
subdivision , installed in accordance with established practices of the City .
В В В A.В В В For subdivisions with tentative plats , the survey monuments shall be
installed after the tentative plat approval and before final plat recordation,
unless the subdivider has posted assurances with the City to assure the
installation of all monuments after recordation.
В В В B.В В В Prior to recordation, the minor subdivision documents shall be
submitted to identify all monuments (interior and exterior), which an Arizona
registered land surveyor has set to show compliance with the approved minor
subdivision documents.
8.6.4.В В В PERMITS FOR MODEL HOMES
В В В Upon approval of the tentative plat, up to five single-family model homes
may be authorized for construction prior to recordation of the final plat,
provided:
В В В A.В В В Sale or occupancy of an individual unit as a residence does not occur
until after recordation of the final plat and the City вҖҷs release of assurances
for improvements ; and,
В В В B.В В В The location of each unit is based on the lot configuration approved
for the tentative plat at one unit per proposed lot .
8.6.5.В В В LAND CLEARING AND SUBDIVISION В GRADING
В В В A.В В В Land clearing or grading may begin after grading plans are approved,
provided the plans are prepared in compliance with an approved tentative plat
and such tentative plat is in conformance with the underlying zoning. Mass
graded subdivisions include grading of all the platted lots and other
subdivision improvements by the developer. Custom graded lot В subdivisions
include the road improvements that guarantee access to each lot , and other
subdivision improvements where access to a future building pad is feasible for
each lot .
В В В B.В В В Grading plans may be submitted for review prior to approval of the
tentative plat in accordance with Section
2-06.4.9.M, Grading Plan, of the Administrative Manual and Section
2-01.0.0, Excavating and Grading, of the Technical Standards Manual, but cannot
be approved until after the tentative plat has been approved.
В В В C.В В В Concurrent review of a grading plan and a tentative plat may be
permitted when submitting a development package in accordance with Section
2-06.0.0 of the Administrative Manual.
В В В D.В В В When applicable, phased grading information shall be provided on the
tentative plat and grading plan.
8.6.6.В В В PLAT В ABANDONMENT
В В В A recorded plat that is filed for the purpose of abandoning the plat,
meaning reverting to the configuration of land previously subdivided, or
vacating streets or easements previously dedicated to the public; or vacating
or redescribing lot or parcel boundaries previously recorded may be replatted
in accordance with Section
8.4.1, Block Plats, if all other criteria of Section
8.4.1.E, Criteria, are met. Otherwise, the plat shall be replatted in
accordance with Sections
8.4.4, Tentative Plat, and
8.4.5, Final Plats.
8.7.В В В SUBDIVISION DESIGN STANDARDS
8.7.1.В В В PARKS, RECREATIONAL FACILITIES, FIRE STATIONS, AND SCHOOL SITES
В В В Where, in accordance with an adopted plan, it is determined that there are
inadequate parks and recreational facilities, fire stations, or school sites,
the Mayor and Council may require that land area within the subdivision be
reserved for one or more of those uses. Such requirement shall be in accordance
with state В subdivision statutes regulating reservation of parks, recreational
facilities, fire stations, and school sites.
(Am. Ord. 11070, 5/14/2013)
8.7.2.В В В PHASED SUBDIVISIONS
В В В All plats for subdivisions platted in phases shall comply with this Article
and all other relevant City regulations and standards.
8.7.3.В В В FLEXIBLE LOT DEVELOPMENT (FLD)
В В В A.В В В Purpose
В В В The purpose of the Flexible Lot Development (FLD) is to provide greater
flexibility and creativity in the design of residential development by:
В В В В В В 1.В В В Providing incentives to achieve community goals, such as historic
and archaeological preservation, preservation of native vegetation ,
development within low-income areas , and in-fill housing projects ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Implementing the goals and objectives of the General Plan , Area
Plans, and Neighborhood Plans;
В В В В В В 3.В В В Providing open space that is usable and includes suitably located
active and passive recreational amenities, such as trails, walking paths,
picnic areas, and playgrounds;
В В В В В В 4.В В В Providing for visual, and where achievable, physical connections to
open space areas on adjacent properties;
В В В В В В 5.В В В Efficiently using land and public facilities by means of a more
economical arrangement of buildings , circulation systems, land uses , and
utilities ;
В В В В В В 6.В В В Preserving to the greatest extent possible existing Natural
Undisturbed Open Space , environmentally sensitive areas, and landscape
features and amenities, such as significant topography, protected peaks and
ridges , natural vegetation, washes, riparian areas, and floodplains, and
integrating such features with structures and other improvements ;
В В В В В В 7.В В В Coordinating architectural styles, building forms, and building
relationships within the development and with surrounding land development ;
В В В В В В 8.В В В Providing high-quality sustainable development within the City that
incorporates вҖңgreen building вҖқ techniques such as water harvesting, solar
access , and passive solar orientation;
В В В В В В 9.В В В Mitigating the urban heat island effect by requiring such measures
as canopy trees throughout the FLD project and other acceptable mitigation
efforts; and,
В В В В В В 10.В В В Creating incentives for appropriate urban infill development on
lots with site constraints.
В В В B.В В В Applicability
В В В FLDs may be developed in the following zones :
В В В В В В 1.В В В Single-family detached residential development in the SR, SH, RX-1,
and RX-2 zones ;
В В В В В В 2.В В В Single-family residential development , attached or detached, in the
R-1, MH-1, and MH-2 zones ; and,
В В В В В В 3.В В В Single-family attached or detached, and multifamily residential
development in the R-2, R-3, O-1, O-2, O-3, C-1, C-2, and C-3 zones .
В В В C.В В В General Development Criteria
В В В В В В 1.В В В Conformance with the General Plan and other Applicable Plans
В В В В В В An FLD shall be in conformance with the General Plan and any of its
components, including any applicable adopted area and neighborhood plans.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Applicability of General UDC and Technical Standards Manual
Requirements
В В В В В В Except as provided in this section, all applicable standards of the UDC
and the Technical Standards Manual apply to FLDs.
В В В В В В 3.В В В Development Alternatives
В В В В В В FLDs shall be developed using one of the following alternatives:
В В В В В В В В В a.В В В Standard FLD
В В В В В В В В В Standard FLD projects shall not exceed the dimensional standards for
Development Alternative A in Section
8.7.3.D, Regulations for FLD Projects.
В В В В В В В В В b.В В В Maximum Density Option
В В В В В В В В В FLD projects that meet at least one of the following development
options may develop to the dimensional standards for the Development
Alternative B in Section
8.7.3.D, Regulations for FLD Projects.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (1)В В В Low Income Housing
В В В В В В В В В В В В A minimum of 10% of the project вҖҷs total number of units or minimum
of two units, whichever is greater, are constructed and used for low-income
housing .
В В В В В В В В В В В В (2)В В В Housing for the Elderly
В В В В В В В В В В В В The entire project is designed and constructed only for the
elderly. A covenant shall be recorded for the project site stating that the
housing is restricted for use by the elderly. Residents of an FLD for elderly
shall be at minimum 62 years old.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (3)В В В Historic Preservation
В В В В В В В В В В В В The project includes preservation of a historic site , structure ,
or landmark or leads to the preservation or scientific study and archaeological
documentation of prehistoric or historic buildings or sites, in accordance with
Section
3-01.2.0, Historic Preservation Requirements, of the Technical Standards
Manual. Features eligible for use of this option are those identified in the
required archaeological study as meeting the criteria in the Technical
Standards Manual. A recorded covenant preserving the historical site is
required.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (4)В В В Additional Functional Open Space
В В В В В В В В В В В В The project preserves at least 20% more Functional Open Space than
is required by Section
8.7.3.F.1, Functional Open Space Requirements. The additional open space  shall
be usable for passive or active recreational uses, such as trails, walking
paths, picnic areas, and playgrounds.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (5)В В В Additional Open Space within an FLD Greater than 5 Acres
В В В В В В В В В В В В The project preserves features in a natural state at least 15% more
area than is required by other sections of the UDC , including, but not limited
to: Sections
5.2, Hillside Development Zone;
5.7, Environmental Resource Zone; or
7.7, Native Plant Preservation. These natural features include, but are not
limited to, vegetation, washes, riparian  floodplain, and hillsides.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (6)В В В Proximity to an Arterial Street
В В В В В В В В В В В В The project is located in the City вҖҷs Central Core (as defined in
the City of TucsonвҖҷs General Plan ) and is on a designated arterial street near
transit facilities to promote the use of transit and reduce vehicle trips. The
project В density does not conflict with any applicable area or neighborhood
plan.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (7)В В В Trail or Wildlife Corridor Dedication
В В В В В В В В В В В В The FLD provides for dedication of trails or wildlife corridors, or
both, that connect to offsite trails and wildlife corridors as approved by the
Parks and Recreation Department.
В В В В В В В В В В В В (8)В В В Green Building
В В В В В В В В В В В В The project is designed and located to comply with the energy
efficiency requirements listed Section
3-01.3.0, Green Building Requirements, of the Technical Standards Manual.
В В В D.В В В Regulations for FLD Projects
В В В The following regulations are required of all FLD projects :
В В В В В В 1.В В В Development Alternative A is for standard FLDs.
В В В В В В 2.В В В Development Alternative B is for Maximum Density Option FLDs in
accordance with Section
8.7.3.C.3.b, Maximum Density Option.
TABLE 8.7.3-1: Dimensional Standards for FLDs

     Alternative                       (max)

               Alternative (max %)        (max)              (ft)
